**The opening — deference to the messenger.**

[49:1]: "O you who believe, do not place your opinion above that of GOD and His messenger. You shall reverence GOD. GOD is Hearer, Omniscient." — *lā tuqaddimū bayna yadayi llāhi wa-rasūlih*. *Do not advance ahead of God and His messenger*.

[49:2]: "O you who believe, do not raise your voices above the voice of the prophet, nor shall you shout at him as you shout at each other, lest your works become nullified while you do not perceive." — voice-volume in the Prophet's presence; *works can be nullified* by *speaking loudly* — a striking severity around social-conduct.

[49:3]: "Surely, those who lower their voices at the messenger of GOD are the ones whose hearts are prepared by GOD to become righteous. They have deserved forgiveness and a great recompense."

**Verification of news.**

[49:6]: "O you who believe, if a wicked person brings any news to you, you shall first investigate, lest you commit injustice towards some people, out of ignorance, then become sorry and remorseful for what you have done." — *fa-tabayyanū*. *Investigate*. The verse is one of the Quran's most-cited *epistemological* instructions: *verify news from a wicked source before acting on it*.

[49:7]: "And know that GOD's messenger is among you. Had he listened to you in many things, you would have made things difficult for yourselves."

**Conflict mediation.**

[49:9]: "If two groups of believers fought with each other, you shall reconcile them. If one group aggresses against the other, you shall fight the aggressing group until they submit to GOD's command. Once they submit, you shall reconcile the two groups equitably. You shall maintain justice; GOD loves those who are just." — *fa-aṣliḥū baynahumā*. *Reconcile them*. The verse is the Quran's *foundational text* on *Muslim-on-Muslim conflict resolution*. The pattern: (a) reconcile, (b) if one side aggresses, the *community* fights the aggressor *until they submit*, (c) then *equitable reconciliation*.

[49:10]: "The believers are members of one family; you shall keep the peace among your family and reverence GOD, that you may attain mercy." — *innamā l-mu'minūna ikhwah*. *The believers are siblings*. The verse is the canonical *family-of-believers* claim.

**The mockery prohibition.**

[49:11]: "O you who believe, no people shall ridicule other people, for they may be better than they. Nor shall any women ridicule other women, for they may be better than they. Nor shall you mock one another, or make fun of your names. Evil indeed is the reversion to wickedness after attaining faith. Anyone who does not repent after this, these are the transgressors." — three separate prohibitions: (a) one group ridiculing another, (b) one woman ridiculing another, (c) mocking each other's names. *The mocked might be better than the mocker* — the *humility-clause* is built into the prohibition. The verse is one of the Quran's most direct *anti-bullying* texts.

**The suspicion-slander-gossip trio.**

[49:12]: "O you who believe, you shall avoid any suspicion, for even a little bit of suspicion is sinful. You shall not spy on one another, nor shall you backbite one another; this is as abominable as eating the flesh of your dead brother. You would hate that. You shall observe GOD. GOD is Redeemer, Most Merciful." — *ijtanibū kathīran mina ẓ-ẓann, inna ba`ḍa ẓ-ẓanni ithm*. *Avoid much suspicion, for some suspicion is sin*. *Do not spy* (*lā tajassasū*). *Do not backbite* (*lā yaghtab*).

The *backbiting analogy* — *as abominable as eating the flesh of your dead brother* — is one of the Quran's most striking moral images. The Quran *constructs* the analogy explicitly to make *ghībah* viscerally repugnant.

**The categorical equality.**

[49:13]: "O people, we created you from the same male and female, and rendered you distinct peoples and tribes, that you may recognize one another. The best among you in the sight of GOD is the most righteous. GOD is Omniscient, Cognizant." — *yā ayyuhā n-nāsu innā khalaqnākum min dhakarin wa-unthā wa-ja`alnākum shu`ūban wa-qabā'ila li-ta`ārafū inna akramakum `inda llāhi atqākum*.

This is the Quran's *anti-racism / anti-tribalism* verse. The structure of the argument:

(a) *We created you from one male and one female* — common ancestry.

(b) *We made you peoples and tribes* — diversity is *deliberately created*.

(c) *That you may know one another* (*li-ta`ārafū*) — the *purpose* of ethnic and tribal diversity is *mutual recognition*, not hierarchy.

(d) *The most honorable of you with God is the most reverent* — the *ranking criterion* is *taqwā*, not lineage.

The verse rules out, by direct theological grounds: racial hierarchy, tribal nobility, national supremacy.

**The conditional "we believe."**

[49:14]: "The Arabs said, 'We are Mu'mens (believers).' Say, 'You have not believed; what you should say is, "We are Muslims (submitters)," until belief is established in your hearts.' If you obey GOD and His messenger, He will not put any of your works to waste. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful." — *aslamnā* (submitted) is permitted; *āmannā* (believed) is *conditional on belief settling in the heart*. The verse is one of the Quran's clearest *distinctions* between *islām* (submission, the outer act) and *īmān* (belief, the interior conviction).

**Central message.**

Do not advance ahead of God and His messenger ([49:1]); verify news from a wicked source ([49:6]); reconcile two-side conflicts equitably ([49:9]); the believers are *siblings* ([49:10]); *no people ridicule other people; the mocked might be better* ([49:11]); avoid suspicion, spying, backbiting — *backbiting is like eating your dead brother's flesh* ([49:12]); *racial and tribal diversity exists so you recognize each other, not to rank you; the most honorable with God is the most reverent* ([49:13]); *submission* is the outer act, *belief* the interior conviction ([49:14]).

49:1.text: O you who believe, do not place your opinion above that of GOD and His messenger. You shall reverence GOD. GOD is Hearer, Omniscient.
49:1.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا لا تقدموا بين يدي الله ورسوله واتقوا الله إن الله سميع عليم

49:2.text: O you who believe, do not raise your voices above the voice of the prophet, nor shall you shout at him as you shout at each other, lest your works become nullified while you do not perceive.
49:2.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا لا ترفعوا أصوتكم فوق صوت النبي ولا تجهروا له بالقول كجهر بعضكم لبعض أن تحبط أعملكم وأنتم لا تشعرون

49:3.text: Surely, those who lower their voices at the messenger of GOD are the ones whose hearts are prepared by GOD to become righteous. They have deserved forgiveness and a great recompense.
49:3.arabic: إن الذين يغضون أصوتهم عند رسول الله أولـئك الذين امتحن الله قلوبهم للتقوى لهم مغفرة وأجر عظيم

49:6.text: O you who believe, if a wicked person brings any news to you, lest you commit injustice towards some you shall first investigate, people, out of ignorance, then become sorry and remorseful for what you have done.
49:6.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا إن جاءكم فاسق بنبإ فتبينوا أن تصيبوا قوما بجهلة فتصبحوا على ما فعلتم ندمين

49:9.text: If two groups of believers fought with each other, you shall reconcile them. If one group aggresses against the other, you shall fight the aggressing group until they submit to GOD's command. Once they submit, you shall reconcile the two groups equitably. You shall maintain justice; GOD loves those who are just.
49:9.arabic: وإن طائفتان من المؤمنين اقتتلوا فأصلحوا بينهما فإن بغت إحدىهما على الأخرى فقتلوا التي تبغي حتى تفيء إلى أمر الله فإن فاءت فأصلحوا بينهما بالعدل وأقسطوا إن الله يحب المقسطين

49:10.text: The believers are members of one family; you shall keep the peace within your family and reverence GOD, that you may attain mercy.
49:10.arabic: إنما المؤمنون إخوة فأصلحوا بين أخويكم واتقوا الله لعلكم ترحمون

49:11.text: O you who believe, no people shall ridicule other people, for they may be better than they. Nor shall any women ridicule other women, for they may be better than they. Nor shall you mock one another, or make fun of your names. Evil indeed is the reversion to wickedness after attaining faith. Anyone who does not repent after this, these are the transgressors.
49:11.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا لا يسخر قوم من قوم عسى أن يكونوا خيرا منهم ولا نساء من نساء عسى أن يكن خيرا منهن ولا تلمزوا أنفسكم ولا تنابزوا بالألقب بئس الاسم الفسوق بعد الإيمن ومن لم يتب فأولـئك هم الظـلمون

49:12.text: O you who believe, you shall avoid any suspicion, for even a little bit of suspicion is sinful. You shall not spy on one another, nor shall you backbite one another; this is as abominable as eating the flesh of your dead brother. You certainly abhor this. You shall observe GOD. GOD is Redeemer, Most Merciful.
49:12.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا اجتنبوا كثيرا من الظن إن بعض الظن إثم ولا تجسسوا ولا يغتب بعضكم بعضا أيحب أحدكم أن يأكل لحم أخيه ميتا فكرهتموه واتقوا الله إن الله تواب رحيم

49:13.text: O people, we created you from the same male and female, and rendered you distinct peoples and tribes, that you may recognize one another. The best among you in the sight of GOD is the most righteous. GOD is Omniscient, Cognizant. Muslim vs Mu'men
49:13.arabic: يـأيها الناس إنا خلقنكم من ذكر وأنثى وجعلنكم شعوبا وقبائل لتعارفوا إن أكرمكم عند الله أتقىكم إن الله عليم خبير

49:14.text: The Arabs said, "We are Mu'mens (believers)." Say, "You have not believed; what you should say is, `We are Muslims (submitters),' until belief is established in your hearts." If you obey GOD and His messenger, He will not put any of your works to waste. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
49:14.arabic: ۞قالت الأعراب ءامنا قل لم تؤمنوا ولكن قولوا أسلمنا ولما يدخل الإيمن في قلوبكم وإن تطيعوا الله ورسوله لا يلتكم من أعملكم شيـا إن الله غفور رحيم

question: What is the central message of Surah 46 (The Dunes)?
answer:
Surah 46 (Al-Aḥqāf — "The Dunes") is thirty-five verses, Meccan. The surah's title comes from [46:21], which names *Al-Aḥqāf* — the *curved sand-dunes* of southern Arabia where Hood was sent to `Aad. The central message: *the truth of the Quran is testified by witnesses both contemporary and prior*, *the human's life-arc is constrained by what he does at forty*, and *the jinn-encounter at Mecca* makes monotheism *transethnic and transtemporal*.

**The opening — the book-claim with a fixed term.**

[46:1]–[46:3]: "H. M. The revelation of the scripture is from GOD, the Almighty, Most Wise. We did not create the heavens and the earth, and everything between them except for a specific purpose, and for a specific predetermined life span." — *illā bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-ajalin musammā*. The creation has a *purpose* (*bi-l-ḥaqq*) and a *named term* (*ajal musammā*). The *fixed-term* clause appears throughout the Quran ([39:5], [42:14], [46:3], etc.) as the *temporal* qualifier of the cosmos.

**The Prophet's defense — the only-warner clause.**

[46:9]: "Say, 'I am not different from other messengers. I have no idea what will happen to me or to you. I only follow what is revealed to me. I am no more than a profound warner.'" — *qul mā kuntu bid`an mina r-rusul*. *I am not the first of the messengers*. The verse rejects the *innovation*-charge: *the Prophet is in the lineage*, *not introducing a new religion*.

The verse continues: *mā adrī mā yuf`alu bī wa-lā bikum* — *I do not know what will be done to me or to you*. The Prophet *explicitly* disclaims knowledge of the future, including his own. *I only follow what is revealed to me*. *I am only a clear warner*.

**The Israelite witness.**

[46:10]: "Say, 'What if it is from GOD and you disbelieved in it? A witness from the Children of Israel has borne witness to a similar phenomenon, and he has believed, while you have turned arrogant.'" — the *cross-confessional confirmation*: a Jewish witness recognizes the *family-resemblance* of the new revelation to *their own scripture*. The Quran preserves this as *interfaith evidence*.

**The parents-clause.**

[46:15]: "We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. His mother bore him arduously, gave birth to him arduously, and took intimate care of him for thirty months. When he reaches maturity, and reaches the age of forty, he should say, 'My Lord, direct me to appreciate the blessings You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do the righteous works that please You. Let my children be righteous as well. I have repented to You; I am a submitter.'" — the *forty-year prayer*. The verse is unique in the Quran for *naming a specific age* at which a *characteristic prayer* should be uttered. *Thirty months* (cf. weaning at two years per [2:233]) is the period of *intimate maternal care* — and the *forty-year prayer* is the *adult arrival prayer*. The verse contains the implicit *minimum-gestation* (cf. classical fiqh derivation of six months minimum pregnancy from [46:15] + [2:233]).

**The eternal-paradise promise.**

[46:16]–[46:17]: "It is from these that we accept the righteous works, and overlook their sins. They have deserved Paradise. This is the truthful promise that is promised to them." — the *acceptance-and-overlooking* clauses.

[46:17] continues with the *opposite* — the *parent-dishonoring* son who says *uffin lakumā* (*ugh, the two of you*) — *the same parents* the previous verse honored are here disrespected by the *bad son*.

**The dignity-of-fathers and the resurrection denial.**

[46:18]: "Such are the ones stamped as disbelievers among every generation of jinns and humans; they are losers."

**The historical example — `Aad in al-Aḥqāf.**

[46:21]: "Recall that the brother of `Aad warned his people at the dunes — numerous warnings were also delivered before him and after him: 'You shall not worship except GOD. I fear for you the retribution of a great day.'"

[46:22]–[46:24]: They demanded the retribution; a *cloud headed for their valleys* — they thought it was rain bringing relief; *it was actually the wind they sought*.

[46:25]: "It destroyed everything, as commanded by its Lord. By morning, nothing was standing except their homes. We thus requite the guilty people." — *the wind annihilated everything except their houses*.

**The jinn encounter.**

[46:29]: "Recall that we directed a number of jinns to you, in order to let them hear the Quran. When they got there, they said, 'Listen.' As soon as it was over, they rushed to their people, warning."

[46:30]: "They said, 'O our people, we have heard a book that was revealed after Moses, and confirms the previous scriptures. It guides to the truth; to the right path.'" — *kitāban unzila min ba`di Mūsā muṣaddiqan li-mā bayna yadayh*. The jinns' identification of the Quran: *a book revealed after Moses, confirming what came before, guiding to the truth*.

[46:31]: "O our people, respond to the call of GOD, and believe in Him. He will then forgive your sins, and spare you a painful retribution."

The verse parallels surah 72 (Al-Jinn) — both surahs preserve a jinn-encounter with the Quran, both describe the jinns *running back to their people to preach*. The two surahs together establish *jinn-evangelism* as a Quranic motif.

**The closing — patience.**

[46:35]: "Therefore, be patient like the messengers before you who possessed strength and resorted to patience. Do not be in a hurry to see the retribution that will inevitably come to them. The day they see it, it will seem as if they lasted (in this world) one hour of the day. This is a proclamation: Is it not the wicked who are consistently annihilated?" — *fa-ṣbir kamā ṣabara ūlū l-`azmi mina r-rusul*. *Be patient like the resolute messengers were patient* — the *ūlū l-`azm* (the "resolute") prophets — classically identified as Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, Muhammad.

**Central message in one sentence.**

The Quran is from God, who created with *purpose and named term* ([46:3]); the Prophet is *not a new species of messenger* — he stands in the lineage and disclaims future-knowledge ([46:9]); a *Jewish witness* affirms the cross-confessional family-resemblance ([46:10]); the *forty-year prayer* is the canonical adult-arrival utterance — gratitude for parents, request for righteous children, repentance and submission ([46:15]); the *Aḥqāf-dunes* preserve the historical lesson of `Aad's wind-annihilation ([46:21]–[46:25]); a *jinn-delegation* identifies the Quran as *the book after Moses* ([46:30]); and the Prophet is instructed to *be patient like the resolute messengers* ([46:35]).

46:2.text: The revelation of this scripture is from GOD, the Almighty, Most Wise.
46:2.arabic: تنزيل الكتب من الله العزيز الحكيم

46:3.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth, and everything between them except for a specific purpose, and for a finite interim. Those who disbelieve are totally oblivious to the warnings given to them.
46:3.arabic: ما خلقنا السموت والأرض وما بينهما إلا بالحق وأجل مسمى والذين كفروا عما أنذروا معرضون

46:9.text: Say, "I am not different from other messengers. I have no idea what will happen to me or to you. I only follow what is revealed to me. I am no more than a profound warner."
46:9.arabic: قل ما كنت بدعا من الرسل وما أدري ما يفعل بي ولا بكم إن أتبع إلا ما يوحى إلي وما أنا إلا نذير مبين

46:13.text: Surely, those who say, "Our Lord is GOD," then lead a righteous life, will have no fear, nor will they grieve.
46:13.arabic: إن الذين قالوا ربنا الله ثم استقموا فلا خوف عليهم ولا هم يحزنون

46:15.text: We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. His mother bore him arduously, gave birth to him arduously, and took intimate care of him for thirty months. When he reaches maturity, and reaches the age of forty, he should say, "My Lord, direct me to appreciate the blessings You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do the righteous works that please You. Let my children be righteous as well. I have repented to You; I am a submitter."
46:15.arabic: ووصينا الإنسن بولديه إحسنا حملته أمه كرها ووضعته كرها وحمله وفصله ثلثون شهرا حتى إذا بلغ أشده وبلغ أربعين سنة قال رب أوزعني أن أشكر نعمتك التي أنعمت علي وعلى ولدي وأن أعمل صلحا ترضىه وأصلح لي في ذريتي إني تبت إليك وإني من المسلمين

46:17.text: Then there is the one who says to his parents, "Woe to you; are you telling me that (after death) I will come back to life? How come those who died before us never come back?" The parents would cry for GOD's help and say, "Woe to you; please believe! GOD's promise is the truth." He would say, "Tales from the past!"
46:17.arabic: والذي قال لولديه أف لكما أتعدانني أن أخرج وقد خلت القرون من قبلي وهما يستغيثان الله ويلك ءامن إن وعد الله حق فيقول ما هذا إلا أسطير الأولين

46:18.text: Such are the ones stamped as disbelievers among every generation of jinns and humans; they are losers.
46:18.arabic: أولـئك الذين حق عليهم القول في أمم قد خلت من قبلهم من الجن والإنس إنهم كانوا خسرين

46:20.text: The day will come when those who disbelieved will be introduced to the hellfire: "You have wasted the good chances given to you during your worldly life, and you rejoiced in them. Consequently, today you incur a shameful retribution as a requital for the arrogance you committed on earth without any basis, and for your evil works."
46:20.arabic: ويوم يعرض الذين كفروا على النار أذهبتم طيبتكم في حياتكم الدنيا واستمتعتم بها فاليوم تجزون عذاب الهون بما كنتم تستكبرون في الأرض بغير الحق وبما كنتم تفسقون

46:21.text: Recall that the brother of `Aad warned his people at the dunes - numerous warnings were also delivered before him and after him: "You shall not worship except GOD. I fear for you the retribution of a great day."
46:21.arabic: ۞واذكر أخا عاد إذ أنذر قومه بالأحقاف وقد خلت النذر من بين يديه ومن خلفه ألا تعبدوا إلا الله إني أخاف عليكم عذاب يوم عظيم

46:22.text: They said, "Did you come to divert us from our gods? We challenge you to bring (the retribution) you threaten, if you are truthful."
46:22.arabic: قالوا أجئتنا لتأفكنا عن ءالهتنا فأتنا بما تعدنا إن كنت من الصـدقين

46:24.text: When they saw the storm heading their way, they said, "This storm will bring to us much needed rain." Instead, this is what you challenged (Hood) to bring; violent wind wherein there is painful retribution.
46:24.arabic: فلما رأوه عارضا مستقبل أوديتهم قالوا هذا عارض ممطرنا بل هو ما استعجلتم به ريح فيها عذاب أليم

46:25.text: It destroyed everything, as commanded by its Lord. By morning, nothing was standing except their homes. We thus requite the guilty people.
46:25.arabic: تدمر كل شيء بأمر ربها فأصبحوا لا يرى إلا مسكنهم كذلك نجزي القوم المجرمين

46:29.text: Recall that we directed a number of jinns to you, in order to let them hear the Quran. When they got there, they said, "Listen." As soon as it was over, they rushed to their people, warning.
46:29.arabic: وإذ صرفنا إليك نفرا من الجن يستمعون القرءان فلما حضروه قالوا أنصتوا فلما قضي ولوا إلى قومهم منذرين

46:30.text: They said, "O our people, we have heard a book that was revealed after Moses, and confirms the previous scriptures. It guides to the truth; to the right path.
46:30.arabic: قالوا يقومنا إنا سمعنا كتبا أنزل من بعد موسى مصدقا لما بين يديه يهدي إلى الحق وإلى طريق مستقيم

46:31.text: "O our people, respond to the call of GOD, and believe in Him. He will then forgive your sins, and spare you a painful retribution."
46:31.arabic: يقومنا أجيبوا داعي الله وءامنوا به يغفر لكم من ذنوبكم ويجركم من عذاب أليم

46:35.text: Therefore, be patient like the messengers before you who possessed strength and resorted to patience. Do not be in a hurry to see the retribution that will inevitably come to them. The day they see it, it will seem as if they lasted one hour of the day. This is a proclamation: Is it not the wicked who are consistently annihilated?
46:35.arabic: فاصبر كما صبر أولوا العزم من الرسل ولا تستعجل لهم كأنهم يوم يرون ما يوعدون لم يلبثوا إلا ساعة من نهار بلغ فهل يهلك إلا القوم الفسقون

question: What is the central message of Surah 15 (Al-Hijr Valley)?
answer:
Surah 15 (Al-Ḥijr) is ninety-nine verses, Meccan. The surah's title comes from [15:80]–[15:82], which references *the people of Ḥijr* — the dwellers of a rocky valley in north-west Arabia (modern Mada'in Saleh / Hegra, the Nabataean ruins), classically identified with *Thamoud*. The surah's central message is *the Quran's preservation as God's promise*, the *creation-of-Adam exchange with the angels and Iblīs*, and *a roster of communities destroyed for rejecting their messengers*.

**The opening — the book-claim.**

[15:1]: "A. L. R. These (letters) are proofs of this scripture; a profound Quran." — *tilka āyātu l-kitābi wa-qur'ānin mubīn*. The standard *muqaṭṭa`āt + scripture-claim* pattern.

**The preservation promise.**

[15:9]: "Absolutely, we have revealed the reminder, and, absolutely, we will preserve it." — *innā naḥnu nazzalnā dh-dhikra wa-innā lahū la-ḥāfiẓūn*. *We sent down the reminder, and we are the guardians of it*. The verse is *the* foundational Quranic statement that *God Himself guarantees the textual preservation of the Quran*. Khalifa cites this verse as proof that the *Code-19 mathematical structure* — once discovered — is *also* preserved.

**The sky as guarded.**

[15:14]–[15:18]: "Even if we opened for them a gate into the sky, through which they climb; they will say, 'Our eyes have been deceived. We have been bewitched.' We placed galaxies in the sky, and adorned it for the beholders. And we guarded it against every rejected devil. If any of them sneaks around to listen, a mighty projectile will chase him back." — the *celestial security system*: even if you opened a sky-gate, they would *still* call it sorcery. The sky has *galaxies and decoration* for *observation*; it is *guarded against listening devils*.

**Water and resources.**

[15:22]: "And we send the winds as pollinators, and cause water to come down from the sky for you to drink. Otherwise, you could not keep it palatable." — *lawāqiḥ* — *pollinating winds*. The double-function of wind: *pollination* (for plants) and *cloud-driving* (for water).

**The creation of Adam.**

[15:26]: "We created the human being from aged mud, like the potter's clay." — *min ṣalṣālin min ḥama'in masnūn*.

[15:27]: "As for the jinns, we created them, before that, from blazing fire." — *al-jānna khalaqnāhu min qablu min nāri s-samūm*. *The jinns from the searing-wind-fire*. The verse establishes the *material taxonomy*: humans from *aged clay*, jinns from *fire*.

[15:28]–[15:33]: God announces the creation, the angels prostrate, Iblīs refuses on *fire-vs-clay* grounds. The exchange is recorded *in direct speech* — both God's and Iblīs's words preserved.

**The Iblīs-vow.**

[15:39]–[15:42]: "He said, 'My Lord, since You have willed that I go astray, I will surely entice them on earth; I will send them all astray. Except those among Your worshipers who are devoted absolutely to You alone.' He said, 'This is a law that is inviolable. You have no power over My servants. You only have power over the strayers who follow you.'" — the vow + *the divine cap*. *The devil has no power over the sincere servants*. The verse is *one of the Quran's strongest anti-determinism statements*: the strayers are those *who follow* the devil, not those *over whom he has independent power*.

**The seven-gated hell.**

[15:43]–[15:44]: Hell has *seven gates*, each receiving a specific category.

**The Abrahamic-guest scene.**

[15:51]–[15:60]: The angels visit Abraham, give him *news of a knowledgeable son*, proceed to Lot's town to destroy the guilty.

[15:51]: "Inform them about Abraham's guests."

**The Lot-destruction.**

[15:61]–[15:77]: Lot's mission, the town's depravity, the dawn-stroke; *we transformed it upside-down* and *showered rocks of clay*; *a permanent sign for the observers*.

**The Forest-dwellers.**

[15:78]–[15:79]: *The people of the Wood* (*Aṣḥāb al-Aykah*) — Shu`aib's people — also destroyed.

**The Ḥijr-people.**

[15:80]–[15:82]: "The people of Al-Hijr disbelieved the messengers. We gave them our revelations, but they disregarded them. They used to carve secure homes out of the mountains." — *the rock-carved dwellings* of Madā'in Ṣāliḥ — the *visible archaeological remnant* the audience could still see.

[15:83]–[15:84]: "The disaster hit them in the morning. What they hoarded did not help them in the least."

**The seven-doubled.**

[15:87]: "We have given you the seven pairs, and the great Quran." — *sab`an mina l-mathānī*. The verse is one of the most-discussed in the Quran. Classical reading: *the seven verses of Al-Fātiḥah*, doubled (recited in every prayer). Khalifa's reading: *the seven Ḥā Mīm initialed surahs* (40-46), with *al-mathānī* read as *the doubles* (since each is a *two-letter Ḥā Mīm initial*).

**The instruction to the Prophet.**

[15:88]: "Do not be jealous of what we bestowed upon the other (messengers), and do not be saddened (by the disbelievers), and lower your wing for the believers." — *humility-toward-believers + non-envy of others' gifts + non-grief at disbelief*.

[15:94]: "Therefore, carry out the orders given to you, and disregard the idol worshipers." — *fa-ṣda` bi-mā tu'mar*. *Proclaim what you are commanded with*.

**The closing.**

[15:99]: "And worship your Lord, in order to attain certainty." — *wa-`bud rabbaka ḥattā ya'tiyaka l-yaqīn*. *Worship your Lord until certainty comes to you*. The closing verse is *the most-cited Quranic instruction* on *worship-until-death*: *al-yaqīn* (certainty) is classically read as *death*. The phrase has shaped Islamic spirituality at every level — *worship is not for a season but until certainty*.

**Central message in one sentence.**

The Quran is *preserved by God's own guarantee* ([15:9]); the sky is *secured against eavesdropping devils* ([15:17]); humans are from *aged clay*, jinns from *fire* ([15:26]–[15:27]); Iblīs vowed assault but *has no power over the sincere* ([15:42]); a roster of destroyed communities — Lot's town, the Forest-dwellers, the Ḥijr-dwellers — establishes the *historical-warning pattern*; *the seven pairs and the great Quran* are given to the Prophet ([15:87]); and the *standing instruction* is *worship your Lord until certainty* ([15:99]).

15:1.text: A.L.R. These (letters) are proofs of this scripture; a profound Quran.
15:1.arabic: الر تلك ءايت الكتب وقرءان مبين

15:9.text: Absolutely, we have revealed the reminder, and, absolutely, we will preserve it.
15:9.arabic: إنا نحن نزلنا الذكر وإنا له لحفظون

15:14.text: Even if we opened for them a gate into the sky, through which they climb;
15:14.arabic: ولو فتحنا عليهم بابا من السماء فظلوا فيه يعرجون

15:15.text: bewitched." they will say, "Our eyes have been deceived. We have been
15:15.arabic: لقالوا إنما سكرت أبصرنا بل نحن قوم مسحورون

15:16.text: We placed galaxies in the sky, and adorned it for the beholders.
15:16.arabic: ولقد جعلنا في السماء بروجا وزينـها للنـظرين

15:17.text: And we guarded it against every rejected devil.

15:18.text: If any of them sneaks around to listen, a mighty projectile will chase him back.
15:18.arabic: إلا من استرق السمع فأتبعه شهاب مبين

15:22.text: And we send the winds as pollinators, and cause water to come down from the sky for you to drink. Otherwise, you could not keep it palatable.
15:22.arabic: وأرسلنا الريح لوقح فأنزلنا من السماء ماء فأسقينكموه وما أنتم له بخزنين

15:26.text: We created the human being from aged mud, like the potter's clay.
15:26.arabic: ولقد خلقنا الإنسن من صلصل من حمإ مسنون

15:27.text: As for the jinns, we created them, before that, from blazing fire.
15:27.arabic: والجان خلقنه من قبل من نار السموم

15:28.text: Your Lord said to the angels, "I am creating a human being from aged mud, like the potter's clay.
15:28.arabic: وإذ قال ربك للملـئكة إني خلق بشرا من صلصل من حمإ مسنون

15:29.text: "Once I perfect him, and blow into him from My spirit, you shall fall prostrate before him."
15:29.arabic: فإذا سويته ونفخت فيه من روحي فقعوا له سجدين

15:30.text: The angels fell prostrate; all of them,

15:32.text: He said, "O Iblis (Satan), why are you not with the prostrators?"
15:32.arabic: قال يـإبليس ما لك ألا تكون مع السـجدين

15:33.text: He said, "I am not to prostrate before a human being, whom You created from aged mud, like the potter's clay."
15:33.arabic: قال لم أكن لأسجد لبشر خلقته من صلصل من حمإ مسنون

15:39.text: He said, "My Lord, since You have willed that I go astray, I will surely entice them on earth; I will send them all astray.
15:39.arabic: قال رب بما أغويتني لأزينن لهم في الأرض ولأغوينهم أجمعين

15:42.text: "You have no power over My servants. You only have power over the strayers who follow you.
15:42.arabic: إن عبادي ليس لك عليهم سلطن إلا من اتبعك من الغاوين

15:45.text: As for the righteous, they will enjoy gardens and springs.

15:80.text: The people of Al-Hijr disbelieved the messengers.

15:82.text: They used to carve secure homes out of the mountains.
15:82.arabic: وكانوا ينحتون من الجبال بيوتا ءامنين

15:87.text: We have given you the seven pairs, and the great Quran.
15:87.arabic: ولقد ءاتينك سبعا من المثاني والقرءان العظيم

15:88.text: Do not be jealous of what we bestowed upon the other (messengers), and do not be saddened (by the disbelievers), and lower your wing for the believers.
15:88.arabic: لا تمدن عينيك إلى ما متعنا به أزوجا منهم ولا تحزن عليهم واخفض جناحك للمؤمنين

15:94.text: Therefore, carry out the orders given to you, and disregard the idol worshipers.
15:94.arabic: فاصدع بما تؤمر وأعرض عن المشركين

15:99.text: And worship your Lord, in order to attain certainty.

question: What is the central message of Surah 35 (Initiator)?
answer:
Surah 35 (Fāṭir — "Initiator") is forty-five verses, Meccan. The surah's title comes from [35:1], which names God as *Fāṭir al-samāwāti wa-l-arḍ* — the *Initiator/Originator of the heavens and the earth*. The central message: *no one bears another's burden*, *humans are different ranks of created beings tested by the same standard*, and *the prophets were sent to every people*.

**The opening — the Originator with angelic messengers.**

[35:1]: "Praise be to GOD, Initiator of the heavens and the earth, and appointer of the angels to be messengers with wings — two, three, and four (wings). He increases the creation as He wills. GOD is Omnipotent." — *fāṭir as-samāwāti wa-l-arḍ, jā`ili l-malā'ikati rusulan ulī ajniḥah*. The angels are *messengers with wings — two, three, four (wings)*. The verse is one of the Quran's most concrete *angelology* statements: *angels have wings, varying in number, sent as messengers*.

**The mercy-and-witholding clause.**

[35:2]: "When GOD showers the people with mercy, no force can stop it. And if He withholds it, no force, other than He, can send it. He is the Almighty, Most Wise."

[35:3]: "O people, remember GOD's blessings upon you. Is there any creator other than GOD who provides for you from the heaven and the earth? There is no other god beside Him. How could you deviate?" — the *standing rhetorical*: *is there any creator besides Him?*

**The devil as enemy.**

[35:5]–[35:6]: "O people, GOD's promise is the truth; therefore, do not be distracted by this lowly life; do not be diverted from GOD by mere illusions. The devil is your enemy, so treat him as an enemy. He only invites his party to be the dwellers of Hell." — direct *enmity recognition*.

**The provision-and-substitution clause.**

[35:8]: "Is the one whose evil work is adorned in his eyes so that he sees it as good? GOD thus sends astray whoever wills (to go astray), and He guides whoever wills (to be guided)." — *the perception-distortion of the evildoer*: his evil *appears beautiful* to him.

[35:11]: "GOD created you from dust, then from a tiny drop, then He made you into pairs. No female becomes pregnant, nor delivers, without His knowledge. No one survives for a long life, and no one's life is snapped short, except in accordance with a pre-existing record. This is easy for GOD." — the *triple-stage creation* (dust, drop, pair) + the *demographic-recording* (no birth or death outside the record).

**The dual-water sign.**

[35:12]: "The two seas are not the same; one is fresh and delicious, while the other is salty and undrinkable. From each of them you eat tender meat, and extract jewelry to wear. And you see the ships sailing through them, seeking His provisions, that you may be appreciative." — *fresh and salt water*, *both providing food*, *both navigated by ships* — *yet they do not mix*.

**The no-burden-bearing.**

[35:18]: "No soul can carry the sins of another soul. If a soul that is loaded with sins implores another to bear part of its load, no other soul can carry any part of it, even if they were related. The only people you can warn are those who reverence GOD, even when alone in their privacy, and observe the contact prayers (Salat)." — *wa-lā taziru wāziratun wizra ukhrā*. This is the surah's *central theological claim* — repeated at six places in the Quran (6:164, 17:15, 35:18, 39:7, 53:38) and one of the foundational statements of *Quranic individual responsibility*.

**The not-equal claim.**

[35:19]–[35:22]: "The blind and the seer are not equal. Nor are the darkness and the light. Nor are the coolness and the heat. Nor are the living and the dead. GOD causes whomever He wills to hear. You cannot make hearers out of those in the graves." — four *not-equal* pairs: *blind/seer, darkness/light, cool/heat, living/dead*. The structural argument: *categorical distinctions are real*; *moral types are not interchangeable*.

[35:22]: closing of the *not-equal* sequence: *you cannot make those in the graves hear*. The Prophet's frustration at *non-responsive audiences* is addressed: the disbelievers are *like the dead in graves*; *making them hear is not your job*.

**The universal messenger claim.**

[35:24]: "We have sent you with the truth, a bearer of good news, as well as a warner. Every community must receive a warner." — *wa-in min ummatin illā khalā fīhā nadhīr*. *No community has been left without a warner*. The verse is one of the Quran's *universalist-prophetology* claims: *every community has been warned*. The implication: *no community can plead ignorance*.

**The natural-color signature.**

[35:27]–[35:28]: "Do you not realize that GOD sends down from the sky water, whereby we produce fruits of various colors? Also, the mountains have peaks of various colors — white, red, or some shade thereof, as well as jet black. Also, the people, the animals, and the livestock come in various colors. This is why the only true worshipers of GOD are those who are knowledgeable. GOD is Almighty, Forgiving." — *fa-akhrajnā bihī thamarātin mukhtalifan alwānuhā*. *Color diversity* across *fruits, mountains, peoples, animals, livestock* — this is a *creation-signature* God produces *deliberately*.

The closing clause is one of the Quran's most-cited theological statements: *innamā yakhshā llāha min `ibādihī l-`ulamā'* — *those who fear God among His servants are the knowledgeable*. *Knowledge* (*`ilm*) is *correlated* with *taqwā*; *learning* and *reverence* are *not opposed* but *connected*.

**The three classes of Quran-recipients.**

[35:32]: "We passed the scripture from generation to generation, and we allowed whomever we chose from among our servants to receive it. Subsequently, some of them wronged their souls, others upheld it only part of the time, while others were eager to work righteousness in accordance with GOD's will; this is the greatest triumph." — *three classes* of those *given the scripture*:

(a) *Those who wronged their souls* (*ẓālimun li-nafsihī*) — failed the trust.

(b) *Those who kept up only partially* (*muqtaṣid*) — middle.

(c) *Those forward in righteousness* (*sābiqun bi-l-khayrāt*) — leaders.

The trichotomy parallels surah 56's *three classes at the resurrection*.

**The hell-dialogue.**

[35:36]–[35:37]: hell-fire never relieved; the dwellers *cry out for relief* but receive only *go on dwelling*; *did we not give you a life-time enough for one who would have taken heed*?

**The reciprocal hostility on the Day.**

[35:39]: "He is the One who made you inheritors of the earth. Subsequently, whoever chooses to disbelieve does so to his own detriment." — *the inheritance-on-earth* clause.

[35:43]: "They resorted to arrogance on earth, and evil scheming, and the evil schemes only backfire on those who scheme them. Should they then expect anything but the fate of those who did the same things in the past?" — *schemes backfire*. The *standing law* of *plot-vs-plot*: *evil plots end up rebounding on their architects*.

**The grace-clause.**

[35:45]: "If GOD punished the people for their sins, He would not leave a single creature on earth. But He respites them for a predetermined interim. Once their interim is fulfilled, then GOD is Seer of His servants." — *the cosmic clemency*: if God punished sin immediately, *no creature would be left*. He *respites* to a *named term*.

**Central message.**

God is the *Initiator* whose angels are wing-bearing messengers ([35:1]); *His mercy and withholding are uncontainable* ([35:2]); the *devil is a known enemy* ([35:5]–[35:6]); creation runs from *dust to drop to pairs*, all on a *pre-existing record* ([35:11]); *no soul carries another's burden* ([35:18]); *blind/seer, dark/light, alive/dead are not interchangeable* ([35:19]–[35:22]); *every community has received a warner* ([35:24]); *color-diversity in fruits, mountains, peoples is divine signature, and knowledge correlates with reverence* ([35:27]–[35:28]); the scripture's recipients are *three classes* — wronging-self, middling, forward-in-good ([35:32]); *evil schemes backfire on the schemers* ([35:43]); and *God's clemency respites all sin to a named term* ([35:45]).

35:1.text: Praise be to GOD, Initiator of the heavens and the earth, and appointer of the angels to be messengers with wings - two, three, and four (wings). He increases the creation as He wills. GOD is Omnipotent.
35:1.arabic: الحمد لله فاطر السموت والأرض جاعل الملـئكة رسلا أولي أجنحة مثنى وثلث وربع يزيد في الخلق ما يشاء إن الله على كل شيء قدير

35:3.text: O people, remember GOD's blessings upon you. Is there any creator other than GOD who provides for you from the heaven and the earth? There is no other god beside Him. How could you deviate?
35:3.arabic: يـأيها الناس اذكروا نعمت الله عليكم هل من خلق غير الله يرزقكم من السماء والأرض لا إله إلا هو فأنى تؤفكون

35:5.text: O people, GOD's promise is the truth; therefore, do not be distracted by this lowly life. Do not be diverted from GOD by mere illusions.
35:5.arabic: يـأيها الناس إن وعد الله حق فلا تغرنكم الحيوة الدنيا ولا يغرنكم بالله الغرور

35:8.text: Note the one whose evil work is adorned in his eyes, until he thinks that it is righteous. GOD thus sends astray whoever wills (to go astray), and He guides whoever wills (to be guided). Therefore, do not grieve over them. GOD is fully aware of everything they do.
35:8.arabic: أفمن زين له سوء عمله فرءاه حسنا فإن الله يضل من يشاء ويهدي من يشاء فلا تذهب نفسك عليهم حسرت إن الله عليم بما يصنعون

35:9.text: GOD is the One who sends the winds to stir up clouds, then we drive them towards barren lands, and revive such lands after they were dead. Thus is the resurrection.
35:9.arabic: والله الذي أرسل الريح فتثير سحابا فسقنه إلى بلد ميت فأحيينا به الأرض بعد موتها كذلك النشور

35:10.text: Anyone seeking dignity should know that to GOD belongs all dignity. To Him ascends the good words, and He exalts the righteous works. As for those who scheme evil works, they incur severe retribution; the scheming of such people is destined to fail. God is in Full Control
35:10.arabic: من كان يريد العزة فلله العزة جميعا إليه يصعد الكلم الطيب والعمل الصـلح يرفعه والذين يمكرون السيـات لهم عذاب شديد ومكر أولـئك هو يبور

35:11.text: GOD created you from dust, then from a tiny drop, then He causes you to reproduce through your spouses. No female becomes pregnant, nor gives birth, without His knowledge. No one survives for a long life, and no one's life is snapped short, except in accordance with a pre-existing record. This is easy for GOD.
35:11.arabic: والله خلقكم من تراب ثم من نطفة ثم جعلكم أزوجا وما تحمل من أنثى ولا تضع إلا بعلمه وما يعمر من معمر ولا ينقص من عمره إلا في كتب إن ذلك على الله يسير

35:13.text: He merges the night into the day, and merges the day into the night. He has committed the sun and the moon to run for a predetermined period of time. Such is GOD your Lord; to Him belongs all kingship. Any idols you set up beside Him do not possess as much as a seed's shell.
35:13.arabic: يولج اليل في النهار ويولج النهار في اليل وسخر الشمس والقمر كل يجري لأجل مسمى ذلكم الله ربكم له الملك والذين تدعون من دونه ما يملكون من قطمير

35:15.text: O people, you are the ones who need GOD, while GOD is in no need for anyone, the Most Praiseworthy.
35:15.arabic: ۞يـأيها الناس أنتم الفقراء إلى الله والله هو الغني الحميد

35:18.text: No soul can carry the sins of another soul. If a soul that is loaded with sins implores another to bear part of its load, no other soul can carry any part of it, even if they were related. The only people to heed your warnings are those who reverence their Lord, even when alone in their privacy, and observe the Contact Prayers (Salat). Whoever purifies his soul, does so for his own good. To GOD is the final destiny.
35:18.arabic: ولا تزر وازرة وزر أخرى وإن تدع مثقلة إلى حملها لا يحمل منه شيء ولو كان ذا قربى إنما تنذر الذين يخشون ربهم بالغيب وأقاموا الصلوة ومن تزكى فإنما يتزكى لنفسه وإلى الله المصير

35:19.text: The blind and the seer are not equal.

35:20.text: Nor are the darkness and the light.

35:21.text: Nor are the coolness of the shade and the heat of the sun.

35:22.text: Nor are the living and the dead; GOD causes whomever He wills to hear. You cannot make hearers out of those in the graves.
35:22.arabic: وما يستوي الأحياء ولا الأموت إن الله يسمع من يشاء وما أنت بمسمع من في القبور

35:24.text: We have sent you with the truth, a bearer of good news, as well as a warner. Every community must receive a warner.
35:24.arabic: إنا أرسلنك بالحق بشيرا ونذيرا وإن من أمة إلا خلا فيها نذير

35:27.text: Do you not realize that GOD sends down from the sky water, whereby we produce fruits of various colors? Even the mountains have different colors; the peaks are white, or red, or some other color. And the ravens are black.
35:27.arabic: ألم تر أن الله أنزل من السماء ماء فأخرجنا به ثمرت مختلفا ألونها ومن الجبال جدد بيض وحمر مختلف ألونها وغرابيب سود

35:28.text: Also, the people, the animals, and the livestock come in various colors. This is why the people who truly reverence GOD are those who are knowledgeable. GOD is Almighty, Forgiving.
35:28.arabic: ومن الناس والدواب والأنعم مختلف ألونه كذلك إنما يخشى الله من عباده العلمـؤا إن الله عزيز غفور

35:32.text: We passed the scripture from generation to generation, and we allowed whomever we chose from among our servants to receive it. Subsequently, some of them wronged their souls, others upheld it only part of the time, while others were eager to work righteousness in accordance with GOD's will; this is the greatest triumph.
35:32.arabic: ثم أورثنا الكتب الذين اصطفينا من عبادنا فمنهم ظالم لنفسه ومنهم مقتصد ومنهم سابق بالخيرت بإذن الله ذلك هو الفضل الكبير

35:33.text: They will enter the gardens of Eden, where they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their garments in it will be made of silk.
35:33.arabic: جنـت عدن يدخلونها يحلون فيها من أساور من ذهب ولؤلؤا ولباسهم فيها حرير

35:36.text: As for those who disbelieve, they have incurred the fire of Hell, where they are never finished by death, nor is the retribution ever commuted for them. We thus requite the unappreciative.
35:36.arabic: والذين كفروا لهم نار جهنم لا يقضى عليهم فيموتوا ولا يخفف عنهم من عذابها كذلك نجزي كل كفور

35:39.text: He is the One who made you inheritors of the earth. Subsequently, whoever chooses to disbelieve does so to his own detriment. The disbelief of the disbelievers only augments their Lord's abhorrence towards them. The disbelief of the disbelievers plunges them deeper into loss.
35:39.arabic: هو الذي جعلكم خلـئف في الأرض فمن كفر فعليه كفره ولا يزيد الكفرين كفرهم عند ربهم إلا مقتا ولا يزيد الكفرين كفرهم إلا خسارا

35:43.text: They resorted to arrogance on earth, and evil scheming, and the evil schemes only backfire on those who scheme them. Should they then expect anything but the fate of those who did the same things in the past? You will find that GOD's system is never changeable; you will find that GOD's system is immutable.
35:43.arabic: استكبارا في الأرض ومكر السيي ولا يحيق المكر السيئ إلا بأهله فهل ينظرون إلا سنت الأولين فلن تجد لسنت الله تبديلا ولن تجد لسنت الله تحويلا

35:45.text: If GOD punished the people for their sins, He would not leave a single creature on earth. But He respites them for a predetermined interim. Once their interim is fulfilled, then GOD is Seer of His servants.
35:45.arabic: ولو يؤاخذ الله الناس بما كسبوا ما ترك على ظهرها من دابة ولكن يؤخرهم إلى أجل مسمى فإذا جاء أجلهم فإن الله كان بعباده بصيرا

question: How does the Quran describe God's mercy (Ar-Rahman, Ar-Rahim)?
answer:
The Quran's portrayal of God's mercy is built on two divine names from the same Arabic root R-Ḥ-M — *ar-Raḥmān* and *ar-Raḥīm* — which are positioned as the *first attributes mentioned* in the standard opening formula and recur as the *load-bearing theological frame* of the entire scripture.

**1. The two names — same root, different scope.**

[1:1]: "In the name of GOD, Most Gracious, Most Merciful." — *bi-smi llāhi r-Raḥmāni r-Raḥīm*. The *basmalah* introduces all but one of the 114 surahs of the Quran (the exception is surah 9). Both names *ar-Raḥmān* and *ar-Raḥīm* come from R-Ḥ-M ("womb" / "mercy") — but classical lexicography distinguishes them:

(a) *Ar-Raḥmān* — usually rendered "Most Gracious" — denotes *universal* mercy extended to *all creation*, believers and disbelievers alike, in this world.

(b) *Ar-Raḥīm* — usually rendered "Most Merciful" — denotes the *particular* mercy extended *specifically* to the believers, especially in the Hereafter.

The Arabic morphology supports this: *Raḥmān* is *intensive*-form (*fa`lān*), implying *quantitatively vast* mercy; *Raḥīm* is *adjectival* form (*fa`īl*), implying *enduring quality* of mercy.

**2. *Ar-Raḥmān* as preferred divine name in surah 19, 20, 25, 55, 67, 78.**

Several Meccan surahs make *ar-Raḥmān* their *characteristic divine name* — substituting it for *Allāh* where one might otherwise expect:

[19:18]: Mary's refuge: "I seek refuge in the Most Gracious, that you may be righteous." — *Maryam* invokes *ar-Raḥmān*.

[19:96]: "Surely, those who believe and lead a righteous life, the Most Gracious will shower them with love." — *sa-yaj`alu lahumu r-Raḥmānu wuddā*. *The Most Gracious will appoint for them love*.

[20:5]: "The Most Gracious has assumed all authority." — *ar-Raḥmānu `alā l-`arshi stawā*.

[25:60]: "When they are told, 'Fall prostrate before the Most Gracious,' they say, 'What is the Most Gracious? Shall we fall prostrate before what you advocate?'" — the Quran preserves the *Quraysh's resistance* to the name *ar-Raḥmān* itself.

[55:1]–[55:4]: "The Most Gracious. Teacher of the Quran. Creator of the human being. Teaches him how to articulate." — surah 55 *opens* with *ar-Raḥmān* as the sole divine name. The four-line opening *attributes* — *teacher of the Quran, creator of humanity, teacher of articulation* — are all built on *ar-Raḥmān* as subject.

**3. The mercy-precedence.**

[6:54]: "When those who believe in our revelations come to you, you shall say, 'Salaam-un alaikum (Peace be upon you). Your Lord has decreed that mercy is His attribute. Thus, anyone among you who commits a transgression out of ignorance, and repents thereafter and reforms, then He is Forgiving, Most Merciful.'" — *kataba `alā nafsihi r-raḥmah*. *He has prescribed for Himself the mercy*. The verse is one of the Quran's strongest claims: God has *committed Himself* to mercy.

[7:156]: "And decree for us righteousness in this world, and in the Hereafter. We have repented to You.' He said, 'My retribution befalls whomever I will. But My mercy encompasses all things. However, I will specify it for those who lead a righteous life, give the obligatory charity (Zakat), and believe in our revelations.'" — *raḥmatī wasi`at kulla shay'*. *My mercy encompasses all things*. The verse is *the* most-quoted statement of *mercy's universal scope*: His *mercy covers everything* — though He *specifies it* in particular for the righteous.

**4. The eclipse-of-despair clause.**

[39:53]: "Proclaim: 'O My servants who exceeded the limits, never despair of GOD's mercy. For GOD forgives all sins. He is the Forgiver, Most Merciful.'" — *lā taqnaṭū min raḥmati llāh*. *Do not despair of God's mercy*. The verse is *the* foundational anti-despair text — addressed *specifically* to *those who exceeded the limits* (i.e., the gravest sinners). The verse is reinforced by [15:56]: *Who could despair of his Lord's mercy except the strayers?* and [12:87]: *None despairs of God's grace except the disbelieving people*.

**5. The mercy as the Prophet's send-off.**

[21:107]: "We have sent you, except as a mercy from us toward the worlds." — *wa-mā arsalnāka illā raḥmatan li-l-`ālamīn*. *We did not send you except as a mercy to the universes*. The Prophet himself is named as *mercy-to-the-worlds*. The verse extends the *raḥmah* category from *divine attribute* to *the Prophet's structural identity*.

**6. The mercy-of-prophets — Joseph and Jacob.**

[12:64]: Jacob says: "I have no choice but to trust GOD; He is the most Merciful of the merciful ones." — *fa-llāhu khayrun ḥāfiẓan wa-huwa arḥamu r-rāḥimīn*. *Most Merciful of the merciful ones* — *the superlative of mercifuls*. The phrase is one of the Quran's intensifications.

**7. The two-name unity in the basmalah.**

[1:3]: "Most Gracious, Most Merciful." — the basmalah's *opening pair* is *repeated* in [1:3] — the second of the seven verses of Al-Fātiḥah. The repetition is not redundancy; classical exegesis reads the *opening basmalah* as introducing the surah, while [1:3]'s repetition asserts the *centrality* of mercy *within* the prayer. The believer who recites Al-Fātiḥah in every prayer *encounters* the mercy-attribute *twice* in seven verses.

**8. The unity of the name.**

[17:110]: "Say, 'Call upon GOD, or call upon the Most Gracious; whichever name you use, to Him belongs the best names.' You shall not utter your contact prayers (Salat) too loudly, nor secretly; use a moderate tone." — *qul-i d`u llāha aw-i d`u r-Raḥmān, ayyan mā tad`ū fa-lahu l-asmā'u l-ḥusnā*. *Whether you call on Allah or on the Most Gracious, He has the most beautiful names*. The verse is the Quran's direct response to the Quraysh's name-resistance ([25:60]): *the names are interchangeable* — *Allāh* and *ar-Raḥmān* refer to the same Lord.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran portrays God's mercy through *two divine names* — *ar-Raḥmān* (universal/quantitative) and *ar-Raḥīm* (particular/qualitative) — both from the womb-root R-Ḥ-M. *God has prescribed mercy for Himself* ([6:54]). *His mercy encompasses everything* ([7:156]). *Do not despair of His mercy* ([39:53]). *He is the most merciful of the merciful* ([12:64]). The *Prophet himself* is *sent as mercy to the worlds* ([21:107]). And the believer encounters *the mercy attribute* every single time they begin a surah — and twice within *Al-Fātiḥah* — embedding *mercy as the default divine orientation* into the daily liturgy.

Thus, anyone among you who commits a transgression out of ignorance, and repents thereafter and reforms, then He is Forgiving, Most Merciful."

However, I will specify it for those who (1) lead a righteous life, (2) give the obligatory charity (Zakat), (3) believe in our revelations, and
7:156.arabic: ۞واكتب لنا في هذه الدنيا حسنة وفي الأخرة إنا هدنا إليك قال عذابي أصيب به من أشاء ورحمتي وسعت كل شيء فسأكتبها للذين يتقون ويؤتون الزكوة والذين هم بـايتنا يؤمنون

11:90.text: "You shall implore your Lord for forgiveness, then repent to Him. My Lord is Most Merciful, Kind."

15:49.text: Inform My servants that I am the Forgiver, Most Merciful.

19:18.text: She said, "I seek refuge in the Most Gracious, that you may be righteous."
19:18.arabic: قالت إني أعوذ بالرحمن منك إن كنت تقيا

19:96.arabic: إن الذين ءامنوا وعملوا الصـلحت سيجعل لهم الرحمن ودا
