question: What does the Quran teach about sincerity (ikhlas) of intention?
answer:
The Quran's *ikhlās* — *sincerity, devotion to God alone, purity-of-intention* — is *one of the foundational-character-virtues* and *a defining-marker of true-submission*. The root خ-ل-ص (*to be pure, free of foreign-admixture*) underlies *ikhlās* (the noun) and *mukhliṣ* (one-who-purifies-intention). The Quran identifies *ikhlās* as *the criterion* of *accepted-worship*: *worship-acts mixed with other-than-God-orientation* are *not accepted*; *pure-orientation-to-God-alone* is *the standard*.

**1. The 'devote religion to God alone' verses.**

[39:2]: "We sent down to you this scripture, truthfully; you shall worship GOD, devoting your religion to Him alone." — *fa-`budi llāha mukhliṣan lahū d-dīn*.

[39:11]: "Say, 'I have been commanded to worship GOD, devoting the religion absolutely to Him alone.'" — *qul innī umirtu an a`buda llāha mukhliṣan lahū d-dīn*.

[39:14]: "Say, 'GOD is the only One I worship, devoting my religion absolutely to Him alone.'"

[40:14]: "Therefore, you shall devote your worship absolutely to GOD ALONE, even if the disbelievers dislike it." — *fa-d`ū llāha mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn*.

[40:65]: "He is the Living; there is no god except He. You shall worship Him alone, devoting your religion absolutely to Him alone."

The *recurring-formula* — *mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn* — *devoting the religion absolutely to Him alone* — appears *over a dozen times* in the Quran. The *commandment* is *categorical*: *the religion is to be devoted absolutely to God alone*. *Mixed-devotion* — partly-to-God, partly-to-other — is *categorically prohibited*.

**2. The 'all that was asked' verse.**

[98:5]: "All that was asked of them was to worship GOD, devoting the religion absolutely to Him alone, observe the contact prayers (Salat), and give the obligatory charity (Zakat). Such is the perfect religion." — *wa-mā umirū illā li-ya`budū llāha mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn*.

The verse is *theologically-condensing*: *the entirety of what was asked of the prior-communities (and is asked of all communities)* is *worship-God-alone with absolute-devotion, observe-the-prayers, give-the-zakat*. The *first-clause* — *worship God with mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn* — is *the foundational-requirement*; the *prayer and zakat* are *its operational-manifestations*.

The verse identifies this *three-component-formula* as *the perfect religion* (*dīn al-qayyimah*). *The complete religion* is *not* a vast-system of rules; *it is the simple-trinity*: *pure-devotion + prayer + zakat*.

**3. The 'storms-at-sea' verses.**

[29:65]: "When they ride on a ship, they implore GOD, devoting their prayers to Him. But as soon as He saves them to the shore, they revert to idolatry." — *fa-idhā rakibū fī l-fulki da`awu llāha mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn*.

[31:32]: "When violent waves surround them, they implore GOD, sincerely devoting their prayers to Him alone. But as soon as He saves them to the shore, some of them revert. None discards our revelations except those who are betrayers, unappreciative." — *da`awu llāha mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn*.

The two verses establish *the human-psychological-reality*: *in extremis* (the *storm-at-sea*), *humans naturally devote prayer to God alone* — *the situation forces ikhlās*. *Upon rescue*, *they revert* to *mixed-devotion* (*idolatry, partner-attribution*).

The *theological-implication*: *true-ikhlās is to maintain the same pure-devotion in safe-circumstances that one naturally exhibits in dire-circumstances*. The *storm-state ikhlās* reveals *the human's authentic-orientation*; *the safe-state mixed-devotion* reveals *the human's superficial-corruption*.

**4. The 'forced-with-them' verse.**

[18:28]: "You shall force yourself to be with those who worship their Lord day and night, seeking Him alone. Do not turn your eyes away from them, seeking the vanities of this world. Nor shall you obey one whose heart we rendered oblivious to our message; one who pursues his own desires, and whose priorities are confused." — *yad`ūna rabbahum bi-l-ghadāti wa-l-`ashiyyi yurīdūna wajhah*.

The phrase *yurīdūna wajhah* — *seeking His face* — is the *positive-formulation of ikhlās*: *the worshiper's intention is the divine-face* (*the divine-presence-and-pleasure*) — *not* the *external-rewards*.

**5. The 'Satan's exception' verse.**

[15:40]: "'Except those among Your worshipers who are devoted absolutely to You alone.'" — *illā `ibādaka minhumu l-mukhlaṣīn*.

The verse — *Iblis's-own-statement* after his refusal-to-prostrate — *concedes* that *he cannot mislead* the *mukhlaṣ-worshipers* (the *purified-ones*). The *theological-implication*: *the devil's power over the human-soul is conditional on the human's lack of ikhlās*; *the mukhlaṣ-worshiper is protected from satanic-influence by the very-quality of their pure-devotion*.

Note: *the verse uses mukhlaṣīn (purified-by-God) rather than mukhliṣīn (purifying-of-self)*. The two are related: *the human's effort to purify (mukhliṣ)* is *crowned by divine-purification (mukhlaṣ)*. *Both are at the high-end of ikhlās*.

**6. The Moses-mukhlaṣ verse.**

[19:51]: "Mention in the scripture Moses. He was devoted, and he was a messenger prophet." — *innahū kāna mukhlaṣan wa-kāna rasūlan nabiyyā*.

The verse identifies *Moses* as *mukhlaṣan* — *purified*. *The prophetic-status* and *the pure-devotion* are *correlated*: *prophets are mukhlaṣūn* — *purified-ones*.

**7. The 'no mediator' implication.**

[39:3]: "Absolutely, the religion shall be devoted to GOD alone. Those who set up idols beside Him say, 'We idolize them only to bring us closer to GOD; for they are in a better position!' GOD will judge them regarding their disputes. GOD does not guide such liars, disbelievers." — *a-lā li-llāhi d-dīnu l-khāliṣ*.

The verse *categorically rejects* the *intermediary-justification* for *idolatry*: *the idolaters claim that the idols bring them closer to God*; *the Quran responds* that *the religion is to be devoted absolutely to God alone* — *no intermediaries-required, no intermediaries-permitted*.

The *theological-implication* is *foundational* — *the rejection-of-mediators* is *the operational-meaning of ikhlās*: *no saint, no priest, no idol, no relic, no living-being* may *intervene* in *the direct-relationship between the soul and God*. The *direct-line* is *the essence of ikhlās*.

**8. The 'devoted to us alone' parallel.**

[2:139]: "Say, 'Do you argue with us about GOD, when He is our Lord and your Lord? We are responsible for our deeds, and you are responsible for your deeds. To Him alone we are devoted.'" — *wa-naḥnu lahū mukhliṣūn*.

The verse — *in the context of Jewish-Christian-Muslim debate* — establishes *the Muslim-position*: *to Him alone we are devoted*. *Ikhlās* is *the Muslim's distinguishing-claim* in *the inter-religious dialogue*: *not the form of worship, not the prayer-direction, not the dietary-laws* — *but the absolute-monotheism of devotion*.

**9. The 'sincerity is the criterion of acceptance' implication.**

[7:29] gives the *the criterion of acceptance*: "Say, 'My Lord advocates justice, and to stand devoted to Him alone at every place of worship. You shall devote your worship absolutely to Him alone.'" — *wa-d`ūhu mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn*.

The *worship-act* must be *sincere-to-God-alone*. *Worship-with-mixed-motives* — *seeking-human-praise alongside divine-pleasure* — is *not the standard*; *pure-devotion* is *the standard*.

The hadith-tradition's famous saying — *innamā l-a`mālu bi-n-niyyāt* (*deeds are by intentions*) — captures *the same theological-principle*: *the value of an act is determined by the intention behind it*; *pure-intention is the criterion*.

**10. The 'Surah Al-Ikhlas' as concentrated-ikhlās.**

[112:1]–[112:4]: "Proclaim, 'He is the One and only GOD. The Absolute GOD. Never did He beget. Nor was He begotten. None equals Him.'" — *qul huwa llāhu aḥad, allāhu ṣ-ṣamad, lam yalid wa-lam yūlad, wa-lam yakun lahū kufuwan aḥad*.

The surah is named *Al-Ikhlās* (*The Sincerity*) — not because it contains the word *ikhlās* (it does not) but because *it is the most-concentrated-monotheistic statement* in the Quran. *Reciting* this surah is *to enact ikhlās in concentrated-form*: *one God, absolute, no parent, no offspring, no equal*. *The theological-content* of *ikhlās* — *uncompromised-monotheism* — is *the entirety of the surah*.

**11. The cultivation of ikhlās.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-methods* for *ikhlās*:

(a) *Direct-prayer to God* — *no intermediaries* ([39:3], [40:14], [40:65]).

(b) *Storm-prayer-pattern* — *recall the storm-at-sea pure-devotion and maintain it in safety* ([29:65], [31:32]).

(c) *Seeking-the-divine-face* — *yurīdūna wajhah* — *the intention is the divine-pleasure, not worldly-reward* ([18:28]).

(d) *Reciting surah Al-Ikhlas frequently* — *re-affirming the absolute-monotheism* ([112:1]–[112:4]).

(e) *Association with the mukhliṣīn* — *the company of the pure-devoted* ([18:28]).

(f) *Avoidance of seeking-human-praise* — *the negative-correlate of ikhlās*.

(g) *Constant-remembrance-of-God in private and public* — *consistency of devotion* across *seen and unseen circumstances*.

**12. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *ikhlās* is *the defining-characteristic of Submission*. The Khalifa-translation consistently renders the *mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn* formula as *devoting the religion absolutely to Him alone* — *foregrounding the absolute-monotheism* dimension. The Submitter-movement's *theological-distinctiveness* — *no idols, no intermediaries, no hadith-as-religious-source, no saint-veneration* — is *the operational-manifestation* of *ikhlās*. *Ikhlās is the test* — *the worshiper who clings to anything alongside God is failing the test*; *the worshiper who devotes absolutely to God alone is passing the test*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *ikhlās* — *sincerity, devotion to God alone, purity-of-intention* — is *the defining-marker of true-submission*. The *recurring-formula mukhliṣīna lahū d-dīn* — *devoting the religion absolutely to Him alone* — appears *over a dozen times* ([39:2], [39:11], [39:14], [40:14], [40:65], [98:5], [29:65], [31:32], [7:29], [2:139]). *All that was asked* of the prior-communities is *the three-component-formula*: *worship-God-alone + prayer + zakat* ([98:5]). *The storm-at-sea verses* ([29:65], [31:32]) reveal *the human's authentic-monotheistic-orientation* and *expose the mixed-devotion of safe-circumstances*. *The rejection-of-mediators* at [39:3] is *the operational-meaning of ikhlās*: *no saint, no priest, no idol* may *intervene* in *the direct-relationship*. *Iblis cannot mislead the mukhlaṣīn* ([15:40]) — *the pure-devoted are protected from satanic-influence by the quality of their devotion*. *Surah Al-Ikhlas* ([112:1]–[112:4]) is *the most-concentrated-monotheistic statement* — *enacting ikhlās in recitation*. The *cultivation-methods*: *direct-prayer-without-intermediaries, maintaining-the-storm-state-orientation, seeking-the-divine-face, surah-Al-Ikhlas recitation, association-with-the-mukhliṣīn, avoidance-of-seeking-human-praise, constant-remembrance*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *ikhlās* as *the defining-characteristic of Submission* — *no idols, no intermediaries, no admixture of any other-than-God orientation*.

question: What is ihsan (excellence) in the Quran?
answer:
The Quran's *iḥsān* — *excellence, doing-the-beautiful, perfecting-one's-deeds* — is *one of the highest-character-virtues* in the scripture. The root ح-س-ن (*to be beautiful, good, fine*) underlies *iḥsān* (the noun-of-act), *muḥsin* (the noun-of-agent, *one-who-does-iḥsān*), and *aḥsan* (the elative, *most-beautiful*). The Quran links *iḥsān* to *divine-love* (*God loves the muḥsinīn*), to *companionship-with-God*, to *reciprocal-good* (*the reward of iḥsān is iḥsān*), and to *the third-level of religious-attainment* (after *islām* and *īmān*).

**1. The 'God loves the muḥsinīn' verses.**

[2:195]: "You shall spend in the cause of GOD; do not throw yourselves with your own hands into destruction. You shall be charitable; GOD loves the charitable." — *wa-aḥsinū, inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn*.

[3:134]: "who give to charity during the good times, as well as the bad times. They are suppressors of anger, and pardoners of the people. GOD loves the charitable." — *wa-llāhu yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn*.

[5:13]: "...You will continue to discover deceit in them, except for a few of them. You shall pardon them and disregard them. GOD loves those who are benevolent." — *wa-aṣfaḥ, inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn*.

[5:93]: "Those who believe and lead a righteous life bear no guilt by eating any food, so long as they observe the commandments, believe and lead a righteous life, then maintain their piety and faith, and continue to observe piety and righteousness. GOD loves the righteous." — *wa-llāhu yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn*.

The *recurring-formula* — *inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn* — *God loves the muḥsinīn* — appears *five times* in the Quran. The *divine-love-attractor* is *iḥsān*. *No other-character-virtue* is *as frequently paired* with *the divine-love-formula*.

**2. The 'God commands justice, iḥsān, and giving-to-relatives' verse.**

[16:90]: "GOD advocates justice, charity, and regarding the relatives. And He forbids evil, vice, and transgression. He enlightens you, that you may take heed." — *inna llāha ya'muru bi-l-`adli wa-l-iḥsāni wa-ītā'i dhī l-qurbā*.

The verse — *one of the most-frequently-recited verses* in *Islamic-tradition* — establishes *the three-component divine-positive-command*:

(a) *`Adl* — *justice* (the *baseline-fairness*).

(b) *Iḥsān* — *excellence / beautiful-doing* (the *above-baseline-virtue*).

(c) *Ītā'u dhī l-qurbā* — *giving to the relatives* (the *concrete-application*).

The *theological-architecture*: *justice is the baseline-minimum*; *iḥsān is the above-baseline*; *the muḥsin gives more-than-required, treats-better-than-deserved, forgives-when-could-prosecute*. *Iḥsān is supererogatory excellence*.

**3. The 'iḥsān and reciprocal good' verse.**

[55:60]: "Is the reward of goodness anything but goodness?" — *hal jazā'u l-iḥsāni illā l-iḥsān*.

The verse — *one of the shortest and most-quoted* in the Quran — establishes *the principle-of-reciprocal-good*: *the reward of iḥsān is iḥsān*. *God responds to human-iḥsān with divine-iḥsān*.

The theological-implication: *the muḥsin's actions are not merely tolerated by God*; *they are reciprocated by God in kind*. *The human's beautiful-deed* attracts *the divine's beautiful-response*.

**4. The 'submit your face to God while being muḥsin' verse.**

[2:112]: "Indeed, those who submit themselves absolutely to GOD alone, while leading a righteous life, will receive their recompense from their Lord; they have nothing to fear, nor will they grieve." — *balā man aslama wajhahū li-llāhi wa-huwa muḥsin*.

The verse establishes *the two-component formula for salvation*: (a) *aslama wajhah li-llāh* — *submit one's face to God* (the *islām-dimension*); (b) *huwa muḥsin* — *while being a muḥsin* (the *iḥsān-dimension*).

The *two together* produce *no-fear and no-grief* — *the highest-eschatological-promise*. *Iḥsān is the perfecting-element* of *islām*: *islām + iḥsān = salvation*.

[4:125]: "Who is better in religion than one who submits absolutely to GOD, leads a righteous life, according to the creed of Abraham: monotheism? GOD has chosen Abraham as a beloved friend." — *wa-man aḥsanu dīnan mim-man aslama wajhahū li-llāhi wa-huwa muḥsinun wa-ttaba`a millata Ibrāhīma ḥanīfā*.

The verse parallels [2:112] and identifies *the Abrahamic-monotheistic submission-with-iḥsān* as *the standard-of-the-best-religion*. *Abraham's status as the beloved-friend of God* (*khalīl Allāh*) is *grounded in his iḥsān*.

**5. The 'iḥsān to parents' command.**

[4:36]: "You shall worship GOD alone - do not associate anything with Him. You shall regard the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, the related neighbor, the unrelated neighbor, the close associate, the traveling alien, and your servants. GOD does not like the arrogant show-offs."

The verse — *the operational-list of muḥsinūn-iḥsān-recipients* — identifies *nine categories* who *deserve iḥsān*: (a) *parents*; (b) *relatives*; (c) *orphans*; (d) *poor*; (e) *related-neighbor*; (f) *unrelated-neighbor*; (g) *close-associate*; (h) *traveling-alien*; (i) *servants*.

The verse establishes *iḥsān as practical-relational-virtue*: *not abstract-good-feeling*; *concrete-good-treatment of specific-categories of fellow-humans*.

**6. The 'iḥsān-with-them' divorce verses.**

[33:29]: "But if you are seeking GOD and His messenger, and the abode of the Hereafter, then GOD has prepared for the righteous among you a great recompense." — pairs with [33:28] *if you choose this life and its vanities, then come; I will provide for you and let you go amicably*.

The *amicable-divorce* model — *tasrīḥun bi-iḥsān* — is *the Quranic-standard* for *marital-dissolution*: *not bitter-acrimony*; *iḥsān-treatment* even in *separation*. The *muḥsin* maintains *beautiful-treatment* even when *the relationship ends*.

**7. The 'iḥsān-with-our-mother' Jesus verse.**

[19:32] (Jesus speaking): "'I am to honor my mother; He did not make me a disobedient tyrant.'" — *wa-barran bi-wālidatī*.

The verse — *Jesus's-self-description* — uses *barr* (the *parallel-term* to *iḥsān* for *filial-piety*) as *one of his defining-characteristics*. *Jesus exemplifies* the *iḥsān-to-parents* model.

**8. The 'God is with the muḥsinīn' verse.**

[16:128]: "GOD is with those who lead a righteous life, and those who are charitable." — *inna llāha ma`a lladhīna ttaqaw wa-lladhīna hum muḥsinūn*.

The verse pairs *the muttaqūn* and *the muḥsinūn* as the *two-categories* with whom *God is*. The *divine-companionship-attractors* are *taqwa* and *iḥsān*. The *Quranic-frame* — *God's company is the highest-blessing* — makes *taqwa-and-iḥsān* the *highest-aspirations*.

**9. The 'do not fail the muḥsinīn's reward' verse.**

[11:115]: "You shall steadfastly persevere, for GOD never fails to recompense the righteous." — *inna llāha lā yuḍī`u ajra l-muḥsinīn*.

The verse pairs *patience* and *iḥsān* — *the steadfast muḥsin's reward is never wasted*. The *divine-protection of the iḥsān-reward* is *categorical*.

[12:90] (Joseph's testimony): *if one leads a righteous life, and steadfastly perseveres, GOD never fails to reward the righteous* — *the same formula* with *taqwa + sabr → iḥsān-reward-protection*.

**10. The 'iḥsān as the third-level' (hadith-tradition).**

The Quran does *not explicitly* layout *the three-levels* of *islām, īmān, iḥsān* as a *hierarchical-progression* — *that systematization* is *from the hadith of Gabriel*. *The Quranic-data*, however, supports the *layered-reading*:

(a) *Islām* — *outward-submission*; *the baseline-membership-status*; *peace-with-God-through-surrender*.

(b) *Īmān* — *inner-faith*; *the heart-acceptance*; *belief in God, angels, scripture, prophets, the Last Day*.

(c) *Iḥsān* — *excellence in worship and action*; *the perfecting-virtue*; *worshiping God as if you see Him; if you do not see Him, He sees you* (the *famous-hadith definition*).

The *Quranic-iḥsān-verses* (above) all *fit* this *third-level-reading*: *iḥsān is the high-attainment* that *crowns* the *prior-attainments of islām and īmān*.

**11. The cultivation of iḥsān.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-methods* for *iḥsān*:

(a) *Practice-of-the-iḥsān-relational-list* — *iḥsān to parents, relatives, orphans, poor, neighbors, associates, aliens, servants* ([4:36]).

(b) *Recitation of the iḥsān-formula verses* — *internalizing the divine-love-attractor* ([2:195], [3:134], [5:13], [5:93]).

(c) *Combining-islām-with-iḥsān* — *submission-with-perfection* ([2:112], [4:125]).

(d) *Maintaining-iḥsān-in-dissolution* — *amicable-divorce, kind-parting from any-relationship* ([33:28], [2:229]).

(e) *Recognizing-the-reciprocal-good principle* — *the reward of iḥsān is iḥsān* ([55:60]) — *the more iḥsān you give, the more iḥsān you receive*.

(f) *Imitation-of-prophetic-models* — *Jesus's filial-piety* ([19:32]); *Abraham's beloved-friend status* ([4:125]); *Joseph's combined-taqwa-and-sabr-and-iḥsān* ([12:90]).

(g) *Perpetual-presence-orientation* — *worship God as if you see Him* (the *hadith-formulation* of the *Quranic-iḥsān*).

**12. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *iḥsān* is *the perfecting-virtue* — *the above-baseline-excellence* that *brings divine-love*. The Khalifa-translation consistently renders *muḥsinīn* as *the charitable* (in the broad-sense) or *those who lead a righteous life* — *foregrounding the practical-action-character* of *iḥsān*. The *Submitter-emphasis*: *iḥsān is practical and relational*; *not contemplative-mysticism alone*; *manifests in concrete-good-treatment of specific-categories of fellow-humans* ([4:36]).

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *iḥsān* — *excellence, doing-the-beautiful, perfecting-one's-deeds* — is *one of the highest-character-virtues*. *God loves the muḥsinīn* — the *recurring-formula* appears *five times* ([2:195], [3:134], [5:13], [5:93], plus parallels). *God commands justice, iḥsān, and giving-to-relatives* ([16:90]) — *the three-component divine-positive-command*. *The reward of iḥsān is iḥsān* ([55:60]) — *reciprocal-good principle*. *Submitting one's face to God while being a muḥsin* ([2:112], [4:125]) — *the two-component formula for salvation*. *Iḥsān to nine categories of relational-recipients* ([4:36]) — *the operational-list*. *God is with the muḥsinīn* ([16:128]) — *divine-companionship-attractor*. The *cultivation-methods*: *practice-of-the-iḥsān-relational-list, combining-islām-with-iḥsān, maintaining-iḥsān-in-dissolution, recognizing-the-reciprocal-good principle, imitation-of-prophetic-models* (Jesus, Abraham, Joseph). The *Khalifa framework* reads *iḥsān* as *practical-relational-excellence* — *not contemplative-mysticism alone*; *manifests in concrete-good-treatment* of *specific-categories* of *fellow-humans*. The *three-level-hierarchical-reading* (islām, īmān, iḥsān — from the hadith of Gabriel) is *consistent with* the Quranic-iḥsān-verses but *the explicit-systematization* is *from the hadith-tradition*.

question: How does the Quran value truthfulness (sidq)?
answer:
The Quran's *ṣidq* — *truthfulness, honesty, true-statement* — is one of the foundational-character-virtues. The root ص-د-ق (*to be true, sincere, authentic*) underlies *ṣidq* (the noun-of-attribute) and *ṣādiq* (the noun-of-agent, *the truthful*). The Quran identifies *truthfulness* as *the standard of believer-character*, *a divine-attribute that the messengers embody*, and *the criterion of acceptance on the Day of Judgment*.

**1. The 'be among the truthful' command.**

[9:119]: "O you who believe, you shall reverence GOD, and be among the truthful." — *yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-kūnū ma`a ṣ-ṣādiqīn*.

The verse pairs *taqwa* with *truthfulness*: *reverence God + be among the truthful*. The *community-dimension* — *be among the truthful* — establishes that *truthfulness is cultivated in community* through *association with the truthful*.

**2. The 'truthful men and women' verse.**

[33:35]: "...the truthful men, the truthful women...; GOD has prepared for them forgiveness and a great recompense." — *aṣ-ṣādiqīna wa-ṣ-ṣādiqāt*.

The verse — the *ten-quality-list* of *the righteous* — includes *truthfulness* (*ṣidq*) as *one of the ten qualities* that attract *forgiveness and great recompense*. *Truthfulness is gender-symmetric* — *both men and women* are *equally addressed*.

**3. The 'speak the most-correct word' verse.**

[33:70]: "O you who believe, reverence GOD and utter only the correct utterances." — *yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-qūlū qawlan sadīdā*.

[33:71]: "He will then fix your works, and forgive your sins. Those who obey GOD and His messenger have triumphed a great triumph."

The verse-pair establishes *truthful-speech* as *a precondition* for *divine-fixing-of-works* and *forgiveness-of-sins*. *The reform-of-action* begins with *the reform-of-speech*. The Arabic *qawlan sadīdā* — *the well-aimed, straight, correct word* — is *more than not-lying*; it is *speech that goes-to-the-mark*.

**4. The 'truthfulness on the Day of Judgment' verse.**

[5:119]: "GOD will proclaim, 'This is a day when the truthful will be saved by their truthfulness.' They have deserved gardens with flowing streams. They abide therein forever. GOD is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. This is the greatest triumph." — *hādhā yawmu yanfa`u ṣ-ṣādiqīna ṣidquhum*.

The verse establishes *truthfulness* as *the salvific-virtue on the Day of Judgment*: *the truthful are saved by their truthfulness*. The *theological-implication* is *categorical* — *no other-virtue* is *as explicitly named as the Day-of-Judgment-saving-attribute* as *truthfulness*.

**5. The 'truthfulness as the criterion of belief' verse.**

[49:15]: "Believers are those who believe in GOD and His messenger, then attain the status of having no doubt whatsoever, and strive with their money and their lives in the cause of GOD. These are the truthful ones." — *ulā'ika humu ṣ-ṣādiqūn*.

The verse identifies *the believers' authentic-status* as *the truthful*. *Belief without striving* is *not the believer-truthfulness*; *belief with striving in money and life* is *the believer-truthfulness*. *Ṣidq* here is *not just verbal-truth*; it is *the alignment-of-life-with-confessed-belief*.

[29:3]: "We have tested those before them, for GOD must distinguish those who are truthful, and He must expose the liars." — *fa-la-ya`lamanna llāhu lladhīna ṣadaqū wa-la-ya`lamanna l-kādhibīn*.

The verse establishes *life-testing* as *the divine-mechanism* to *distinguish the truthful from the liars*. *Truthfulness is revealed through trial*; *empty-claim is exposed through trial*.

**6. The 'one who brings the truth' verse.**

[39:33]: "As for the one who promotes the truth, and confirms it, these are the righteous." — *wa-lladhī jā'a bi-ṣ-ṣidqi wa-ṣaddaqa bihī ulā'ika humu l-muttaqūn*.

The verse identifies *the truth-bringer-and-truth-confirmer* as *the muttaqūn* — *the righteous*. The chain: *speak the truth + confirm the truth* = *attain the righteous-status*.

**7. The 'truthful-tongue' inheritance.**

[19:50] (about Abraham, Isaac, Jacob): "We showered them with our mercy, and we granted them an honorable position in history." — *wa-ja`alnā lahum lisāna ṣidqin `aliyyā*. The literal: *we made for them a truthful-tongue, elevated*.

The phrase *lisān ṣidq* — *truthful-tongue* — is *the Quranic-image* of *the lasting-honorable-reputation*. The Abrahamic-lineage is *granted lasting truthful-mention* — *their truthfulness* in this-life *produces* *the truthful-tongue* in *subsequent-history*.

[19:54] (about Ishmael): "And mention in the scripture Ishmael. He was truthful when he made a promise, and he was a messenger prophet." — *kāna ṣādiqa l-wa`d*.

Ishmael's *defining-characteristic* is *promise-truthfulness* — *ṣādiq al-wa`d*. The *prophet-model* of *truthfulness* is *not just verbal-statement* but *promise-keeping over time*.

**8. The 'truthfulness-came-from-God' verse.**

[12:51]: "(The king) said (to the women), 'What do you know about the incident when you tried to seduce Joseph?' They said, 'GOD forbid; we did not know of anything evil committed by him.' The wife of the governor said, 'Now the truth has prevailed. I am the one who tried to seduce him, and he was the truthful one.'" — *al-āna ḥaṣḥaṣa l-ḥaqq, anā rāwadtuhū `an nafsihī wa-innahū la-mina ṣ-ṣādiqīn*.

The verse — *Potiphar's-wife's-confession* — narrates *the eventual-vindication* of *Joseph's truthfulness*. The verse-pattern: *truthfulness ultimately prevails*; *the lying-accusation is ultimately exposed*. *Sufficient-time* + *divine-design* = *truth-emergence*.

**9. The 'house-of-truthfulness' eschatological promise.**

[54:55]: "In a position of honor, at an Omnipotent King." — *fī maq`adi ṣidqin `inda malīkin muqtadir*.

The phrase *maq`ad ṣidq* — *seat of truthfulness* — is *the eschatological-honor-place* for *the righteous*. *The truthful in this-life* are *seated in the truthful-position in the next-life*. *The truthfulness-virtue produces the truthful-position-reward*.

[57:19]: "Those who believed in GOD and His messengers are the truthful ones. The witnesses at their Lord will receive their rewards and their light. As for those who disbelieved and rejected our revelations, they have incurred Hell." — *wa-lladhīna āmanū bi-llāhi wa-rusulihī ulā'ika humu ṣ-ṣiddīqūn*.

The verse identifies *the believers-in-God-and-His-messengers* as *the ṣiddīqūn* — *the truth-confirmers / the deeply-truthful*. The form *ṣiddīq* is *the intensified form of ṣādiq* — *the believer is not just truthful, but deeply-truthful*.

**10. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *truthfulness* is *foundational to authentic-submission*: *the Submitter cannot live a doubled-life* — *one face for God, another for humans*. *The salvific-truthfulness* at [5:119] is *the categorical-promise*: *the truthful are saved by their truthfulness on the Day of Judgment*.

The Khalifa-translation consistently renders *ṣādiq* as *truthful* and *ṣidq* as *truth / truthfulness* — preserving the *categorical-virtue character*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *ṣidq* — *truthfulness, honesty, true-statement* — is *foundational to believer-character*. *Be among the truthful* ([9:119]) — *the community-dimension*. *Truthful men and women* attract *forgiveness and great recompense* ([33:35]). *Speak the well-aimed correct word* ([33:70]) — *the precondition* for *divine-fixing-of-works*. *Truthfulness is the salvific-virtue on the Day of Judgment* ([5:119]) — *the truthful are saved by their truthfulness*. *The truthful are those who believe and strive with money and life* ([49:15]) — *truthfulness is alignment-of-life-with-confessed-belief*. *Life-testing distinguishes the truthful from the liars* ([29:3]). *The truthful-tongue* — *the lasting-honorable-reputation* of *the Abrahamic-lineage* ([19:50]); *Ishmael's promise-truthfulness* ([19:54]). *Joseph's truthfulness is vindicated by Potiphar's-wife's-confession* ([12:51]). *The eschatological seat-of-truthfulness* ([54:55]); *the believers as the ṣiddīqūn* — the deeply-truthful ([57:19]). The *Khalifa framework* identifies *truthfulness* as *foundational to authentic-submission* — *the Submitter cannot live a doubled-life*.

question: What does the Quran say about honesty in trade and measure?
answer:
The Quran takes *honesty in commercial-measurement* with extraordinary seriousness — *dedicating an entire surah* (*Al-Muṭaffifīn*, *The Defrauders*, 83) to *the cheating-in-measure problem*. The *prophet Shu`ayb*'s mission to *the people of Midian* centers on *commercial-honesty*; his preaching is *the most-detailed prophetic-commerce-ethics narrative* in the Quran. *Honesty in measure* is *not* a *minor commercial-regulation*; it is *a foundational-moral-virtue* tied to *the very-balance of cosmic-order*.

**1. The 'defrauders' surah opening.**

[83:1]: "Woe to the defrauders." — *waylun li-l-muṭaffifīn*.

[83:2]: "Who demand full measure when receiving from the people."

[83:3]: "But when giving them the measures or weights, they cheat."

[83:4]: "Do they not know that they will be resurrected?"

[83:5]: "On a tremendous day?"

[83:6]: "That is the day when all people will stand before the Lord of the universe."

The opening of *surah Al-Muṭaffifīn* establishes the *commercial-cheating problem* with *Quranic-moral-condemnation* at the *highest-intensity*: *waylun li-l-muṭaffifīn* — *woe to the defrauders*. The surah identifies the *specific cheating-pattern*: *demand full measure when receiving, cheat when giving*. The *theological-frame* — *do you not know you will be resurrected on a tremendous day?* — links *commercial-honesty* to *eschatological-accountability*.

**2. The 'give full measure' commands.**

[17:35]: "You shall give full measure when you trade, and weigh with an equitable balance. This is better and more righteous."

[6:152]: "...You shall give full weight and full measure when you trade, equitably. We do not burden any soul beyond its means. You shall be absolutely just when you bear witness, even against your relatives..."

[11:85] (Shu`ayb to the Midians): "'O my people, you shall give full measure and full weight, equitably. Do not cheat the people out of their rights, and do not roam the earth corruptingly.'"

The *full-measure-give-with-equitable-balance* command is *recurrent* across the Quran. The *standard formula*: *give full measure, weigh with the equitable balance*. The *theological-implication* — *this is better and more righteous* — places *honest-commerce* within *the broader-righteousness category*.

**3. The Shu`ayb mission to Midian.**

[7:85]: "To Midyan we sent their brother Shu`aib. He said, 'O my people, worship GOD; you have no other god beside Him. Proof has come to you from your Lord. You shall give full weight and full measure when you trade. Do not cheat the people out of their rights. Do not corrupt the earth after it has been set straight. This is better for you, if you are believers.'"

[11:84]: "To Midyan we sent their brother Shu`aib. He said, 'O my people, worship GOD; you have no other god beside Him. Do not cheat when you measure or weigh. I see that you are prosperous, and I fear for you the retribution of an overwhelming day.'"

[11:86]: "'Whatever GOD provides for you, no matter how small, is far better for you, if you are really believers. I am not a guardian over you.'"

The *Shu`ayb mission* establishes *the prophetic-commerce-ethics narrative*: (a) *worship God alone* (the standard-monotheism); (b) *give full measure and weight*; (c) *do not cheat people out of their rights*; (d) *do not corrupt the earth*; (e) *what God provides is better than the cheating-gain*. The *theological-frame*: *commercial-honesty* is *part of the monotheism-package* — *one cannot worship God alone while cheating fellow-humans*.

[26:181]–[26:183] (parallel Shu`ayb passage): "'You shall give full measure when you trade; do not cheat. You shall weigh with an equitable balance. Do not cheat the people out of their rights, and do not roam the earth corruptingly.'"

**4. The 'cosmic-balance' parallel.**

[55:7]: "He constructed the sky and established the law."

[55:8]: "You shall not transgress the law."

[55:9]: "You shall establish justice; do not violate the law."

The verses — *opening surah Ar-Raḥmān* — pair *the cosmic-balance* (*al-mīzān*, established by God) with *the human-balance* (*al-mīzān*, to be established by humans). The *theological-architecture*: *the cosmic-order is built on balance*; *the human-commercial-order must also be built on balance*. *Cheating in measure* is *not* just *commercial-fraud*; it is *cosmic-disorder-introduction*. The *human's balance* should *mirror the cosmic-balance*.

The use of *the same word* — *mīzān* — for *the cosmic-balance* and *the commercial-weighing-instrument* is *theologically deliberate*. The *commercial-mīzān* should *image the cosmic-mīzān*; *to cheat in the commercial-mīzān* is *to introduce-corruption-into-the-cosmic-image*.

**5. The 'cheat the people out of their rights' theme.**

The recurring phrase across Shu`ayb-passages — *lā tabkhasū n-nāsa ashyā'ahum* — *do not cheat the people out of their things* — establishes *commercial-cheating* as *a rights-violation*, not *a regulatory-infraction*. *The customer has a right* to *the full-measure-they-paid-for*; *cheating* is *the violation of that right*.

The *modern-relevance*: *short-weighing of products, false-product-claims, deceptive-pricing, hidden-fees, misleading-advertising* — *all are forms of* the *Quranic-bakhs* (*cheating-out-of-rights*).

**6. The 'do not corrupt the earth' clause.**

The recurring closing-clause — *wa-lā tufsidū fī l-arḍ ba`da iṣlāḥihā* — *do not corrupt the earth after it has been set right* — places *commercial-cheating* within *the broader-environmental-and-social-corruption category*. *Commercial-cheating* is *one form* of *fasād* (corruption); *the prophets are sent to set the earth right* (*iṣlāḥ*); *cheating-after-the-prophets' work* is *the worst-form* of *fasād*.

**7. The historical Midian destruction.**

[11:94]–[11:95]: "When our judgment came, we saved Shu`aib and those who believed with him, by mercy from us. As for the evil ones, they were struck by a disaster that left them dead in their homes. It was as if they never existed. Thus, Midyan was annihilated, just like Thamood was annihilated before that."

The verses *narrate* the *historical-destruction* of the *Midian-people* for *their commercial-cheating* (combined with *idolatry*). The *theological-message*: *cheating in commerce attracts divine-destruction* — *the same severity as polytheism*. *Midian's destruction* parallels *Thamud's*; both *destroyed-by-cosmic-disaster*.

**8. The 'tremendous day' framing.**

[83:5]–[83:6]: "On a tremendous day? That is the day when all people will stand before the Lord of the universe."

The *theological-frame* — *the Day of Judgment as the accounting-day* for *commercial-deals* — establishes *the divine-witness* over *every transaction*. *Every cheating-act* is *recorded*; *every short-weight* is *noted*; *every false-claim* is *documented*. The *accounting* will be *on the Tremendous Day*.

**9. The 'recorded book of the wicked' verses.**

[83:7]–[83:10]: "Indeed, the book of the wicked is in Sijjeen. Do you know what Sijjeen is? A numerically structured book. Woe on that day to the rejectors."

The surah continues: *the cheaters' deeds are recorded* in *Sijjīn* (a *sealed-book*); *the day of judgment will expose all*. The *commercial-cheating* is *not forgotten*; it is *recorded with mathematical-precision*. The cheater's *deceitful-gain* in *this-life* is *exchanged-for-disgrace* in *the next-life*.

**10. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *honesty in commerce* is *part of the comprehensive-righteousness package*. The Submitter cannot *pray, fast, give zakat* while *cheating in business* — *the integrity of the religious-life* requires *commercial-honesty*. The *Khalifa-translation* consistently renders the *commercial-honesty commands* with *categorical-emphasis*: *give full measure, weigh with the equitable balance, do not cheat the people out of their rights*.

The *Shu`ayb-mission* — *the prophet specifically-sent-to-commercial-cheaters* — is *the Quranic-paradigm* of *prophetic-engagement with the marketplace*. The *modern-application*: *every Submitter in business* should *measure their practices* against *the Shu`ayb-standard*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran takes *honesty in commercial-measurement* with extraordinary seriousness — *dedicating surah Al-Muṭaffifīn* to *the cheating-in-measure problem* ([83:1]–[83:6]). The *Shu`ayb-mission to Midian* ([7:85], [11:84]–[11:86], [26:181]–[26:183]) is *the most-detailed prophetic-commerce-ethics narrative* in the Quran. The *standard formula* — *give full measure and weight, weigh with the equitable balance, do not cheat the people out of their rights, do not corrupt the earth* — appears *recurrently* ([17:35], [6:152], [11:85], [26:181]). The *cosmic-balance parallel* at [55:7]–[55:9] (*al-mīzān*) — *the cosmic-balance is the model for the commercial-balance*; *cheating in measure is cosmic-disorder-introduction*. The *Midian-destruction* ([11:94]–[11:95]) — *cheating attracts divine-destruction* — *the same severity as polytheism*. The *eschatological-accounting* — *the cheaters' deeds are recorded in Sijjīn*; *every short-weight is noted*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *commercial-honesty* as *part of the comprehensive-righteousness package* — *the Submitter cannot pray and cheat*; *the integrity of the religious-life requires commercial-honesty*.

6:152.text: You shall not touch the orphans' money except in the most righteous manner, until they reach maturity. You shall give full weight and full measure when you trade, equitably. We do not burden any soul beyond its means. You shall be absolutely just when you bear witness, even against your relatives. You shall fulfill your covenant with GOD. These are His commandments to you, that you may take heed.
6:152.arabic: ولا تقربوا مال اليتيم إلا بالتي هي أحسن حتى يبلغ أشده وأوفوا الكيل والميزان بالقسط لا نكلف نفسا إلا وسعها وإذا قلتم فاعدلوا ولو كان ذا قربى وبعهد الله أوفوا ذلكم وصىكم به لعلكم تذكرون

7:85.text: To Midyan we sent their brother Shu`aib. He said, "O my people, worship GOD; you have no other god beside Him. Proof has come to you from your Lord. You shall give full weight and full measure when you trade. Do not cheat the people out of their rights. Do not corrupt the earth after it has been set straight. This is better for you, if you are believers.
7:85.arabic: وإلى مدين أخاهم شعيبا قال يقوم اعبدوا الله ما لكم من إله غيره قد جاءتكم بينة من ربكم فأوفوا الكيل والميزان ولا تبخسوا الناس أشياءهم ولا تفسدوا في الأرض بعد إصلحها ذلكم خير لكم إن كنتم مؤمنين

11:84.text: To Midyan we sent their brother Shu`aib. He said, "O my people, worship GOD; you have no other god beside Him. Do not cheat when you measure or weigh. I see that you are prosperous, and I fear for you the retribution of an overwhelming day.
11:84.arabic: ۞وإلى مدين أخاهم شعيبا قال يقوم اعبدوا الله ما لكم من إله غيره ولا تنقصوا المكيال والميزان إني أرىكم بخير وإني أخاف عليكم عذاب يوم محيط

11:85.text: "O my people, you shall give full measure and full weight, equitably. Do not cheat the people out of their rights, and do not roam the earth corruptingly.
11:85.arabic: ويقوم أوفوا المكيال والميزان بالقسط ولا تبخسوا الناس أشياءهم ولا تعثوا في الأرض مفسدين

11:86.text: "Whatever GOD provides for you, no matter how small, is far better for you, if you are really believers. I am not a guardian over you."
11:86.arabic: بقيت الله خير لكم إن كنتم مؤمنين وما أنا عليكم بحفيظ

17:35.text: You shall give full measure when you trade, and weigh equitably. This is better and more righteous.
17:35.arabic: وأوفوا الكيل إذا كلتم وزنوا بالقسطاس المستقيم ذلك خير وأحسن تأويلا

26:181.text: "You shall give full measure when you trade; do not cheat.

26:182.text: "You shall weigh with an equitable scale.

26:183.text: "Do not cheat the people out of their rights, and do not roam the earth corruptingly.
26:183.arabic: ولا تبخسوا الناس أشياءهم ولا تعثوا في الأرض مفسدين

55:7.text: He constructed the sky and established the law.

55:8.text: You shall not transgress the law.

55:9.text: You shall establish justice; do not violate the law.
55:9.arabic: وأقيموا الوزن بالقسط ولا تخسروا الميزان

83:2.text: Who demand full measure when receiving from the people.
83:2.arabic: الذين إذا اكتالوا على الناس يستوفون

83:3.text: But when giving them the measures or weights, they cheat.

83:4.text: Do they not know that they will be resurrected?

83:6.text: That is the day when all people will stand before the Lord of the universe.

question: What duties does the Quran assign children toward their parents?
answer:
The Quran's *filial-duty* command is *one of the most-emphasized practical-ethical commands* in the scripture. Multiple verses *pair the worship-of-God* with *kindness-to-parents* — making *parent-honor* the *second-priority* after *the monotheism-commitment itself*. The *operational-vocabulary* (*iḥsān, birr, qawl karīm, junāḥ ar-raḥmah*) maps *a layered-set of duties*: *kind-treatment, gracious-speech, humility-of-attitude, protection-from-distress, prayer-for-them*. The *Quranic-framing* is *particularly-tender* toward *aged-parents*.

**1. The foundational verses.**

[17:23]: "Your Lord has decreed that you shall not worship except Him, and your parents shall be honored. As long as one or both of them live, you shall never say to them, 'Uff' (the slightest gesture of annoyance), nor shall you shout at them; you shall treat them amicably." — *wa-qaḍā rabbuka allā ta`budū illā iyyāhu wa-bi-l-wālidayni iḥsānā*.

[17:24]: "And lower for them the wings of humility, and kindness, and say, 'My Lord, have mercy on them, for they have raised me from infancy.'"

The verse-pair is *the central-filial-duty passage* in the Quran. *Six operational-components*:

(a) *Bi-l-wālidayni iḥsānā* — *to the two-parents iḥsān* — *the categorical-duty*.

(b) *Lā taqul lahumā uff* — *do not say "uff"* (the smallest sound of annoyance, the *uff* of frustration).

(c) *Wa-lā tanharhumā* — *do not shout at them*.

(d) *Wa-qul lahumā qawlan karīmā* — *speak to them with gracious-speech*.

(e) *Wa-khfiḍ lahumā janāḥa dh-dhulli mina r-raḥmah* — *lower for them the wing of humility from mercy*.

(f) *Wa-qul rabbi rḥamhumā kamā rabbayānī ṣaghīrā* — *and say: My Lord, have mercy on them as they raised me as a small child*.

The *pairing of "worship-Him-alone"* with *"honor-parents"* establishes *parent-honor* as *the second-priority*. The *prohibition of even saying "uff"* sets *the standard* at *zero-toleration of any disrespect*.

**2. The 'thank Me and your parents' verse.**

[31:14]: "We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. His mother bore him, and the load got heavier and heavier. It takes two years (of intensive care) until weaning. You shall be appreciative of Me, and of your parents. To Me is the ultimate destiny." — *an ushkur lī wa-li-wālidayk*.

The verse — *Luqman's wisdom-context* — establishes *thanksgiving-to-parents* alongside *thanksgiving-to-God*. The *theological-pairing* is *unique* — *no other-creature* is *named alongside God* as *a thanksgiving-recipient*. *Parents alone* share *the thanksgiving-honor* with *God*.

The verse *emphasizes* the *maternal-burden* — *the mother bore him, and the load got heavier and heavier, and weaning took two years*. The *biological-cost of motherhood* is *named explicitly* to *ground* the *daughter-and-son's-debt*.

**3. The 'except in idolatry' qualification.**

[31:15]: "If they try to force you to set up any idols beside Me, do not obey them. But continue to treat them amicably in this world. You shall follow only the path of those who have submitted to Me. Ultimately, you all return to Me, then I will inform you of everything you have done."

[29:8]: "We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. But if they try to force you to set up any idols beside Me, do not obey them. To Me is your ultimate return, then I will inform you of everything you had done."

The two verses *qualify* the *parent-honor command*: *obedience-in-everything-except idolatry*. *Parents cannot command idolatry*; *if they do*, the *child must refuse*. But *the refusal* must *still be coupled* with *amicable-treatment*: *do not obey their idolatry-command, but treat them amicably in this world*.

The *theological-architecture*: *the worship-of-God-alone is the absolute-first-priority*; *parent-honor is the strong-second-priority*; *the second cannot override the first* — *but the first does not eliminate the second*.

**4. The 'recommended in the Quran's commands' verse.**

[4:36]: "You shall worship GOD alone - do not associate anything with Him. You shall regard the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, the related neighbor, the unrelated neighbor, the close associate, the traveling alien, and your servants. GOD does not like the arrogant show-offs." — *wa-bi-l-wālidayni iḥsānā*.

The verse — the *operational-iḥsān-list* — places *parents-first* on the *nine-category-list* of *iḥsān-recipients*. *Parents are the first of the nine*; *the closest-relational priority*.

**5. The 'forty-year prayer' verse.**

[46:15]: "We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. His mother bore him arduously, gave birth to him arduously, and took intimate care of him for thirty months. When he reaches maturity, and reaches the age of forty, he should say, 'My Lord, direct me to appreciate the blessings You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do the righteous works that please You. Let my children be righteous as well. I have repented to You; I am a submitter.'"

The verse *models* the *adult-child's-prayer*: *prayer for parents, prayer for righteous-children, repentance, declaration of submission*. The *age-of-forty* is *significant* — *the maturity-threshold* at which the *adult-child* should *make this prayer-formula*.

The verse *parallels* the *biological-burden-emphasis* of [31:14] — *the mother's bearing, birthing, intimate-care for thirty months*. The *biological-debt* is *named explicitly* to *ground* the *adult-child's-prayer-duty*.

[46:17]: "Then there is the one who says to his parents, 'Woe to you; are you telling me that (after death) I will come back to life? How come those who died before us never come back?' The parents would cry for GOD's help and say, 'Woe to you; please believe! GOD's promise is the truth.' He would say, 'Tales from the past!'"

The verse *contrasts* the *righteous-adult-child* of [46:15] with *the disbelieving-adult-child* of [46:17]. The *contrast* shows the *Quranic-recognition* that *not all adult-children* respond to *parental-instruction* — *some reject it*. The Quranic-judgment is *severe* — *the disbeliever-child* is *categorized with the rejected-of-the-prior-nations*.

**6. The Yahya-and-Jesus filial-models.**

[19:14] (about John): "He honored his parents, and he was never a disobedient tyrant." — *wa-barran bi-wālidayh*.

[19:32] (Jesus speaking): "'I am to honor my mother; He did not make me a disobedient tyrant.'" — *wa-barran bi-wālidatī*.

Two of the *Quranic-prophet-models* — *John (Yaḥyā)* and *Jesus (`Īsā)* — are *characterized* by *barr bi-wālidayh / wālidatih* — *parent-honor*. The *parent-honor virtue* is *one of the defining-prophetic-characteristics* — *not* an *optional-add-on*.

The contrast — *was never a disobedient tyrant* — is *theologically-significant*: *the alternative* to *parent-honor* is *disobedient-tyranny*. The *Quranic-categorization* is *bipolar*: *either parent-honor or disobedient-tyranny*; *no middle-ground*.

**7. The 'lower the wing of mercy-humility' image.**

[17:24]'s *wa-khfiḍ lahumā janāḥa dh-dhulli mina r-raḥmah* — *lower for them the wing of humility from mercy* — is *one of the most-tender images* in the Quran.

The image: *a bird lowering its wing protectively* over *its young* — *but here inverted* — *the adult-child lowers the wing* over *the elderly-parent*. *The aged-parent becomes the protected-one*; *the adult-child becomes the protective-bird*. The *role-reversal* is *the Quranic-recognition* of *the life-cycle*: *parents protected children for years*; *adult-children must now protect aged-parents*.

The *qualifier* — *mina r-raḥmah* — *from mercy* — *names the motive*: *not from obligation, not from social-pressure, but from mercy* — *the same mercy parents extended to the infant*.

**8. The 'pray for them' duty.**

[17:24]'s *wa-qul rabbi rḥamhumā kamā rabbayānī ṣaghīrā* — *and say: My Lord, have mercy on them as they raised me as a small child* — establishes *the prayer-for-parents duty*.

The *theological-principle*: *adult-children's prayer is one of the things that continues to benefit parents after parents' death*. The *Quran does not explicitly state this principle* in *one verse*, but *the prayer-instruction at [17:24]* is *not time-limited* — *the prayer continues* even *after the parents' death*.

The *Khalifa-translation* of the *prayer-content* — *as they raised me as a small child* — preserves the *reciprocal-mercy theme*: *the prayer asks God for the same mercy on the parents that the parents extended to the infant*.

**9. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *parent-honor* is *the second-priority after monotheism* — *as the Quran explicitly establishes* at [17:23] and [31:14]. The Submitter's *daily-practical-religion* must *include parent-honor* — *no Submitter can claim authentic-submission while disrespecting parents*. The *Khalifa-translation* consistently renders the *parent-duty commands* with *categorical-clarity*: *do not even say "uff"*; *lower the wing of humility from mercy*; *say "My Lord, have mercy on them as they raised me as a small child"*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *filial-duty* command is *the second-priority after monotheism*. *Six operational-components* at [17:23]–[17:24]: *iḥsān to both parents, no "uff" of annoyance, no shouting, gracious-speech, lowering the wing of humility from mercy, prayer-for-them*. *Thank Me and your parents* at [31:14] — *parents alone share the thanksgiving-honor with God*. *Obedience-in-everything-except idolatry* at [31:15] and [29:8] — *if parents command idolatry, refuse, but still treat them amicably*. *Parents first on the nine-category iḥsān-list* at [4:36]. *The forty-year prayer* at [46:15]: *direct me to appreciate the blessings You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents*. *John* ([19:14]) and *Jesus* ([19:32]) are *characterized by parent-honor* — *one of the defining-prophetic-characteristics*. *The lower-the-wing-of-mercy-humility image* at [17:24] — *the adult-child becomes the protective-bird over the aged-parent*. *Prayer-for-parents* — *as they raised me as a small child* — establishes *the reciprocal-mercy theme*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *parent-honor* as *the second-priority after monotheism* — *the Submitter cannot claim authentic-submission while disrespecting parents*.

17:23.text: Your Lord has decreed that you shall not worship except Him, and your parents shall be honored. As long as one or both of them live, you shall never say to them, "Uff" (the slightest gesture of annoyance), nor shall you shout at them; you shall treat them amicably.
17:23.arabic: ۞وقضى ربك ألا تعبدوا إلا إياه وبالولدين إحسنا إما يبلغن عندك الكبر أحدهما أو كلاهما فلا تقل لهما أف ولا تنهرهما وقل لهما قولا كريما

17:24.text: And lower for them the wings of humility, and kindness, and say, "My Lord, have mercy on them, for they have raised me from infancy."
17:24.arabic: واخفض لهما جناح الذل من الرحمة وقل رب ارحمهما كما ربياني صغيرا

29:8.text: We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. But if they try to force you to set up idols beside Me, do not obey them. To Me is your ultimate return, then I will inform you of everything you had done.
29:8.arabic: ووصينا الإنسن بولديه حسنا وإن جهداك لتشرك بي ما ليس لك به علم فلا تطعهما إلي مرجعكم فأنبئكم بما كنتم تعملون

31:14.text: We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. His mother bore him, and the load got heavier and heavier. It takes two years (of intensive care) until weaning. You shall be appreciative of Me, and of your parents. To Me is the ultimate destiny.
31:14.arabic: ووصينا الإنسن بولديه حملته أمه وهنا على وهن وفصله في عامين أن اشكر لي ولولديك إلي المصير

31:15.text: If they try to force you to set up any idols beside Me, do not obey them. But continue to treat them amicably in this world. You shall follow only the path of those who have submitted to Me. Ultimately, you all return to Me, then I will inform you of everything you have done.
31:15.arabic: وإن جهداك على أن تشرك بي ما ليس لك به علم فلا تطعهما وصاحبهما في الدنيا معروفا واتبع سبيل من أناب إلي ثم إلي مرجعكم فأنبئكم بما كنتم تعملون

46:15.text: We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. His mother bore him arduously, gave birth to him arduously, and took intimate care of him for thirty months. When he reaches maturity, and reaches the age of forty, he should say, "My Lord, direct me to appreciate the blessings You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do the righteous works that please You. Let my children be righteous as well. I have repented to You; I am a submitter."
46:15.arabic: ووصينا الإنسن بولديه إحسنا حملته أمه كرها ووضعته كرها وحمله وفصله ثلثون شهرا حتى إذا بلغ أشده وبلغ أربعين سنة قال رب أوزعني أن أشكر نعمتك التي أنعمت علي وعلى ولدي وأن أعمل صلحا ترضىه وأصلح لي في ذريتي إني تبت إليك وإني من المسلمين

46:17.text: Then there is the one who says to his parents, "Woe to you; are you telling me that (after death) I will come back to life? How come those who died before us never come back?" The parents would cry for GOD's help and say, "Woe to you; please believe! GOD's promise is the truth." He would say, "Tales from the past!"
46:17.arabic: والذي قال لولديه أف لكما أتعدانني أن أخرج وقد خلت القرون من قبلي وهما يستغيثان الله ويلك ءامن إن وعد الله حق فيقول ما هذا إلا أسطير الأولين

46:18.text: Such are the ones stamped as disbelievers among every generation of jinns and humans; they are losers.
46:18.arabic: أولـئك الذين حق عليهم القول في أمم قد خلت من قبلهم من الجن والإنس إنهم كانوا خسرين

4:36.text: You shall worship GOD alone - do not associate anything with Him. You shall regard the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, the related neighbor, the unrelated neighbor, the close associate, the traveling alien, and your servants. GOD does not like the arrogant showoffs.
4:36.arabic: ۞واعبدوا الله ولا تشركوا به شيـا وبالولدين إحسنا وبذي القربى واليتمى والمسكين والجار ذي القربى والجار الجنب والصاحب بالجنب وابن السبيل وما ملكت أيمنكم إن الله لا يحب من كان مختالا فخورا

19:14.text: He honored his parents, and was never a disobedient tyrant.
19:14.arabic: وبرا بولديه ولم يكن جبارا عصيا

19:32.arabic: وبرا بولدتي ولم يجعلني جبارا شقيا

question: How does the Quran instruct treatment of neighbours?
answer:
The Quran's *neighbor-ethics* is *one of the most-distinctive features* of *Quranic-social-ethics*. The *neighbor-list* at [4:36] *distinguishes* between *the related-neighbor* (*al-jār dhū l-qurbā*) and *the unrelated-neighbor* (*al-jār al-junub*) — establishing *neighbor-treatment* as *a categorical-duty regardless of relationship-distance*. The *Quranic-neighbor* extends from *the immediate-physical-neighbor* to *the broader-community-membership*, and the *characteristic-virtues* of *neighbor-treatment* are *iḥsān, generosity, non-harm, and active-helpfulness*.

**1. The foundational verse.**

[4:36]: "You shall worship GOD alone - do not associate anything with Him. You shall regard the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, the related neighbor, the unrelated neighbor, the close associate, the traveling alien, and your servants. GOD does not like the arrogant show-offs."

The verse — the *operational-iḥsān-list* — explicitly distinguishes *two neighbor-categories*:

(a) *Al-jār dhū l-qurbā* — *the related-neighbor* (the *neighbor who is also a relative*).

(b) *Al-jār al-junub* — *the unrelated-neighbor* (the *neighbor who is not a relative*).

The *Quranic-specificity* in *naming both categories* — and giving them *equal iḥsān-treatment* — is *theologically-significant*: *iḥsān-to-neighbor is not contingent on tribal-or-family relationship*; *it extends to any-resident-of-one's-physical-vicinity*.

The verse also names *al-ṣāḥib bi-l-janb* — *the close associate / the side-companion* — sometimes interpreted as *the work-colleague*, *the travel-companion*, or *the spouse*. The *modern-application*: *neighbor-ethics extends to office-colleagues, online-community-members, fellow-passengers*.

**2. The 'true-religion-test' surah.**

[107:1]: "Do you know who really rejects the faith?"

[107:2]: "That is the one who mistreats the orphans."

[107:3]: "And does not advocate the feeding of the poor."

[107:4]: "And woe to those who observe the contact prayers (Salat) -"

[107:5]: "Who are totally heedless of their prayers."

[107:6]: "They only show off."

[107:7]: "And they forbid charity."

Surah *Al-Mā`ūn* (*The Common Goodness*) — *one of the shortest surahs* — establishes the *startling claim*: *the rejector-of-faith is identified by mistreating orphans, not advocating feeding the poor, performing prayers heedlessly for show, and forbidding small kindnesses*.

The closing-clause — *forbid charity* (*wa-yamna`ūna l-mā`ūn*) — *literally*: *withhold the common-utilities*. *Al-mā`ūn* refers to *the small-everyday-borrowings between neighbors* — *a cup of salt, a bucket, a tool*. The surah *identifies* the *refusal of small-everyday-neighborly-borrowings* as *evidence of faith-rejection*.

The *theological-implication* is *striking*: *prayer-performance is insufficient*; *the proof of faith* is *in the small-everyday-neighborly-helpfulness*.

**3. The 'do not be the kind' verse.**

[2:177]'s comprehensive-righteousness verse includes — among the *eight components* of *righteousness* — *giving the money cheerfully to the relatives, the orphans, the needy, the traveling alien, the beggars, and to free the slaves*. The *traveling alien* (*ibn as-sabīl*) and *the beggars* are *the broader-neighbor-categories*.

**4. The 'climb the difficult path' verses.**

[90:13]: "The freeing of slaves."

[90:14]: "Feeding, during the time of hardship."

[90:15]: "Orphans who are related."

[90:16]: "Or the poor who is in need."

[90:17]: "And being one of those who believe, and exhorting one another to be steadfast, and exhorting one another to be kind."

[90:18]: "These have deserved happiness."

Surah *Al-Balad* — *the climb of the difficult path* — identifies *the steep-path* of *righteousness* as *concrete-acts of social-helpfulness*: *slave-freeing, feeding-in-famine, orphan-care* (especially *the relative-orphan*), *poor-feeding*. The *neighbor-ethics* extends to *all those in need within one's reach*.

The closing-clause — *exhorting one another to kindness* (*tawāṣaw bi-l-marḥamah*) — establishes *the community-dimension*: *neighbor-ethics is mutually-exhorted in the believer-community*.

**5. The Quranic-vocabulary of neighbor.**

The Arabic *jār* (neighbor) is used in *multiple contexts*:

(a) *Al-jār dhū l-qurbā* — *the related-neighbor* (relative-residing-nearby).

(b) *Al-jār al-junub* — *the unrelated-neighbor* (non-relative-residing-nearby).

(c) The verb *jāwara* — *to be a neighbor / to live next-to* — used elsewhere for *broader proximity-relationships*.

The *Quranic-distinction* between *related-and-unrelated neighbors* — *with equal iḥsān-duty* — is *unique* in *the comparative-ancient-ethics literature*. *Greek polis-ethics* prioritized *fellow-citizens over foreigners*. *Roman patron-client* prioritized *the lower in the patron-network*. *Hebrew Levitical* prioritized *fellow-Hebrews over Gentiles*. The *Quranic-neighbor-ethics* is *non-discriminatory by residence-proximity alone* — *the unrelated-neighbor* deserves *the same iḥsān* as *the related-neighbor*.

**6. The 'helping the wayfarer' duty.**

The *wayfarer* (*ibn as-sabīl*) recurs throughout the Quran as *a category for charitable-assistance*. The *Quranic-frame* — *the traveler stranded-away-from-resources* — is *the prototype-helpless-neighbor*. *Helping the wayfarer* is *one form* of *neighbor-ethics* — *the temporary-resident-of-one's-vicinity* deserves *iḥsān*.

**7. The 'do not harm' negative-formulation.**

The *hadith-tradition* identifies *the negative-pole* of *neighbor-ethics*: *do not harm your neighbor*. *The Quranic-source* for this is *the general-prohibition-of-corruption* (*lā tufsidū fī l-arḍ*) and *the iḥsān-formula* itself (*iḥsān = positive-good-treatment*; *the negation = no-harm*).

The *neighbor-rights*, by tradition, include: (a) *no physical-harm*; (b) *no verbal-harm*; (c) *no harm-to-property*; (d) *no encroachment-on-living-space*; (e) *no disturbance-of-peace*; (f) *no disrespect-of-privacy*.

**8. The 'do not enter without permission' privacy-rules.**

[24:27]: "O you who believe, do not enter homes other than yours without permission from their inhabitants, and without greeting them. This is better for you, that you may take heed."

[24:28]: "If you find no one in them, do not enter them until you obtain permission. If you are told, 'Go back,' you must go back. This is purer for you. GOD is fully aware of everything you do."

The verses establish *the privacy-and-permission code* for *visiting neighbors*: (a) *no entry without permission*; (b) *no entry without greeting*; (c) *if told to go back, go back*; (d) *if no one is in, do not enter*.

The *Quranic-emphasis on privacy* is *part of the neighbor-ethics*: *the neighbor's home is the neighbor's private-space*; *iḥsān-to-neighbor includes respecting-the-neighbor's-privacy*.

**9. The historical Quranic-neighbor-norms.**

The *7th-century Arabian-Quranic-neighbor norms*:

(a) *Shared-water-rights* — *the well/oasis* was *neighbor-shared*; *no neighbor* could *monopolize the water-source*.

(b) *Shared-livestock-grazing* — *common pastoral-land* shared between *neighbor-tribes*.

(c) *Hospitality-obligations* — *the unexpected guest* must be *received and fed*.

(d) *Cooperation-in-distress* — *neighbor-villages* assisted *one another* in *raid-recovery, famine-relief, defense*.

The *Quranic-neighbor-ethics* preserves *the positive-features* of *these pre-Islamic norms* while *purifying* them of *tribal-discrimination* (*the unrelated-neighbor* receives *the same treatment as the related-neighbor*).

**10. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *neighbor-ethics* is *one of the operational-tests* of *authentic-submission*. The Submitter's *daily-practical-religion* must *include neighbor-helpfulness*. The *Khalifa-translation* of *surah Al-Mā`ūn* renders *the rejecting-of-faith identifier* as *one who mistreats orphans, does not feed the poor, prays for show, and forbids charity* — *foregrounding* the *practical-neighbor-ethics test*.

The *modern-application*: *neighbor-ethics* extends to *apartment-building neighbors, office-colleagues, online-community-members, fellow-passengers*. The *Quranic-principle* — *no discrimination by relationship-distance* — applies *to all* of these *modern neighbor-categories*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *neighbor-ethics* is *distinctive* in *establishing equal iḥsān-duty* toward *both the related-neighbor and the unrelated-neighbor* ([4:36]). *The neighbor-list* extends to *parents, relatives, orphans, poor, related-neighbor, unrelated-neighbor, close-associate, traveling-alien, servants* — *nine categories of iḥsān-recipients*. *Surah Al-Mā`ūn* ([107:1]–[107:7]) establishes the *striking claim*: *the rejector-of-faith is identified by mistreating orphans, not advocating feeding the poor, prayer-for-show, and forbidding small everyday-neighborly-borrowings*. *Surah Al-Balad* ([90:13]–[90:18]) identifies *the steep-path of righteousness* as *slave-freeing, famine-feeding, orphan-care, poor-feeding, mutual-exhortation to kindness*. The *privacy-and-permission code* at [24:27]–[24:28] establishes *neighbor-home privacy* as *part of neighbor-ethics*. The *Quranic-distinction between related-and-unrelated neighbors with equal iḥsān-duty* is *unique* in *comparative-ancient-ethics literature*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *neighbor-ethics* as *operational-test of authentic-submission* — *the Submitter cannot pray-for-show while refusing small everyday-neighborly-helpfulness*.

4:36.text: You shall worship GOD alone - do not associate anything with Him. You shall regard the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, the related neighbor, the unrelated neighbor, the close associate, the traveling alien, and your servants. GOD does not like the arrogant showoffs.

These are the truthful; these are the righteous.

question: What does the Quran say about the rights of orphans?
answer:
The Quran's *orphan-care* is *one of the most-frequently-emphasized social-ethics commands* — appearing *over 20 times* across the scripture. The *orphan* (*yatīm*) — *the child whose father has died* — is *paradigmatically vulnerable* and *receives extensive Quranic-protection*. The *Prophet Muhammad himself was an orphan* (his father died before his birth, his mother when he was a child) — a *biographical-fact* the Quran *explicitly references* ([93:6]–[93:9]) and *uses as the model* for *the orphan-care command*.

**1. The 'do not consume the orphan's property' commands.**

[4:2]: "You shall hand over to the orphans their rightful properties. Do not substitute the bad for the good, and do not consume their properties by combining them with yours. This would be a gross injustice." — *wa-ātū l-yatāmā amwālahum*.

[4:10]: "Those who consume the orphans' properties unjustly, eat fire into their bellies, and will suffer in a blazing Hell." — *innamā ya'kulūna fī buṭūnihim nārā, wa-sa-yaṣlawna sa`īrā*.

[6:152]: "Do not touch the orphans' money except in the most righteous manner, until they reach maturity..."

[17:34]: "You shall not touch the orphans' money except for their own good, until they reach maturity..."

The recurring command — *do not consume the orphan's property* — is *one of the most-categorically-prohibited acts* in the Quran. The *theological-imagery* at [4:10] — *consuming the orphan's property is literally eating fire into one's belly* — is *one of the most-vivid sin-consequence images* in the scripture.

**2. The 'test the orphans before releasing their property' verse.**

[4:6]: "You shall test the orphans when they reach puberty. As soon as you find them mature enough, give them their property. Do not consume it extravagantly in a hurry, before they grow up. The rich guardian shall not charge any wage, but the poor guardian may charge equitably. When you give them their properties, you shall have witnesses. GOD suffices as Reckoner."

The verse establishes *the operational-procedure* for *the orphan's-property-administration*: (a) *test the orphans when they reach puberty* — *intellectual-maturity-assessment*; (b) *if mature, give them their property*; (c) *do not consume the property extravagantly*; (d) *the rich-guardian works for free*; (e) *the poor-guardian may charge equitable-compensation*; (f) *transfer with witnesses*.

The *Quranic-procedure* is *theologically-rigorous*: *the guardianship is a trust*; *the transfer must be documented*; *the maturity-test prevents premature-release-to-incompetent-adolescents*.

**3. The 'orphans on the giving-list' verses.**

[2:177]: "...righteous are those who... give the money, cheerfully, to the relatives, the orphans, the needy, the traveling alien, the beggars, and to free the slaves..."

[2:215]: "They ask you about giving: say, 'The charity you give shall go to the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor, and the traveling alien.' Any good you do, GOD is fully aware thereof."

[4:36]: "...You shall regard the parents, the relatives, the orphans, the poor..."

[76:8]: "They donate their favorite food to the poor, the orphan, and the captive."

[90:14]: "Feeding, during the time of hardship."

[90:15]: "Orphans who are related."

The recurring inclusion of *orphans* in *the standard-charity-list* establishes *orphan-feeding-and-clothing* as *one of the routine-charity-categories*. The *Quranic-emphasis* is *categorical* — *no comprehensive-charity-list omits orphans*.

[107:2]: "That is the one who mistreats the orphans." — *the surah Al-Mā`ūn's identification of the faith-rejector as one who mistreats orphans*.

**4. The 'do not push the orphan' verses.**

[93:9]: "Therefore, you shall not wrong the orphan." — *fa-ammā l-yatīma fa-lā taqhar*.

[89:17]: "Wrong! It is you who brought it on yourselves by not regarding the orphan."

The Arabic verb *qahara* (used at [93:9]) means *to oppress, suppress, dominate-harshly*. *Do not qahara the orphan* — *do not press the orphan down, do not dominate the orphan*. The Quranic-vocabulary preserves *the asymmetric-power-relationship* between *the guardian/adult* and *the orphan* — *the guardian must not exploit the asymmetry*.

[89:17] expands: *the disrespect-of-orphans* is *one of the principal-sins* God *holds against the wicked* on the *Day of Judgment*.

**5. The Prophet's-biographical-context.**

[93:6]: "Did He not find you orphaned and gave you a home?"

[93:7]: "He found you astray, and guided you."

[93:8]: "He found you poor, and made you rich."

The verses — *the early-Meccan surah Aḍ-Ḍuḥā* — *explicitly reference* the *Prophet's-orphan-childhood*: *He found you an orphan, and sheltered you*. The *biographical-fact* — *Muhammad was an orphan* — is *theologically-significant* and *grounds* the *Quranic-orphan-care command*: *God Himself sheltered the Prophet-as-orphan*; *therefore* the believers must *do likewise for other orphans*.

The closing verse [93:9]–[93:11] — *do not wrong the orphan, do not deny the beggar, recount your Lord's blessings* — establishes *the operational-response* to *God's mercy on the Prophet-as-orphan*: *extend the same mercy to other orphans*.

**6. The marriage-with-orphans verse.**

[4:3]: "If you deem it best for the orphans, you may marry their mothers - you may marry two, three, or four. If you fear lest you become unfair, then you shall be content with only one, or with what you already have. Additionally, you are thus more likely to avoid financial hardship."

The verse — *contextually-linked* to *the orphan-property-administration* — addresses *the problem of orphan-protection*: *if the orphan-property cannot be properly-protected by the existing-arrangement*, *one option* is *to marry the orphan's mother* (thus *integrating the orphan into one's family*). The *polygyny-permission* in this verse is *originally-contextualized* by *the orphan-care problem*.

**7. The 'orphan's-marriage-rights' verse.**

[4:127]: "They consult you concerning women: say, 'GOD enlightens you regarding them, as recited for you in the scripture. You shall restore the rights of orphaned girls whom you cheat out of their due dowries when you wish to marry them: you shall be equitable to the helpless children, and to deal equitably with the orphans...'"

The verse extends *orphan-protection* to *the orphan-girl's-marriage-rights*: *do not cheat the orphan-girl of her dowry*; *be equitable in the marriage-arrangement*. *Orphan-girls* are *particularly-vulnerable* to *exploitation* in *marriage-arrangements*; *the Quran specifically-protects them*.

**8. The 'orphan's questions' verse.**

[2:220]: "...They consult you about the orphans: say, 'Bringing them up as righteous persons is the best you can do for them. If you mix their property with yours, you shall treat them as family members.' GOD knows the righteous and the wicked. Had GOD willed, He could have imposed harsher rules upon you. GOD is Almighty, Most Wise."

The verse responds to *the practical-question*: *how should the orphan's-property be administered* if *the guardian mixes property* with *family-property*? The answer: *if you mix property, treat the orphans as family-members*. *Mixing is permitted* only on the condition *of treating-as-family*.

The *theological-frame*: *bringing up the orphans as righteous-persons* is *the best you can do for them* — *the religious-character formation* is *the highest-priority* of *orphan-care*; *the property-administration* is *secondary*.

**9. The cultural-context.**

The *pre-Islamic Arabian context* of *orphan-care*:

(a) *The orphan-property* was *often consumed by the tribe* — *no individual-property-rights for minors*.

(b) *Orphan-girls were sometimes married off* without *dowry or consent*.

(c) *Orphans were treated as servants* in the *guardian-family*.

(d) *In some cases, orphans were exposed* or *enslaved*.

The *Quranic-orphan-protections* — *property-rights, dowry-rights, religious-character-formation, family-integration* — *categorically-improved* the *orphan's-status* compared to *the pre-Islamic norm*.

**10. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *orphan-care* is *one of the operational-tests of authentic-submission*. The *Khalifa-translation* of *surah Al-Mā`ūn* identifies *the faith-rejector* as *one who mistreats orphans*; the Submitter's *daily-practical-religion* must include *active-orphan-care*.

The *Khalifa-emphasis*: *orphan-care* is *not* a *charitable-option*; it is *a categorical-duty*. The *modern-application*: *foster-care, adoption-support, orphanage-funding, orphan-scholarship-programs* — *all are forms* of *the Quranic-orphan-care command*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *orphan-care* command is *one of the most-frequently-emphasized social-ethics commands*. *Do not consume the orphan's property* ([4:2], [4:10], [6:152], [17:34]) — *the consequence is literally eating fire into one's belly*. *Test the orphans before releasing property* ([4:6]) — *operational-procedure for property-administration*. *Orphans on the standard-charity-list* ([2:177], [2:215], [4:36], [76:8], [90:14]–[90:15], [107:2]). *Do not push the orphan down* ([93:9]); *the wicked are categorized by their disrespect of orphans* ([89:17]). *The Prophet's-orphan-childhood* at [93:6] *grounds* the *orphan-care command*: *God sheltered the Prophet-as-orphan; therefore the believers must shelter other orphans*. *Marriage-with-orphans context* at [4:3] (the polygyny-permission was originally-contextualized by the orphan-care problem). *Orphan-girls' marriage-rights* at [4:127]. *Bringing them up as righteous-persons is the best you can do for them* ([2:220]) — *religious-character formation* is *the highest-priority*. The *pre-Islamic Arabian context* — *no orphan-property-rights, exploitation in marriage, treatment-as-servants* — was *categorically-improved* by *the Quranic-orphan-protections*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *orphan-care* as *operational-test of authentic-submission* — *not a charitable-option but a categorical-duty*.
