2:83.arabic: وإذ أخذنا ميثق بني إسرـءيل لا تعبدون إلا الله وبالولدين إحسانا وذي القربى واليتمى والمسكين وقولوا للناس حسنا وأقيموا الصلوة وءاتوا الزكوة ثم توليتم إلا قليلا منكم وأنتم معرضون

4:5.text: Do not give immature orphans the properties that GOD has entrusted with you as guardians. You shall provide for them therefrom, and clothe them, and treat them kindly.
4:5.arabic: ولا تؤتوا السفهاء أمولكم التي جعل الله لكم قيما وارزقوهم فيها واكسوهم وقولوا لهم قولا معروفا

4:8.text: During distribution of the inheritances, if relatives, orphans, and needy persons are present, you shall give them therefrom, and treat them kindly.
4:8.arabic: وإذا حضر القسمة أولوا القربى واليتمى والمسكين فارزقوهم منه وقولوا لهم قولا معروفا

17:23.text: Your Lord has decreed that you shall not worship except Him, and your parents shall be honored. As long as one or both of them live, you shall never say to them, "Uff" (the slightest gesture of annoyance), nor shall you shout at them; you shall treat them amicably.
17:23.arabic: ۞وقضى ربك ألا تعبدوا إلا إياه وبالولدين إحسنا إما يبلغن عندك الكبر أحدهما أو كلاهما فلا تقل لهما أف ولا تنهرهما وقل لهما قولا كريما

17:28.text: Even if you have to turn away from them, as you pursue the mercy of your Lord, you shall treat them in the nicest manner.
17:28.arabic: وإما تعرضن عنهم ابتغاء رحمة من ربك ترجوها فقل لهم قولا ميسورا

33:32.text: O wives of the prophet, you are not the same as any other women, if you observe righteousness. (You have a greater responsibility.) Therefore, you shall not speak too softly, lest those with disease in their hearts may get the wrong ideas; you shall speak only righteousness.
33:32.arabic: ينساء النبي لستن كأحد من النساء إن اتقيتن فلا تخضعن بالقول فيطمع الذي في قلبه مرض وقلن قولا معروفا

33:70.text: O you who believe, reverence GOD and utter only the correct utterances.
33:70.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا اتقوا الله وقولوا قولا سديدا

20:53.text: He is the One who made the earth habitable for you, and paved in it roads for you. And He sends down from the sky water with which we produce many different kinds of plants.
20:53.arabic: الذي جعل لكم الأرض مهدا وسلك لكم فيها سبلا وأنزل من السماء ماء فأخرجنا به أزوجا من نبات شتى

31:19.text: "Walk humbly and lower your voice - the ugliest voice is the donkey's voice."
31:19.arabic: واقصد في مشيك واغضض من صوتك إن أنكر الأصوت لصوت الحمير

41:33.text: Who can utter better words than one who invites to GOD, works righteousness, and says, "I am one of the submitters"?
41:33.arabic: ومن أحسن قولا ممن دعا إلى الله وعمل صلحا وقال إنني من المسلمين

16:125.text: You shall invite to the path of your Lord with wisdom and kind enlightenment, and debate with them in the best possible manner. Your Lord knows best who has strayed from His path, and He knows best who are the guided ones.
16:125.arabic: ادع إلى سبيل ربك بالحكمة والموعظة الحسنة وجدلهم بالتي هي أحسن إن ربك هو أعلم بمن ضل عن سبيله وهو أعلم بالمهتدين

question: How does the Quran condemn lying and false witness?
answer:
The Quran's *condemnation of lying and false-witness* is *systematic and severe*. The Quran *categorically prohibits* (a) *lying-against-God* (the *worst-form*), (b) *false-witness in judicial-proceedings*, (c) *bearing-falsehood as social-practice*, and (d) *consuming-the-property-of-others-through-false-testimony*. The *Quranic-architecture* — *truth-as-divine-attribute, the truthful-as-saved, the liars-as-damned* — places *truthfulness* and *its opposite* at *the highest-theological-stakes*.

**1. The 'do not bear false witness' verse.**

[25:72]: "They do not bear false witness. When they encounter vain talk, they ignore it." — *wa-lladhīna lā yashhadūna z-zūr*.

The verse — *describing the `ibād ar-Raḥmān* (*servants of the Most-Merciful*) — names *one of their defining-characteristics*: *they do not bear false-witness; they ignore vain-talk*. *Zūr* (root ز-و-ر) = *false, fabricated, perjurious*. *Lā yashhadūna z-zūr* = *they do not give false-testimony*.

The *theological-implication*: *the muḥsin / muttaqī* is *characterized by absolute-truthfulness in testimony*. *False-witness is incompatible with the believer-character*.

**2. The 'shun false statements' command.**

[22:30]: "...You shall avoid the abomination of idol worship, and avoid bearing false witness." — *fa-jtanibū r-rijsa mina l-awthāni wa-jtanibū qawla z-zūr*.

The verse establishes *the parallel*: *shun idol-worship* and *shun false-statements*. The *two prohibitions* are *paired in the same imperative*. The *theological-implication*: *false-statement is a form-of-idolatry* — *it elevates falsehood to the position of truth*; *it sets up a false-image of reality in the place of the real-truth*.

**3. The 'judicial-witness' protocols.**

[2:283]: "...You shall record what is being witnessed... You should not refrain from listing the debts, no matter how small, until the time of maturity, for this is more equitable in the sight of GOD, ensures better witnessing, and eliminates any doubts you may have. Business transactions that you execute on the spot need not be recorded, but have them witnessed..."

[5:8]: "O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses. Do not be provoked by your conflicts with some people into committing injustice. You shall be absolutely equitable, for it is more righteous. You shall observe GOD. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do."

[4:135]: "O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses, even against yourselves, or your parents, or your relatives. Whether the accused is rich or poor, GOD takes care of both. Therefore, do not be biased by your personal wishes. If you deviate or disregard (this commandment), then GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do."

The verses establish *the absolute-equity standard for testimony*:

(a) *Be absolutely equitable* even *when you serve as witnesses*.

(b) *Do not be provoked by conflicts into injustice*.

(c) *Be equitable even against yourselves, parents, relatives*.

(d) *Do not be biased by rich-vs-poor distinctions*.

(e) *Do not be biased by personal-wishes*.

The *Quranic-judicial-witness standard* is *categorically-rigorous*: *truth-witnessing is required even against one's own family or one's own self*. *No relationship-loyalty justifies false-witness*.

**4. The 'who-can-lie-more-than-the-one-who-lies-against-God' verses.**

[39:32]: "Who is more evil than one who attributes lies to GOD, while disbelieving in the truth that has come to him? Is Hell not a just retribution for the disbelievers?" — *fa-man aẓlamu mim-man kadhaba `alā llāhi wa-kadhdhaba bi-ṣ-ṣidqi idh jā'ah*.

[39:33]: "As for those who promote the truth, and confirm it, they are the righteous." — *wa-lladhī jā'a bi-ṣ-ṣidqi wa-ṣaddaqa bihī ulā'ika humu l-muttaqūn*.

The verse-pair establishes *the inverse-pair*: *the worst-evil* is *lying against God*; *the highest-virtue* is *bringing the truth and confirming the truth*. *Lying against God* and *bringing the truth* are *the two-pole categorical-evaluations*; *no other-evil exceeds lying-against-God*; *no other-virtue exceeds bringing-the-truth*.

**5. The 'fabricating lies about God' verse.**

[16:105]: "The only ones who fabricate false doctrines are those who do not believe in GOD's revelations; these are the real liars." — *innamā yaftarī l-kadhiba lladhīna lā yu'minūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-ulā'ika humu l-kādhibūn*.

The verse identifies *fabrication-of-religious-doctrine* as *the work of unbelievers*; *they are the real liars*. The *theological-implication*: *false religious-doctrine* is *the worst-form of lying*; *its propagators are categorized as "the real liars"* — *the title-holders of falsehood*.

**6. The 'one-who-takes-an-oath-against-Muslims' verse.**

[5:106]–[5:108]: "O you who believe, witnessing a will when one of you is dying shall be done by two equitable people from among you. If you are traveling, then two others may do the witnessing. After observing the Contact Prayer (Salat), let the witnesses swear by GOD, to alleviate your doubts: 'We will not use this to attain personal gains, even if the testator is related to us. Nor will we conceal GOD's testimony. Otherwise, we would be sinners.' If the witnesses are found to be guilty of bias, then two others shall be asked to take their places. Two persons who were victimized by the first witnesses shall swear by GOD: 'Our testimony is more truthful than theirs; we will not be biased. Otherwise, we would be transgressors.' This is more apt to encourage an honest testimony on their part, fearing that their oath may be disregarded like those before them. They shall observe GOD and listen. GOD does not guide the wicked."

The cluster — *the will-and-witness procedure* — establishes *the detailed-protocol for testimony-in-the-deathbed-will-context*. The *theological-architecture*: *two equitable witnesses*, *oath-by-God after prayer*, *no personal-gain*, *no concealment-of-God's-testimony*, *replacement-with-two-others if bias is found*, *the replaced-witnesses swear that their testimony is more-truthful*.

The *Quranic-procedure* is *theologically-rigorous*: *the testimony is sworn-by-God*; *the witnesses face-eschatological-accountability for false-witness*; *the protocol includes a check against bias*.

**7. The 'liars are uninterested in salvation' verse.**

[39:32]: "Who is more evil than one who attributes lies to GOD..."

[70:33]: "And their testimonies are valid."

The verse-pair (from different surahs but theologically-connected) describes *the integrity-of-testimony* as *part of the believer-character*. *Their testimonies are valid (qā'imūn)* — *their testimonies stand firm*. The believer's-testimony is *reliable*; *the unbeliever's-testimony is unreliable*.

**8. The 'do not consume what is not yours by false-testimony' verse.**

[2:188]: "You shall not take each others' money illicitly, nor shall you bribe the officials to deprive others of some of their rights illicitly, while you know."

The verse establishes *the consumption-by-false-testimony prohibition*: *do not take others' money illicitly*; *do not bribe officials to deprive others of their rights*; *while you know*. The *closing-clause* — *while you know* — establishes *the conscious-knowing dimension*: *the false-testifier knows they are testifying-falsely*; *the knowing makes the act categorically-condemnable*.

**9. The 'follow what you know' command.**

[17:36]: "You shall not accept any information, unless you verify it for yourself. I have given you the hearing, the eyesight, and the brain, and you are responsible for using them." — *wa-lā taqfu mā laysa laka bihī `ilm*.

The verse — *the verification-mandate* — *categorically prohibits* *acting-on or repeating un-verified information*. *False-witness* is *one form* of *acting-on un-verified information*. The *Quranic-discipline*: *only testify to what you have directly-witnessed*; *do not repeat what you have not verified*.

**10. The 'speak the correct word' command.**

[33:70]: "O you who believe, reverence GOD and utter only the correct utterances." — *qawlan sadīdā*.

The verse establishes *qawl sadīd* (well-aimed / straight / correct speech) as *the standard*. *Speech must aim-at-truth*; *deviation-from-truth is prohibited*.

**11. The cultivation of truth-discipline.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-methods* for *truth-discipline*:

(a) *Recognition of false-testimony as parallel to idolatry* — *the parallel-prohibition at [22:30]*.

(b) *Recognition of lying-against-God as the worst-evil* — *no other-evil exceeds it* ([39:32]).

(c) *Recognition of bringing-the-truth as the highest-virtue* — *no other-virtue exceeds it* ([39:33]).

(d) *Absolute-equity-as-witness, even against self / parents / relatives* — [4:135], [5:8].

(e) *Verification-before-accepting-information* — [17:36].

(f) *Well-aimed truth-aiming-speech* — [33:70].

(g) *Awareness of the recording-angels* — [50:18]: *not an utterance does he utter without an alert witness*.

(h) *Oath-by-God-discipline* — *the post-prayer oath at [5:106]–[5:108]*.

(i) *Awareness of God's-knowledge of all internal-states* — [16:23]: *He knows what you conceal and declare*.

**12. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *truth-discipline* is *one of the foundational-virtues* of *authentic-submission*. The Submitter's *daily-discipline*: *truthful-speech in all-contexts*; *no false-witness even against opponents*; *no lying-against-God even by repeating-questionable-religious-doctrines without verification*; *no false-business-claims*; *no false-political-claims*.

The Khalifa-translation consistently renders *kadhib* (lying) and *zūr* (false-testimony) with *categorical-clarity*; *fabrication-of-religious-doctrine* is identified as *the worst-form of lying* — *consistent with the Khalifa-tradition's rejection of hadith-fabrications-attributed-to-the-Prophet*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *condemnation of lying and false-witness* is *systematic and severe*. *The servants of the Most-Merciful do not bear false-witness* ([25:72]). *Shun idol-worship and shun false-statements* — *the parallel-prohibition* ([22:30]). *The judicial-witness standard*: *be absolutely equitable, even against yourselves, parents, relatives; do not be biased by rich-vs-poor; do not be biased by personal-wishes* ([4:135], [5:8]). *Who-is-more-evil than one who attributes lies to God?* — *no other-evil exceeds lying-against-God* ([39:32]). *Who-can-bring-the-truth is the highest-virtue* — *no other-virtue exceeds bringing-the-truth* ([39:33]). *Fabrication-of-religious-doctrine is the work of unbelievers* — *they are the real liars* ([16:105]). *The will-and-witness procedure* at [5:106]–[5:108] — *detailed-protocol for testimony-in-the-deathbed-will-context*. *Do not take others' money illicitly through false-testimony, while you know* ([2:188]). *Verification-before-accepting-information* ([17:36]). *Qawl sadīd — well-aimed, straight, correct speech* ([33:70]). The *cultivation-methods*: *recognition-of-false-testimony-as-idolatry-parallel, recognition-of-lying-against-God-as-worst-evil, recognition-of-bringing-the-truth-as-highest-virtue, absolute-equity-as-witness, verification-before-accepting-information, well-aimed-truth-speech, awareness-of-recording-angels, oath-by-God-discipline, awareness-of-God's-knowledge*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *truth-discipline* as *foundational-virtue of authentic-submission* — *fabrication-of-religious-doctrine is identified as the worst-form of lying, consistent with the Khalifa-tradition's rejection of hadith-fabrications-attributed-to-the-Prophet*.

25:72.text: They do not bear false witness. When they encounter vain talk, they ignore it.
25:72.arabic: والذين لا يشهدون الزور وإذا مروا باللغو مروا كراما

22:30.text: Those who reverence the rites decreed by GOD have deserved a good reward at their Lord. All livestock is made lawful for your food, except for those specifically prohibited for you. You shall avoid the abomination of idol worship, and avoid bearing false witness.
22:30.arabic: ذلك ومن يعظم حرمت الله فهو خير له عند ربه وأحلت لكم الأنعم إلا ما يتلى عليكم فاجتنبوا الرجس من الأوثن واجتنبوا قول الزور

2:283.text: If you are traveling, and no scribe is available, a bond shall be posted to guarantee repayment. If one is trusted in this manner, he shall return the bond when due, and he shall observe GOD his Lord. Do not withhold any testimony by concealing what you had witnessed. Anyone who withholds a testimony is sinful at heart. GOD is fully aware of everything you do.
2:283.arabic: ۞وإن كنتم على سفر ولم تجدوا كاتبا فرهن مقبوضة فإن أمن بعضكم بعضا فليؤد الذي اؤتمن أمنته وليتق الله ربه ولا تكتموا الشهدة ومن يكتمها فإنه ءاثم قلبه والله بما تعملون عليم

5:8.text: O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses. Do not be provoked by your conflicts with some people into committing injustice. You shall be absolutely equitable, for it is more righteous. You shall observe GOD. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do.
5:8.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا كونوا قوـمين لله شهداء بالقسط ولا يجرمنكم شنـان قوم على ألا تعدلوا اعدلوا هو أقرب للتقوى واتقوا الله إن الله خبير بما تعملون

65:2.text: Once the interim is fulfilled, you may reconcile with them equitably, or go through with the separation equitably. You shall have two equitable witnesses witness the divorce before GOD. This is to enlighten those who believe in GOD and the Last Day. Anyone who reverences GOD, He will create an exit for him.
65:2.arabic: فإذا بلغن أجلهن فأمسكوهن بمعروف أو فارقوهن بمعروف وأشهدوا ذوي عدل منكم وأقيموا الشهدة لله ذلكم يوعظ به من كان يؤمن بالله واليوم الأخر ومن يتق الله يجعل له مخرجا

4:135.text: O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses, even against yourselves, or your parents, or your relatives. Whether the accused is rich or poor, GOD takes care of both. Therefore, do not be biased by your personal wishes. If you deviate or disregard (this commandment), then GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do.
4:135.arabic: ۞يـأيها الذين ءامنوا كونوا قوـمين بالقسط شهداء لله ولو على أنفسكم أو الولدين والأقربين إن يكن غنيا أو فقيرا فالله أولى بهما فلا تتبعوا الهوى أن تعدلوا وإن تلوا أو تعرضوا فإن الله كان بما تعملون خبيرا

4:6.text: You shall test the orphans when they reach puberty. As soon as you find them mature enough, give them their property. Do not consume it extravagantly in a hurry, before they grow up. The rich guardian shall not charge any wage, but the poor guardian may charge equitably. When you give them their properties, you shall have witnesses. GOD suffices as Reckoner.
4:6.arabic: وابتلوا اليتمى حتى إذا بلغوا النكاح فإن ءانستم منهم رشدا فادفعوا إليهم أمولهم ولا تأكلوها إسرافا وبدارا أن يكبروا ومن كان غنيا فليستعفف ومن كان فقيرا فليأكل بالمعروف فإذا دفعتم إليهم أمولهم فأشهدوا عليهم وكفى بالله حسيبا

17:36.text: You shall not accept any information, unless you verify it for yourself. I have given you the hearing, the eyesight, and the brain, and you are responsible for using them.
17:36.arabic: ولا تقف ما ليس لك به علم إن السمع والبصر والفؤاد كل أولـئك كان عنه مسـولا

33:70.text: O you who believe, reverence GOD and utter only the correct utterances.
33:70.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا اتقوا الله وقولوا قولا سديدا

16:105.text: The only ones who fabricate false doctrines are those who do not believe in GOD's revelations; they are the real liars.
16:105.arabic: إنما يفتري الكذب الذين لا يؤمنون بـايت الله وأولـئك هم الكذبون

39:32.text: Who is more evil than one who attributes lies to GOD, while disbelieving in the truth that has come to him? Is Hell not a just requital for the disbelievers? Quran: Absolute Truth
39:32.arabic: ۞فمن أظلم ممن كذب على الله وكذب بالصدق إذ جاءه أليس في جهنم مثوى للكفرين

39:33.text: As for those who promote the truth, and believe therein, they are the righteous.
39:33.arabic: والذي جاء بالصدق وصدق به أولـئك هم المتقون

5:106.text: O you who believe, witnessing a will when one of you is dying shall be done by two equitable people among you. If you are traveling, then two others may do the witnessing. After observing the Contact Prayer (Salat), let the witnesses swear by GOD, to alleviate your doubts: "We will not use this to attain personal gains, even if the testator is related to us. Nor will we conceal GOD's testimony. Otherwise, we would be sinners."
5:106.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا شهدة بينكم إذا حضر أحدكم الموت حين الوصية اثنان ذوا عدل منكم أو ءاخران من غيركم إن أنتم ضربتم في الأرض فأصبتكم مصيبة الموت تحبسونهما من بعد الصلوة فيقسمان بالله إن ارتبتم لا نشتري به ثمنا ولو كان ذا قربى ولا نكتم شهدة الله إنا إذا لمن الأثمين

5:107.text: If the witnesses are found to be guilty of bias, then two others shall be asked to take their places. Choose two persons who were victimized by the first witnesses, and let them swear by GOD: "Our testimony is more truthful than theirs; we will not be biased. Otherwise, we will be transgressors."
5:107.arabic: فإن عثر على أنهما استحقا إثما فـاخران يقومان مقامهما من الذين استحق عليهم الأولين فيقسمان بالله لشهدتنا أحق من شهدتهما وما اعتدينا إنا إذا لمن الظـلمين

5:108.text: This is more apt to encourage an honest testimony on their part, fearing that their oath may be disregarded like that of the previous witnesses. You shall observe GOD and listen. GOD does not guide the wicked.
5:108.arabic: ذلك أدنى أن يأتوا بالشهدة على وجهها أو يخافوا أن ترد أيمن بعد أيمنهم واتقوا الله واسمعوا والله لا يهدي القوم الفسقين

question: What are the marks of the hypocrite (munafiq) in the Quran?
answer:
The Quran's *munāfiqūn* (*hypocrites*) — *those who profess belief externally but conceal disbelief internally* — receive *extensive treatment* across the scripture, with *a dedicated chapter* (*Surah Al-Munāfiqūn*, 63) and *frequent treatment* in *Surahs Al-Baqarah, An-Nisā', At-Tawbah, and Al-Aḥzāb*. The Quranic-marks of the hypocrite are *detailed and behavioral* — *making the type identifiable through observable conduct*.

**1. The 'two-faces' core verses.**

[2:8]: "Then there are those who say, 'We believe in GOD and the Last Day,' when they are not really believers."

[2:9]: "In trying to deceive GOD and those who believe, they only deceive themselves without perceiving."

[2:10]: "In their minds there is a disease. Consequently, GOD augments their disease. They have incurred a painful retribution for their lying."

The opening verses of *Surah Al-Baqarah's* hypocrite-section establish *the foundational-claim*: *they verbally-profess belief while not really-believing*. *They think they deceive God and the believers, but they deceive only themselves*. *Their hearts have a disease (maraḍ); God augments the disease*.

The *theological-frame* — *the disease in their hearts* — is *one of the most-frequent Quranic-descriptions of the hypocrite-condition*: *qulūbihim maraḍ* — *in their hearts is a sickness*. The hypocrisy is *not just behavioral*; it is *a spiritual-pathology*.

**2. The 'two-tongued' behavior.**

[2:14]: "When they meet the believers, they say, 'We believe,' but when alone with their devils, they say, 'We are with you; we were only mocking.'"

The verse establishes *the two-tongued behavior*: *one-tongue in believer-company* (*we believe*); *another-tongue in disbeliever-company* (*we are with you; we were only mocking*). The *defining-behavior* of *the hypocrite* is *the social-context-dependent speech*.

**3. The 'sweet-talk hides corruption' verse.**

[2:204]: "Among the people, one may impress you with his utterances concerning this life, and may even call upon GOD to witness the thoughts in his heart, while he is a most ardent opponent."

[2:205]: "As soon as he leaves, he roams the earth corruptingly, destroying properties and lives. GOD does not love corruption."

The verses establish *the public-private split*: *eloquent in public*; *corrupt in private*. *He even calls upon God to witness the thoughts in his heart* — *the deepest-deception*; *while he is a most-ardent opponent*. *As soon as he leaves, he roams the earth corruptingly*.

**4. The 'lazy in prayer' marks.**

[4:142]: "The hypocrites think that they are deceiving GOD, but He is the One who leads them on. When they get up for the Contact Prayer (Salat), they get up lazily. That is because they only show off in front of the people, and rarely do they think of GOD."

[4:143]: "Wavering in between, neither belonging to these, nor those. Whoever GOD sends astray, you will never find a way to guide him."

The verses establish *two prayer-marks of the hypocrite*: (a) *lazy in standing for the prayer*; (b) *showing-off in front of people, rarely thinking of God*. The third mark: *wavering between believer-and-disbeliever camps* — *not committed to either*.

The *theological-implication*: *the hypocrite's prayer is for-show, not for-God*; *the inner-discipline is absent*; *the external-form is performed grudgingly*.

**5. The 'enjoining evil, forbidding good' inversion.**

[9:67]: "The hypocrite men and the hypocrite women belong with each other - they advocate evil and prohibit righteousness, and they are stingy. They forgot GOD, so He forgot them. Indeed, the hypocrites are truly wicked."

The verse — *describing the hypocrite-collective* — identifies *four marks*:

(a) *Advocate evil* — *encouraging the wrong*.

(b) *Prohibit righteousness* — *discouraging the right*.

(c) *Stingy* — *withholding what they should give*.

(d) *Forgot God* — *and so God forgot them*.

The *theological-inversion*: *the believer enjoins-the-good and forbids-the-evil* ([3:104], [3:110], [9:71]); *the hypocrite inverts this* — *enjoining-evil and forbidding-good*. *The inversion is the diagnostic-marker*.

**6. The 'broken-promise' mark.**

[9:75]: "Some of them even pledged: 'If GOD showered us with His grace, we would be charitable, and would lead a righteous life.'"

[9:76]: "But when He did shower them with His provisions, they became stingy, and turned away in aversion."

[9:77]: "Consequently, He plagued them with hypocrisy in their hearts, till the day they meet Him. This is because they broke their promises to GOD, and because of their lying."

The verses establish *the broken-promise-to-God mark*: *promising charity when desperate*; *withholding when prosperous*. *God's-response*: *plaguing them with hypocrisy in their hearts until they meet Him*. *The hypocrisy is a divine-punishment* for *the broken-promise*.

The *theological-architecture*: *some-people become-hypocrites through specific-broken-promises*; *the hypocrisy is the consequence of broken-commitment*, not *just a personality-type*.

**7. The 'Surah Al-Munāfiqūn' opening.**

[63:1]: "When the hypocrites come to you they say, 'We bear witness that you are the messenger of GOD.' GOD knows that you are His messenger, and GOD bears witness that the hypocrites are liars."

[63:2]: "Under the guise of their (apparent) faith, they repel the people from the path of GOD. Miserable indeed is what they do."

[63:3]: "This is because they believed, then disbelieved. Hence, their minds are blocked; they do not understand."

[63:4]: "When you see them, you may be impressed by their looks. And when they speak, you may listen to their eloquence. They are like standing logs. They think that every call is intended against them. These are the real enemies; beware of them. GOD condemns them; they have deviated."

The opening of *Surah Al-Munāfiqūn* establishes *additional marks*:

(a) *Verbally-witness the Prophet's-status while internally-denying* — *the foundational hypocrisy*.

(b) *Repel people from the path of God under the guise of faith* — *the actively-harmful behavior*.

(c) *Believed-then-disbelieved* — *the trajectory of falling-into-hypocrisy*.

(d) *Impressive appearance and eloquent speech* — *the deceptive-presentation*.

(e) *Standing logs (deceptively-imposing but empty)* — *the vivid-imagery*.

(f) *Paranoid — think every call is against them* — *the persecution-anxiety*.

(g) *Real enemies — beware of them* — *the divine-warning*.

**8. The 'lowest depths of hell' verse.**

[4:145]: "The hypocrites will be committed to the lowest pit of Hell, and you will find no one to help them." — *inna l-munāfiqīna fī d-darki l-asfali mina n-nār*.

The verse establishes *the eschatological-destination of the hypocrites*: *the lowest-pit of Hell*. *No one will help them*. *The hypocrites occupy a worse-place in hell than the open-disbelievers* — *the deception-of-faith is theologically-worse than the open-rejection-of-faith*.

The *theological-rationale*: *the open-disbeliever's-rejection is honest*; *the hypocrite's-rejection is dishonest*; *the dishonest-rejection compounds the rejection with deception*. *The compound-sin receives the worst-place*.

**9. The 'historical context' of the Munāfiqūn-verses.**

The *historical-context* of *the Quranic-munāfiqūn verses* is *Medina-period*: *after the Hijrah, when the Muslim-community had political-power in Medina, a faction of opportunists outwardly-professed faith while internally-conspiring against the Prophet's-leadership*. The *paradigmatic-hypocrite* in the *biographical-tradition* is *Abdullah ibn Ubayy*, *the leader of the Medinan-hypocrites*.

The *verses' historical-context* — *political-opportunism in a community-in-power* — is *theologically-significant*: *the hypocrite-type emerges where there is something to gain by professing belief while not actually believing*. *In persecution-contexts*, *open-disbelief carries less-cost*; *the hypocrisy-emergence is conditional on the believer-community having political-or-social-power*.

**10. The contemporary-application.**

The *Quranic-marks of the hypocrite* apply *across times*:

(a) *Verbally-professing belief while internally-disbelieving* — *applies anywhere there is a community-of-believers with social-or-political-power*.

(b) *Two-tongued behavior in different-social-contexts* — *applies to anyone who tailors their professed-belief to their audience*.

(c) *Lazy in prayer; show-off in religious-performance* — *applies in any religious-community*.

(d) *Enjoining-evil and forbidding-righteousness* — *the moral-inversion*.

(e) *Breaking promises to God* — *especially financial-promises*.

(f) *Repelling people from the path of God under the guise of faith* — *applies to any-influencer who externally-promotes religion while actually-undermining it*.

(g) *Deceptive-presentation, eloquent-empty-speech, persecution-anxiety* — *applies in any social-context with religious-leadership*.

**11. The 'recognition not invocation' principle.**

The Quran *names the marks* — *not to invite the believer to publicly-accuse particular-individuals* of *hypocrisy* (that *judgment is for God*) — but *to enable the believer to (a) self-examine for the marks in oneself, and (b) discern the deceptive-behavior in oneself and others*. *The discernment is the purpose*; *the accusation is not the believer's prerogative*.

The *Quranic-discipline*: *recognize the marks in oneself first*; *guard against the marks in one's own conduct*; *do not be deceived by deceptive-behavior in others, but also do not publicly-accuse without certainty*.

**12. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *the hypocrite-marks* serve as *the diagnostic-mirror for self-examination*. The Submitter's *self-examination*: *do I tailor my speech to my audience? do I stand for prayer lazily? do I rarely think of God? do I show off in religious-performance? do I make-and-break promises to God? do I enjoin-good and forbid-evil consistently?*

The Khalifa-translation of [63:4]'s *standing logs* preserves *the vivid-imagery*: *deceptively-imposing but empty*. The *modern-application*: *the religious-performer who is internally-empty* is *the modern-hypocrite-type*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *munāfiqūn* (hypocrites) — *those who profess belief externally but conceal disbelief internally* — receive *extensive treatment* across the scripture. *The disease in their hearts* ([2:10]); *the two-tongued behavior in different social-contexts* ([2:14]); *eloquent-sweet-public-speech but corrupt-private-conduct* ([2:204]–[2:205]). *Lazy in prayer; show-off in front of people, rarely thinking of God* ([4:142]); *wavering between believer-and-disbeliever camps* ([4:143]). *Advocate evil, prohibit righteousness, stingy, forgot God* ([9:67]). *Broken-promise mark*: *promising charity when desperate, withholding when prosperous* ([9:75]–[9:77]); *God plagues them with hypocrisy as a punishment for broken-commitment*. *Surah Al-Munāfiqūn* ([63:1]–[63:4]): *verbal-witness while internal-denial, repelling people under guise of faith, believed-then-disbelieved, impressive appearance and eloquent speech, standing logs (deceptively-imposing but empty), paranoid, real enemies*. *The lowest-pit of Hell* ([4:145]) — *the hypocrites occupy a worse-place in hell than the open-disbelievers*. The *historical-context* — *Medina-period political-opportunism* (Abdullah ibn Ubayy paradigm). The *contemporary-application*: *the marks apply across times wherever there is a community-of-believers with social-power*. The *Quranic-purpose*: *self-examination and discernment*, not *public-accusation*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *the hypocrite-marks* as *diagnostic-mirror for self-examination*.

2:8.text: Then there are those who say, "We believe in GOD and the Last Day," while they are not believers.
2:8.arabic: ومن الناس من يقول ءامنا بالله وباليوم الأخر وما هم بمؤمنين

2:9.text: In trying to deceive GOD and those who believe, they only deceive themselves without perceiving.
2:9.arabic: يخدعون الله والذين ءامنوا وما يخدعون إلا أنفسهم وما يشعرون

2:10.text: In their minds there is a disease. Consequently, GOD augments their disease. They have incurred a painful retribution for their lying.
2:10.arabic: في قلوبهم مرض فزادهم الله مرضا ولهم عذاب أليم بما كانوا يكذبون

2:11.text: When they are told, "Do not commit evil," they say, "But we are righteous!"
2:11.arabic: وإذا قيل لهم لا تفسدوا في الأرض قالوا إنما نحن مصلحون

2:14.text: When they meet the believers, they say, "We believe," but when alone with their devils, they say, "We are with you; we were only mocking."
2:14.arabic: وإذا لقوا الذين ءامنوا قالوا ءامنا وإذا خلوا إلى شيطينهم قالوا إنا معكم إنما نحن مستهزءون

2:204.text: Among the people, one may impress you with his utterances concerning this life, and may even call upon GOD to witness his innermost thoughts, while he is a most ardent opponent.
2:204.arabic: ومن الناس من يعجبك قوله في الحيوة الدنيا ويشهد الله على ما في قلبه وهو ألد الخصام

2:205.text: As soon as he leaves, he roams the earth corruptingly, destroying properties and lives. GOD does not love corruption.
2:205.arabic: وإذا تولى سعى في الأرض ليفسد فيها ويهلك الحرث والنسل والله لا يحب الفساد

4:142.text: The hypocrites think that they are deceiving GOD, but He is the One who leads them on. When they get up for the Contact Prayer (Salat), they get up lazily. That is because they only show off in front of the people, and rarely do they think of GOD.
4:142.arabic: إن المنفقين يخدعون الله وهو خدعهم وإذا قاموا إلى الصلوة قاموا كسالى يراءون الناس ولا يذكرون الله إلا قليلا

4:143.text: They waver in between, neither belonging to this group, nor that group. Whomever GOD sends astray, you will never find a way to guide him.
4:143.arabic: مذبذبين بين ذلك لا إلى هـؤلاء ولا إلى هـؤلاء ومن يضلل الله فلن تجد له سبيلا

4:145.text: The hypocrites will be committed to the lowest pit of Hell, and you will find no one to help them.
4:145.arabic: إن المنفقين في الدرك الأسفل من النار ولن تجد لهم نصيرا

9:67.text: The hypocrite men and the hypocrite women belong with each other - they advocate evil and prohibit righteousness, and they are stingy. They forgot GOD, so He forgot them. The hypocrites are truly wicked.
9:67.arabic: المنفقون والمنفقت بعضهم من بعض يأمرون بالمنكر وينهون عن المعروف ويقبضون أيديهم نسوا الله فنسيهم إن المنفقين هم الفسقون

9:75.text: Some of them even pledged: "If GOD showered us with His grace, we would be charitable, and would lead a righteous life."
9:75.arabic: ۞ومنهم من عهد الله لئن ءاتىنا من فضله لنصدقن ولنكونن من الصـلحين

9:77.text: Consequently, He plagued them with hypocrisy in their hearts, till the day they meet Him. This is because they broke their promises to GOD, and because of their lying.
9:77.arabic: فأعقبهم نفاقا في قلوبهم إلى يوم يلقونه بما أخلفوا الله ما وعدوه وبما كانوا يكذبون

63:1.text: When the hypocrites come to you they say, "We bear witness that you are the messenger of GOD." GOD knows that you are His messenger, and GOD bears witness that the hypocrites are liars.
63:1.arabic: إذا جاءك المنفقون قالوا نشهد إنك لرسول الله والله يعلم إنك لرسوله والله يشهد إن المنفقين لكذبون

63:2.text: Under the guise of their apparent faith, they repel the people from the path of GOD. Miserable indeed is what they do.
63:2.arabic: اتخذوا أيمنهم جنة فصدوا عن سبيل الله إنهم ساء ما كانوا يعملون

63:3.text: This is because they believed, then disbelieved. Hence, their minds are blocked; they do not understand.
63:3.arabic: ذلك بأنهم ءامنوا ثم كفروا فطبع على قلوبهم فهم لا يفقهون

63:4.text: When you see them, you may be impressed by their looks. And when they speak, you may listen to their eloquence. They are like standing logs. They think that every call is intended against them. These are the real enemies; beware of them. GOD condemns them; they have deviated.
63:4.arabic: ۞وإذا رأيتهم تعجبك أجسامهم وإن يقولوا تسمع لقولهم كأنهم خشب مسندة يحسبون كل صيحة عليهم هم العدو فاحذرهم قتلهم الله أنى يؤفكون

question: What is the process of true repentance (tawbah nasuh) in the Quran?
answer:
The Quran's *tawbah nasūḥ* — *sincere / pure repentance* — is *the foundational pathway* for *the believer's-return to God* after *sin*. The phrase *tawbatan naṣūḥā* appears at [66:8] and *names the highest-quality of repentance*. The Quranic-architecture of *repentance* includes *multiple-components*: *recognition-of-the-sin, immediate-cessation, sincere-regret, prayer-for-forgiveness, restitution-where-possible, commitment-not-to-return*. The *Quranic-promise* is *categorical*: *God forgives all sins* ([39:53]) — *except shirk-persisted-to-death*.

**1. The foundational tawbah-nasūḥ verse.**

[66:8]: "O you who believe, you shall repent to GOD a firm repentance. Your Lord will then remit your sins and admit you into gardens with flowing streams. On that day, GOD will not disappoint the prophet and those who believed with him..." — *yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū tūbū ilā llāhi tawbatan naṣūḥā*.

The verse establishes *the categorical-command for sincere-repentance*. *Tawbatan naṣūḥā* — *a sincere / firm / pure / honest repentance*. The root ن-ص-ح (*to be sincere, give-honest-advice*) implies *the repentance is honest-with-oneself* — *not pro-forma, not hedge-and-half-measure, not waiting-to-return-to-the-sin*.

The *promise*: *your Lord will remit your sins and admit you into gardens with flowing streams*. The *sincere-repentance* attracts *the eschatological-reward of paradise*.

**2. The components of tawbah-nasūḥ.**

The classical-tradition identifies *five components* of *sincere-repentance* — *all grounded in Quranic-verses*:

(a) *Recognition of the sin* — *acknowledging it as a sin, not as a self-justified-action*.

(b) *Immediate cessation* — *stopping the sin immediately, not waiting*.

(c) *Sincere regret* — *feeling genuine-remorse, not mere-regret-of-consequences*.

(d) *Resolution not to return* — *committing to not-repeat the sin*.

(e) *Restitution where possible* — *if the sin involved a wrong to a person, restoring what was taken; if it involved an unfulfilled-duty to God, fulfilling it*.

**3. The 'immediate-cessation' verse.**

[3:135]: "If they fall in sin or wrong their souls, they remember GOD and ask forgiveness for their sins - and who forgives the sins except GOD - and they do not persist in sins, knowingly."

[3:136]: "Their recompense is forgiveness from their Lord, and gardens with flowing streams; they abide therein forever. What a blessed reward for the workers!"

The verses establish *the immediate-cessation principle*: *upon committing a sin, they remember God and ask forgiveness*; *they do not persist in the sin knowingly*. *Persistence-knowingly* is *the disqualifier of repentance*; *immediate-cessation is the qualifier*.

The *theological-implication*: *committing a sin is human*; *the believer-response is immediate-recognition and turning-back*; *the unbeliever-response is persistence-and-rationalization*.

**4. The 'immediate-tawbah' acceptance verse.**

[4:17]: "Repentance is acceptable by GOD from those who fall in sin out of ignorance, then repent immediately thereafter. GOD redeems them. GOD is Omniscient, Most Wise."

[4:18]: "Not acceptable is the repentance of those who commit sins until death comes to them, then say, 'Now I repent.' Nor is it acceptable from those who die as disbelievers; for these we have prepared a painful retribution."

The verse-pair establishes *the temporal-bounds of acceptable-repentance*: *immediate* after the sin is *acceptable*; *death-bed when-the-end-comes is unacceptable*. The *immediacy-principle* is *categorical*: *the repentance must precede the certainty of death*; *the repentance-of-the-cornered is not the repentance-of-the-free-agent*.

The *theological-rationale*: *repentance is a free-choice-of-the-living-soul*; *death-bed repentance is forced-by-circumstance*; *only the free-choice-repentance has spiritual-value*.

**5. The 'God forgives all sins' verse.**

[39:53]: "Proclaim: 'O My servants who exceeded the limits, never despair of GOD's mercy. For GOD forgives all sins. He is the Forgiver, Most Merciful.'" — *innallāha yaghfiru dh-dhunūba jamī`ā*.

[39:54]: "You shall obey your Lord, and submit to Him totally, before the retribution overtakes you; then you cannot be helped."

The verses establish *the categorical-promise* of *divine-forgiveness*: *God forgives all sins*. *Never despair of God's mercy*. *Even those who exceeded the limits* — *the limits-exceeders* — *are addressed* by *the divine-mercy*. The *only-qualifier* — *exceptional shirk-persisted-to-death* — is *clarified at [4:48], [4:116]*: *God does not forgive setting up partners besides Him; He forgives anything else for whomever He wills*.

The *Quranic-architecture* of *forgiveness*: *all sins are forgivable through sincere-repentance*; *only shirk-persisted-to-death is the absolute-disqualifier*; *during life, even shirk-can-be-repented*.

**6. The 'God loves those who repent' verse.**

[2:222]: "...GOD loves those who repent, and He loves those who are clean."

[9:104]: "Do they not realize that GOD accepts the repentance of His worshipers, and takes the charities, and that GOD is the Redeemer, Most Merciful?"

[42:25]: "He is the One who accepts the repentance from His servants, and remits the sins. He is fully aware of everything you do."

The verses establish *the active-divine-acceptance* of *repentance*: *God loves those who repent*; *God accepts the repentance*; *God is the Receiver-of-Repentance (at-Tawwāb)*. The *active-receptivity of God toward the repentant-soul* is *theologically-emphasized*.

**7. The 'sins-transformed-to-merits' verse.**

[25:70]: "Exempted are those who repent, believe, and lead a righteous life. GOD transforms their sins into credits. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful." — *fa-ulā'ika yubaddilu llāhu sayyi'ātihim ḥasanāt*.

[25:71]: "Those who repent and lead a righteous life, GOD redeems them; a complete redemption."

The verses establish *the most-extraordinary divine-response* to *sincere-repentance + righteous-life*: *God transforms their sins into credits*. *Sayyi'āt → ḥasanāt* — *bad-deeds → good-deeds*. *The repentant-believer's sins are not merely-forgiven; they are transformed-into-merits*.

The *theological-significance* is *immense*: *the very-acts that previously-deducted from the soul's-account* now *add to the account*. The *spiritual-economy is generous beyond human-expectation*. *Khalifa-tradition emphasizes this verse* as *one of the most-radical promises* in the Quran.

**8. The 'forgive small-and-large-sins' implication.**

The Quranic-architecture distinguishes:

(a) *Major sins (kabā'ir)* — *the principal-sins listed in various places* (e.g., murder, adultery, theft, false-witness, parental-disobedience). *The major-sins require specific-repentance*.

(b) *Minor sins (ṣaghā'ir, lamam)* — *the small-everyday lapses*. *The minor-sins are wiped-out* by *the regular-righteous-deeds* (*prayer, charity, fasting*) without *requiring specific-repentance for each*.

The *Quranic-verse* at [4:31]: *if you avoid the major-sins... we will remit your sins and admit you with an honorable admittance*. The *avoidance of major-sins* combined with *the regular-righteous-deeds* produces *the remitting of minor-sins*. *The sincere-repentance is required for major-sins*; *the continuous-righteous-life handles the minor-sins*.

**9. The 'one-time-vs-repeated' question.**

The classical-Islamic tradition has *debated* whether *sincere-repentance suffices once* or *requires repetition*. The Quranic-text suggests *repeated-repentance is acceptable*: the verses about *the limits-exceeders* at [39:53] — *do not despair* — *imply that even repeated-sin-and-repentance is welcomed* by *divine-mercy*.

The *classical-position*: *if a sincerely-repented sin is committed-again*, *a fresh-sincere-repentance is required*; *the previous-repentance does not retroactively cover the recurrence*. *Each instance requires its own repentance*. The *Quranic-mercy permits this without disqualification* — *do not despair*.

The *Quranic-discipline*: *do not let-the-fear-of-repeating prevent-the-current-repentance*; *each repentance is valid for its instance*; *the cumulative-pattern of repentance-and-effort is the believer's-pathway*.

**10. The cultivation of tawbah.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-methods* for *sincere-repentance*:

(a) *Daily-self-examination* — *recognizing sins promptly*.

(b) *Immediate-recognition without persistence-knowingly* — [3:135].

(c) *Pre-death-immediate timing* — [4:17]–[4:18].

(d) *Trust in divine-forgiveness without despair* — [39:53].

(e) *Combination with righteous-life* — *the sins-to-credits transformation requires repent + believe + lead a righteous life* ([25:70]).

(f) *Prayer for forgiveness* — *the standard prophetic-prayer formulas*.

(g) *Restitution where possible* — *if wronged a person, restore*; *if missed a duty, fulfill*.

(h) *Combination with charity* — *charity remits sins* ([2:271]: *and remits more of your sins*).

(i) *Combination with prayer* — *the regular-prayers remit minor-sins* (the standard-hadith claim, grounded in the Quranic-verse-cluster).

**11. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *sincere-repentance* is *the constant-discipline of the Submitter*. The Submitter's *daily-practice*: *upon any-sin, immediate-recognition, immediate-cessation, sincere-regret, commitment-not-to-return, restitution-where-possible, prayer-for-forgiveness*. The *promise* — *God forgives all sins* ([39:53]) — *removes the despair-barrier*.

The Khalifa-translation of [25:70] preserves the *transformation-promise*: *GOD transforms their sins into credits*. The Submitter is *invited to trust this transformation* and *to keep returning to God with sincere-repentance regardless of the sin's-severity-or-frequency*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *tawbah nasūḥ* — *sincere repentance* — is *the foundational pathway for the believer's return to God after sin*. *Repent to God a sincere repentance; your Lord will remit your sins and admit you into gardens with flowing streams* ([66:8]). *Five components*: *recognition-of-the-sin, immediate-cessation, sincere-regret, resolution-not-to-return, restitution-where-possible*. *Immediate-cessation principle*: *they do not persist in sins, knowingly* ([3:135]). *Temporal-bounds*: *immediate-after-the-sin acceptable*; *death-bed-when-the-end-comes unacceptable* ([4:17]–[4:18]). *God forgives all sins; never despair of God's mercy* ([39:53]) — *the categorical-promise*; *only shirk-persisted-to-death is the absolute-disqualifier*. *God loves those who repent; God is the Receiver-of-Repentance* ([2:222], [9:104], [42:25]). *Sins-transformed-to-merits*: *GOD transforms their sins into credits* ([25:70]) — *the most-extraordinary divine-response*. *Major-sins (kabā'ir) require specific-repentance; minor-sins (ṣaghā'ir, lamam) are wiped-out by regular-righteous-deeds* ([4:31]). The *cultivation-methods*: *daily-self-examination, immediate-recognition, pre-death-immediate timing, trust in divine-forgiveness without despair, combination-with-righteous-life, prayer-for-forgiveness, restitution-where-possible, combination-with-charity, combination-with-prayer*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *sincere-repentance* as *constant-discipline of the Submitter* — *keep returning to God with sincere-repentance regardless of the sin's-severity-or-frequency*.

66:8.text: O you who believe, you shall repent to GOD a firm repentance. Your Lord will then remit your sins and admit you into gardens with flowing streams. On that day, GOD will not disappoint the prophet and those who believed with him. Their light will radiate in front of them and to their right. They will say, "Our Lord, perfect our light for us, and forgive us; You are Omnipotent."
66:8.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا توبوا إلى الله توبة نصوحا عسى ربكم أن يكفر عنكم سيـاتكم ويدخلكم جنـت تجري من تحتها الأنهر يوم لا يخزي الله النبي والذين ءامنوا معه نورهم يسعى بين أيديهم وبأيمنهم يقولون ربنا أتمم لنا نورنا واغفر لنا إنك على كل شيء قدير

3:135.text: If they fall in sin or wrong their souls, they remember GOD and ask forgiveness for their sins - and who forgives the sins except GOD - and they do not persist in sins, knowingly.
3:135.arabic: والذين إذا فعلوا فحشة أو ظلموا أنفسهم ذكروا الله فاستغفروا لذنوبهم ومن يغفر الذنوب إلا الله ولم يصروا على ما فعلوا وهم يعلمون

3:136.text: Their recompense is forgiveness from their Lord, and gardens with flowing streams; they abide therein forever. What a blessed reward for the workers!
3:136.arabic: أولـئك جزاؤهم مغفرة من ربهم وجنـت تجري من تحتها الأنهر خلدين فيها ونعم أجر العملين

4:17.text: Repentance is acceptable by GOD from those who fall in sin out of ignorance, then repent immediately thereafter. GOD redeems them. GOD is Omniscient, Most Wise.
4:17.arabic: إنما التوبة على الله للذين يعملون السوء بجهلة ثم يتوبون من قريب فأولـئك يتوب الله عليهم وكان الله عليما حكيما

4:18.text: Not acceptable is the repentance of those who commit sins until death comes to them, then say, "Now I repent." Nor is it acceptable from those who die as disbelievers. For these, we have prepared a painful retribution.
4:18.arabic: وليست التوبة للذين يعملون السيـات حتى إذا حضر أحدهم الموت قال إني تبت الـن ولا الذين يموتون وهم كفار أولـئك أعتدنا لهم عذابا أليما

5:39.text: If one repents after committing this crime, and reforms, GOD redeems him. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
5:39.arabic: فمن تاب من بعد ظلمه وأصلح فإن الله يتوب عليه إن الله غفور رحيم

9:104.text: Do they not realize that GOD accepts the repentance of His worshipers, and takes the charities, and that GOD is the Redeemer, Most Merciful?
9:104.arabic: ألم يعلموا أن الله هو يقبل التوبة عن عباده ويأخذ الصدقت وأن الله هو التواب الرحيم

25:70.text: Exempted are those who repent, believe, and lead a righteous life. GOD transforms their sins into credits. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
25:70.arabic: إلا من تاب وءامن وعمل عملا صلحا فأولـئك يبدل الله سيـاتهم حسنت وكان الله غفورا رحيما

25:71.text: Those who repent and lead a righteous life, GOD redeems them; a complete redemption.
25:71.arabic: ومن تاب وعمل صلحا فإنه يتوب إلى الله متابا

39:53.text: Proclaim: "O My servants who exceeded the limits, never despair of GOD's mercy. For GOD forgives all sins. He is the Forgiver, Most Merciful."
39:53.arabic: ۞قل يعبادي الذين أسرفوا على أنفسهم لا تقنطوا من رحمة الله إن الله يغفر الذنوب جميعا إنه هو الغفور الرحيم

39:54.text: You shall obey your Lord, and submit to Him totally, before the retribution overtakes you; then you cannot be helped.
39:54.arabic: وأنيبوا إلى ربكم وأسلموا له من قبل أن يأتيكم العذاب ثم لا تنصرون

42:25.text: He is the One who accepts the repentance from His servants, and remits the sins. He is fully aware of everything you do.
42:25.arabic: وهو الذي يقبل التوبة عن عباده ويعفوا عن السيـات ويعلم ما تفعلون

46:15.text: We enjoined the human being to honor his parents. His mother bore him arduously, gave birth to him arduously, and took intimate care of him for thirty months. When he reaches maturity, and reaches the age of forty, he should say, "My Lord, direct me to appreciate the blessings You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do the righteous works that please You. Let my children be righteous as well. I have repented to You; I am a submitter."
46:15.arabic: ووصينا الإنسن بولديه إحسنا حملته أمه كرها ووضعته كرها وحمله وفصله ثلثون شهرا حتى إذا بلغ أشده وبلغ أربعين سنة قال رب أوزعني أن أشكر نعمتك التي أنعمت علي وعلى ولدي وأن أعمل صلحا ترضىه وأصلح لي في ذريتي إني تبت إليك وإني من المسلمين

question: Why does the Quran make forgiving others a high virtue?
answer:
The Quran's *forgiving-others* is *one of the most-frequently-emphasized character-virtues* — placed *among the defining-characteristics of paradise-bound believers* ([3:134]). The Quranic-theology of *forgiveness-of-others* is *grounded in the reciprocal-divine-mercy principle*: *forgive others as God forgives you*. The *practical-architecture* includes *(a) pardoning-when-able-to-retaliate, (b) reconciling-after-discord, (c) returning-evil-with-good, (d) forgiving-even-when-the-offender-is-wealthy*.

**1. The 'pardoners of the people' verse.**

[3:134]: "who give to charity during the good times, as well as the bad times. They are suppressors of anger, and pardoners of the people. GOD loves the charitable." — *wa-l-`āfīna `ani n-nās*.

The verse — *defining the paradise-bound muttaqīn* — names *al-`āfīna `ani n-nās* — *the pardoners-of-the-people* — as *one of the four defining-virtues*. *Pardoning-others* is *categorical paradise-criterion*.

**2. The 'pardon-and-do-not-care' Abu-Bakr verse.**

[24:22]: "Those among you who are blessed with resources and wealth shall be charitable towards their relatives, the poor, and those who have immigrated for the sake of GOD. They shall treat them with kindness and tolerance; do you not love to attain GOD's forgiveness? GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful." — *wa-l-ya`fū wa-l-yaṣfaḥū, a-lā tuḥibbūna an yaghfira llāhu lakum*.

The verse — *historically-revealed about Abu Bakr after he swore not to support a relative who had participated in slandering Aisha* — establishes *the divine-forgiveness reciprocity principle*: *pardon and disregard the offense*; *do you not love that God forgive you?*

The *theological-architecture*: *the believer's-forgiveness-of-others* and *God's-forgiveness-of-the-believer* are *reciprocally-linked*. *The believer who refuses to forgive others* is *implicitly-refusing the corresponding-divine-mercy for themselves*. *The believer who forgives others* is *enacting the same mercy* they *hope to receive from God*.

**3. The 'when angered they forgive' verse.**

[42:37]: "They avoid gross sins and vice, and when angered they forgive." — *wa-idhā mā ghaḍibū hum yaghfirūn*.

The verse pairs *avoidance-of-major-sins* with *forgiveness-when-angered*. *The two virtues* are *coupled*: *the believer avoids committing-major-sins and avoids responding-vengefully-to-others'-sins*.

The verb *ghaḍiba* (to be angered) is *temporal*: *when angered* (not *if angered*) — *acknowledging the anger as a recurring-experience*. The *believer-response* is *to forgive in the moment of anger* — *not letting the anger drive the retaliation*.

**4. The 'pardon-and-forgive — strength of character' verses.**

[42:40]: "Although the just requital for an injustice is an equivalent retribution, those who pardon and maintain righteousness are rewarded by GOD. He does not love the unjust." — *fa-man `afā wa-aṣlaḥa fa-ajruhū `alā llāh*.

[42:43]: "Resorting to patience and forgiveness reflects a true strength of character." — *wa-la-man ṣabara wa-ghafara inna dhālika la-min `azmi l-umūr*.

The verses establish:

(a) *Equivalent-retribution is the just-requital* — *the legal-baseline*.

(b) *Pardoning-and-maintaining-righteousness is rewarded by God* — *the higher-virtue*.

(c) *Patience-and-forgiveness reflect true strength-of-character* (*`azm al-umūr* — *the resolute-determinations of high-affairs*).

The *Quranic-rejection* of *the weakness-equation*: *forgiveness is not weakness*; *it is the resolute-strength of high-character*. *The weak-person retaliates*; *the strong-person forgives*. *The Quranic-inversion* of the *common-perception*.

**5. The 'do not let your-discord-prevent-your-justice' command.**

[5:8]: "O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses. Do not be provoked by your conflicts with some people into committing injustice. You shall be absolutely equitable, for it is more righteous. You shall observe GOD. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do."

The verse extends *the forgiveness-principle* to *the judicial-context*: *do not let your discord with some-people drive you to injustice against them*. *The forgiveness-and-equity-discipline applies even toward enemies in judicial-proceedings*.

**6. The 'forgive-and-tolerate' Surah Al-Aḥzāb command.**

[5:13]: "...You will continue to discover deceit in them, except for a few of them. You shall pardon them and disregard them. GOD loves those who are benevolent." — *fa-`fu `anhum wa-ṣfaḥ, inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn*.

The verse — *in the context of the Israelite-covenant-breaking* — establishes *the pardon-and-disregard discipline* in the *Prophet's-relationship with the People of the Book*. *Even when the deception is recurring*, the *believer's-response* is *pardon and disregard, not active-retribution*.

**7. The 'forgive-with-beautiful-forgiveness' command.**

[15:85]: "We did not create the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, except for a specific purpose. The end of the world will surely come, so treat them with benign neglect." — *fa-ṣfaḥi ṣ-ṣafḥa l-jamīl*.

The verse establishes *al-ṣafḥ al-jamīl* — *the beautiful-pardon*. *Pardon can be ugly* (*resentful, grudging, condescending*) or *beautiful* (*peaceful, gracious, unconditional*). *The believer's pardon* should be *beautiful*.

[7:199]: "You shall resort to pardon, advocate tolerance, and disregard the ignorant." — *khudhi l-`afwa wa-mur bi-l-`urfi wa-a`riḍ `ani l-jāhilīn*.

The verse establishes *the three-component pardon-discipline*: *(a) take the pardon, (b) advocate the customary-good, (c) disregard the ignorant*. *Pardoning is the active-orientation*; *the ignorant-response is to disregard-not-retaliate*.

**8. The 'forgive-the-disbelievers-and-pray-for-them' command.**

[45:14]: "Tell those who believed to forgive those who do not long for the days of GOD. He will fully pay everyone for whatever they have earned." — *qul li-lladhīna āmanū yaghfirū li-lladhīna lā yarjūna ayyāma llāh*.

The verse — *one of the most-radical pardon-commands* — instructs *the believers to forgive even the disbelievers* (*those who do not hope for the days of God*). *The believer's-pardon extends across-belief-lines*; *God will ultimately settle accounts*.

[64:14]: "O you who believe, your spouses and your children can be your enemies; beware. If you pardon, forget, and forgive, then GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful." — *wa-in ta`fū wa-taṣfaḥū wa-taghfirū fa-inna llāha Ghafūrun Raḥīm*.

The verse — *in the family-context* — establishes *the three-component family-pardon-discipline*: *pardon, forget, forgive*. *In the family-context where intimate-knowledge of grievances can fester*, *the three-fold discipline is required*.

**9. The 'God forgives those who forgive' implication.**

The verses combine to establish *the reciprocal-forgiveness-economy*:

(a) *The believer who pardons-and-forgives others* — *attracts divine-pardon-and-forgiveness for themselves* ([24:22], [3:134]).

(b) *The believer who withholds-pardon* — *implicitly-asks God to withhold-pardon for them*.

(c) *The forgiveness-economy operates by reciprocity* — *the measure-you-extend is the measure-extended-to-you* (a hadith-tradition that *captures* the *Quranic-architecture*).

This is *theologically-foundational*: *the believer cannot expect divine-mercy while denying-it to fellow-humans*. *The hardness-of-heart toward fellow-humans* is *the hardness-of-heart toward God's-mercy reaching oneself*.

**10. The 'reconciliation-after-discord' command.**

[2:224]: "Do not subject GOD's name to your casual swearing, that you may appear righteous, pious, or to attain credibility among the people. GOD is Hearer, Omniscient."

[2:225]: "GOD does not hold you responsible for the mere utterance of oaths; He holds you responsible for your innermost intentions. GOD is Forgiver, Clement."

[49:9]: "If two groups of believers fought with each other, you shall reconcile them. If one group aggresses against the other, you shall fight the aggressing group until they submit to GOD's command. Once they submit, you shall reconcile the two groups equitably. You shall maintain justice; GOD loves those who are just."

[49:10]: "The believers are members of one family; you shall keep the peace among your brethren and reverence GOD, that you may attain mercy."

The verses establish *the active-reconciliation duty*: *the believer-community is responsible for actively-reconciling believers-in-conflict*. *Forgiveness is not just a personal-orientation*; it is *a community-discipline of active-mediation*.

**11. The cultivation of forgiveness-of-others.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-methods* for *forgiveness-of-others*:

(a) *Reciprocity-principle awareness* — *do you not love that God forgive you?* ([24:22]).

(b) *Pardoning-in-the-moment-of-anger* — *when angered they forgive* ([42:37]).

(c) *Recognition-of-forgiveness-as-strength* — *patience and forgiveness reflect true strength-of-character* ([42:43]).

(d) *Beautiful-pardon discipline* — *al-ṣafḥ al-jamīl* ([15:85]).

(e) *Three-component family-pardon* — *pardon, forget, forgive* ([64:14]).

(f) *Cross-belief-line forgiveness* — *forgive those who do not hope for the days of God* ([45:14]).

(g) *Active-community-reconciliation* — *the believers are members of one family; you shall keep the peace among your brethren* ([49:10]).

(h) *Pardon-and-disregard-the-ignorant* — *take the pardon, advocate the customary-good, disregard the ignorant* ([7:199]).

**12. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *forgiving-others* is *one of the practical-tests of authentic-submission*. The Submitter who *prays, fasts, and gives zakat* while *harboring grudges* is *failing the comprehensive-test*. The Khalifa-translation of [24:22] preserves the *reciprocity-principle*: *do you not love that God forgive you?* — *foregrounding* the *reciprocal-economy of divine-and-human forgiveness*.

The Submitter-discipline: *daily-forgiveness of others' offenses*; *immediate-pardon when wronged*; *active-reconciliation when in discord*; *cross-belief-line forgiveness*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *forgiving-others* is *one of the most-frequently-emphasized character-virtues*. *Pardoners-of-the-people* (*al-`āfīna `ani n-nās*) are *one of the four defining-virtues of the paradise-bound muttaqīn* ([3:134]). *The reciprocity-principle*: *pardon and disregard the offense; do you not love that God forgive you?* ([24:22]). *When angered they forgive* ([42:37]). *Equivalent-retribution is the just-requital; pardoning-and-maintaining-righteousness is rewarded by God* ([42:40]); *patience and forgiveness reflect true strength-of-character* ([42:43]) — *Quranic-rejection of the weakness-equation*. *Do not let discord drive you to injustice* ([5:8]). *Pardon them and disregard them — God loves the muḥsinīn* ([5:13]). *Forgive with the beautiful-pardon (al-ṣafḥ al-jamīl)* ([15:85]). *Take the pardon, advocate the customary-good, disregard the ignorant* ([7:199]). *Forgive those who do not hope for the days of God — cross-belief-line forgiveness* ([45:14]). *Pardon, forget, forgive — three-component family-pardon* ([64:14]). *Active-community-reconciliation* ([49:9]–[49:10]). The *reciprocal-forgiveness-economy*: *the believer who pardons-and-forgives others attracts divine-pardon-and-forgiveness for themselves*; *the believer who withholds-pardon implicitly-asks God to withhold-pardon for them*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *forgiveness* as *practical-test of authentic-submission* — *the Submitter who harbors grudges is failing the comprehensive-test*.

3:134.text: who give to charity during the good times, as well as the bad times. They are suppressors of anger, and pardoners of the people. GOD loves the charitable.
3:134.arabic: الذين ينفقون في السراء والضراء والكظمين الغيظ والعافين عن الناس والله يحب المحسنين

5:13.text: It was a consequence of their violating the covenant that we condemned them, and we caused their hearts to become hardened. Consequently, they took the words out of context, and disregarded some of the commandments given to them. You will continue to witness betrayal from them, excepting a few of them. You shall pardon them, and disregard them. GOD loves those who are benevolent. Christians, too, Must Obey God's Messenger
5:13.arabic: فبما نقضهم ميثقهم لعنـهم وجعلنا قلوبهم قسية يحرفون الكلم عن مواضعه ونسوا حظا مما ذكروا به ولا تزال تطلع على خائنة منهم إلا قليلا منهم فاعف عنهم واصفح إن الله يحب المحسنين

7:199.text: You shall resort to pardon, advocate tolerance, and disregard the ignorant.
7:199.arabic: خذ العفو وأمر بالعرف وأعرض عن الجهلين

15:85.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, except for a specific purpose. The end of the world will come, so treat them with benign neglect.
15:85.arabic: وما خلقنا السموت والأرض وما بينهما إلا بالحق وإن الساعة لأتية فاصفح الصفح الجميل

24:22.text: Those among you who are blessed with resources and wealth shall be charitable towards their relatives, the poor, and those who have immigrated for the sake of GOD. They shall treat them with kindness and tolerance; do you not love to attain GOD's forgiveness? GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
24:22.arabic: ولا يأتل أولوا الفضل منكم والسعة أن يؤتوا أولي القربى والمسكين والمهجرين في سبيل الله وليعفوا وليصفحوا ألا تحبون أن يغفر الله لكم والله غفور رحيم

42:37.text: They avoid gross sins and vice, and when angered they forgive.
42:37.arabic: والذين يجتنبون كبـئر الإثم والفوحش وإذا ما غضبوا هم يغفرون

42:40.text: Although the just requital for an injustice is an equivalent retribution, those who pardon and maintain righteousness are rewarded by GOD. He does not love the unjust.
42:40.arabic: وجزـؤا سيئة سيئة مثلها فمن عفا وأصلح فأجره على الله إنه لا يحب الظـلمين

42:43.text: Resorting to patience and forgiveness reflects a true strength of character.
42:43.arabic: ولمن صبر وغفر إن ذلك لمن عزم الأمور

45:14.text: Tell those who believed to forgive those who do not expect the days of GOD. He will fully pay everyone for whatever they have earned.
45:14.arabic: قل للذين ءامنوا يغفروا للذين لا يرجون أيام الله ليجزي قوما بما كانوا يكسبون

64:14.text: O you who believe, your spouses and your children can be your enemies; beware. If you pardon, forget, and forgive, then GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
64:14.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا إن من أزوجكم وأولدكم عدوا لكم فاحذروهم وإن تعفوا وتصفحوا وتغفروا فإن الله غفور رحيم

question: What does the Quran teach about hospitality, drawing on Abraham?
answer:
The Quran's *Abraham-and-the-guests* narrative is *the paradigmatic-hospitality story* in the scripture. The narrative appears in *three distinct passages* — surahs *Hud (11), Al-Ḥijr (15), and Adh-Dhāriyāt (51)* — each *adding details*. The *Abrahamic-hospitality model* — *immediate-and-generous reception of strangers, the prompt-preparation of food, the gracious-engagement-during-eating, the seeking-of-the-guests' identity-only-after-providing-hospitality* — is *the Quranic-standard for hospitality*.

**1. The Hud-version.**

[11:69]: "When our messengers went to Abraham with good news, they said, 'Peace.' He said, 'Peace,' and soon brought a roasted calf." — *qālū salāmā, qāla salāmun, fa-mā labitha an jā'a bi-`ijlin ḥanīdh*.

[11:70]: "When he saw that their hands did not reach for it, he became suspicious and afraid of them. They said, 'Do not be afraid, we are being dispatched to the people of Lot.'"

[11:71]: "His wife was standing, and she laughed when we gave her the good news about Isaac, and after Isaac, Jacob."

The narrative-elements:

(a) *Greeting-exchange*: *they said "peace"; he said "peace"*.

(b) *Immediate-preparation*: *soon brought a roasted calf* — *no delay, no calculation of cost*.

(c) *Quality-of-food*: *a roasted calf* — *a fattened-prepared meal*, *not the bare-minimum*.

(d) *Observation-and-concern*: *when he saw their hands did not reach for it, he became suspicious* — *Abraham noticed the unusual-behavior of the guests*.

(e) *Guests' reassurance*: *do not be afraid; we are dispatched to the people of Lot*.

(f) *Sarah's laughter and the good-news of Isaac*.

**2. The Al-Ḥijr version.**

[15:51]: "Inform them about Abraham's guests."

[15:52]: "When they entered his quarters, they said, 'Peace.' He said, 'We are apprehensive about you.'"

[15:53]: "They said, 'Do not be apprehensive. We have good news for you: an enlightened son.'"

[15:54]: "He said, 'How can you give me such good news, when I am so old? Do you still give me this good news?'"

The Ḥijr-version emphasizes *the conversation-component* of *the hospitality*: *Abraham expresses apprehension*; *the guests reassure*; *the good-news is delivered*; *Abraham questions the possibility given his age*.

[15:68]: "He said, 'These are my guests; do not embarrass me.'"

[15:69]: "'Fear GOD, and do not shame me.'"

The verses — about *Lot's response to the people's-attempt-to-attack-the-guests* — establish *the host's-protection-of-the-guests*: *do not embarrass me; do not shame me*. *The host's-honor* is *bound up with* *the guest's-protection*.

**3. The Adh-Dhāriyāt version.**

[51:24]: "Have you noted the history of Abraham's honored guests?"

[51:25]: "When they entered his quarters, they said, 'Peace.' He said, 'Peace to you, strangers!'"

[51:26]: "He asked his family to prepare a fat calf."

[51:27]: "When he offered it to them, he remarked, 'Do you not eat?'"

[51:28]: "He harbored fear of them. They said, 'Have no fear,' and they gave good news of a knowledgeable son."

[51:29]: "His wife was astonished. Noting her wrinkled face: 'I am a sterile old woman.'"

[51:30]: "They said, 'Thus said your Lord. He is the Most Wise, the Omniscient.'"

The Dhāriyāt-version adds:

(a) *The honored-guests title* — *ḍayfi Ibrāhīma l-mukramīn* — *Abraham's honored-guests*.

(b) *Greeting-with-naming the strangers* — *peace to you, strangers* — *the cordial-acknowledgment of the unknown-status*.

(c) *Asking-family to prepare* — *fa-rāgha ilā ahlihī* — *he turned to his family*. *The hospitality-effort engages the entire household*.

(d) *Offering-the-food explicitly* — *he offered it to them*.

(e) *Inquiring-about-eating* — *do you not eat?* — *Abraham's polite-inquiry when they did not eat*.

**4. The hospitality-components.**

Drawing across the three narratives, *the Abrahamic-hospitality-model* has *seven components*:

(a) *Immediate-greeting with peace* — *the salām-greeting is the foundational-courtesy*.

(b) *Reciprocal-peace-greeting* — *he said, "peace"* — *the greeting is returned*.

(c) *Immediate-preparation of food* — *no delay between greeting and food-preparation*.

(d) *Quality-food, not bare-minimum* — *a fattened-roasted calf*.

(e) *Engagement-of-household* — *asking family to help prepare*.

(f) *Polite-observation of guest-behavior* — *noting if guests do not eat, inquiring without coercion*.

(g) *Identity-inquiry-only-after-providing-hospitality* — *the good-news / message is delivered after the food*.

The seventh-component is *culturally-distinctive*: *Abraham does not interrogate the guests about their identity before providing hospitality*. *Hospitality precedes identity-verification*. *The guests are honored as strangers without prior-verification*.

**5. The 'protect-the-guest' principle.**

[15:68]–[15:69] — *Lot's protection of his guests* — establishes *the protect-the-guest principle*: *do not embarrass me; do not shame me*. *The host's-honor* is *the guest's-protection*.

The *theological-principle*: *the guest enters under the host's-protection*; *any-harm-to-the-guest* is *harm-to-the-host*. *The host's-protection extends-to-the-utmost*; *Lot offered his daughters as a desperate-alternative* (the *narrative's-extreme-example*).

**6. The 'travelers-and-charity' parallel.**

[2:177], [2:215], [4:36], [9:60] — *the standard-charity-list* — includes *ibn as-sabīl* (*the wayfarer / traveler*) as *one of the categories*. *The traveler is the prototype-stranger* who *deserves hospitality and material-assistance*.

The Quranic-architecture *integrates*: *hospitality (immediate-personal welcome) + charity (material-assistance to the traveler) + protection (the host's-honor-of-the-guest)*. *Three-component aid* to *the stranger*.

**7. The 'household-cooperation' theme.**

[51:26]: *Abraham turned to his family* — *the hospitality engages the entire household*.

[11:71]: *Sarah was standing* — *Sarah is present, witnessing the hospitality*.

The Quranic-narrative includes *Sarah* in *the hospitality-scene*; *the wife is not absent*. *Hospitality is a household-collaboration*; *the women are not excluded*. The *gendered-segregation* that *some later-Islamic traditions developed* is *not-supported* by *the Quranic-Abrahamic-hospitality-narrative*.

**8. The 'cultural-context' of Abrahamic-hospitality.**

The *ancient Near-Eastern context* of *hospitality*:

(a) *The desert-environment* made *travel-dangerous and resources-scarce*; *the unexpected-stranger could be in genuine-need*.

(b) *The traveler-might-be-anyone* — *a prophet (as in this case), a king, an angel*. *The cultural-tradition* of *generous-hospitality protected against accidentally-mistreating an important-person*.

(c) *The reciprocity-system* meant *today's-host could be tomorrow's-guest*; *the hospitality-tradition was a survival-system*.

(d) *The Genesis-parallel* (Genesis 18:1–8) shows *the same Abrahamic-hospitality-story*: *three strangers, immediate-preparation, fattened-calf, hosting under the tree*. *The Quranic and Genesis versions agree in detail*.

The *Quranic-narrative* preserves *the historical-Abrahamic-hospitality model* and *establishes it as the standard for the believer-community*.

**9. The 'beware-of-strangers' counter-discipline.**

The Quranic-text does *not naively suggest* that *all strangers are angels*. *Abraham himself became suspicious* when the guests did not eat ([11:70]). *Lot recognized the threat-to-his-guests-and-protected them at great-cost* ([15:68]–[15:69]). *Discernment is required*; *generous-hospitality does not require naive-trust*.

The *Quranic-discipline*: *initial-generosity to the stranger* + *prudent-observation* + *protection-when-needed*. *The hospitality-orientation is generous*; *the observation-is-discerning*; *the protection-is-firm*.

**10. The contemporary-application.**

The *Abrahamic-hospitality-model* applies *across-times*:

(a) *Immediate-warm-welcome to guests*.

(b) *Quality-food-and-environment* (within one's means).

(c) *Household-engagement in hosting*.

(d) *Polite-observation without interrogation*.

(e) *Hospitality-precedes-identity-verification* (within reasonable-bounds).

(f) *Protection of the guest under one's-roof*.

(g) *Following-up with material-assistance for travelers in need*.

**11. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *Abrahamic-hospitality* is *one of the practical-virtues* of *the Submitter*. The Submitter-host's *standard-discipline*: *follow the Abrahamic-model* of *immediate-warm-welcome, quality-preparation, household-engagement, polite-observation, hospitality-before-interrogation, protection-of-the-guest, material-aid for travelers*.

The Khalifa-translation consistently renders the *Abrahamic-narrative* with *attention to the hospitality-details*: *fat calf, peace-greeting-exchange, do you not eat?*. The *narrative-details* are *preserved* — *not abstracted-away* — to *model* the *specific behaviors* of *Abrahamic-hospitality*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *Abraham-and-the-guests* narrative — *appearing in three passages* ([11:69]–[11:73], [15:51]–[15:74], [51:24]–[51:30]) — is *the paradigmatic-hospitality story*. The *seven-component model*: *immediate-greeting with peace, reciprocal-peace-greeting, immediate-preparation of food, quality-food not bare-minimum (fattened-roasted calf), engagement-of-household, polite-observation of guest-behavior (do you not eat?), identity-inquiry-only-after-providing-hospitality*. *The protect-the-guest principle* ([15:68]–[15:69]) — *Lot's protection of his guests*: *do not embarrass me; do not shame me; the host's-honor is the guest's-protection*. *The traveler is included in the standard-charity-list* ([2:177], [2:215], [4:36], [9:60]). *Household-cooperation theme* — *Sarah is present, witnessing the hospitality* ([11:71], [51:29]). The *ancient Near-Eastern context* — *desert-environment, traveler-might-be-anyone, reciprocity-system* — was *culturally-preserved* by the Quranic-narrative and *established as the standard for the believer-community*. *Discernment is required* — *Abraham became suspicious when guests did not eat* ([11:70]) — *initial-generosity to the stranger, prudent-observation, protection-when-needed*. The *contemporary-application*: *immediate-warm-welcome, quality-preparation, household-engagement, polite-observation without interrogation, hospitality-precedes-identity-verification, protection of the guest, material-aid for travelers*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *Abrahamic-hospitality* as *practical-virtue of the Submitter*; *the narrative-details are preserved to model the specific behaviors*.

11:69.text: When our messengers went to Abraham with good news, they said, "Peace." He said, "Peace," and soon brought a roasted calf.
11:69.arabic: ولقد جاءت رسلنا إبرهيم بالبشرى قالوا سلما قال سلم فما لبث أن جاء بعجل حنيذ

15:52.arabic: إذ دخلوا عليه فقالوا سلما قال إنا منكم وجلون

15:53.arabic: قالوا لا توجل إنا نبشرك بغلم عليم

15:54.arabic: قال أبشرتموني على أن مسني الكبر فبم تبشرون

15:68.arabic: قال إن هـؤلاء ضيفي فلا تفضحون

51:24.arabic: هل أتىك حديث ضيف إبرهيم المكرمين

51:25.arabic: إذ دخلوا عليه فقالوا سلما قال سلم قوم منكرون

51:28.arabic: فأوجس منهم خيفة قالوا لا تخف وبشروه بغلم عليم

51:29.arabic: فأقبلت امرأته في صرة فصكت وجهها وقالت عجوز عقيم
