65:2.text: Once the interim is fulfilled, you may reconcile with them equitably, or go through with the separation equitably. You shall have two equitable witnesses witness the divorce before GOD. This is to enlighten those who believe in GOD and the Last Day. Anyone who reverences GOD, He will create an exit for him.
65:2.arabic: فإذا بلغن أجلهن فأمسكوهن بمعروف أو فارقوهن بمعروف وأشهدوا ذوي عدل منكم وأقيموا الشهدة لله ذلكم يوعظ به من كان يؤمن بالله واليوم الأخر ومن يتق الله يجعل له مخرجا

65:4.text: As for the women who have reached menopause, if you have any doubts, their interim shall be three months. As for those who do not menstruate, and discover that they are pregnant, their interim ends upon giving birth. Anyone who reverences GOD, He makes everything easy for him.
65:4.arabic: والــي يئسن من المحيض من نسائكم إن ارتبتم فعدتهن ثلثة أشهر والــي لم يحضن وأولت الأحمال أجلهن أن يضعن حملهن ومن يتق الله يجعل له من أمره يسرا

65:6.text: You shall allow them to live in the same home in which they lived with you, and do not make life so miserable for them that they leave on their own. If they are pregnant, you shall spend on them until they give birth. If they nurse the infant, you shall pay them for this service. You shall maintain the amicable relations among you. If you disagree, you may hire another woman to nurse the child.
65:6.arabic: أسكنوهن من حيث سكنتم من وجدكم ولا تضاروهن لتضيقوا عليهن وإن كن أولت حمل فأنفقوا عليهن حتى يضعن حملهن فإن أرضعن لكم فـاتوهن أجورهن وأتمروا بينكم بمعروف وإن تعاسرتم فسترضع له أخرى

question: How does the Quran specify inheritance shares?
answer:
The Quran's *inheritance-shares* (*farā'iḍ*) constitute *one of the most-detailed legal-codes* in the scripture. The foundational-passage is *Surah An-Nisā' (4:11-12, 4:176)*, with *additional-verses providing context*. The Quranic-architecture: *(a) wills-and-bequests may dispose of up-to-one-third of the estate before farā'iḍ-distribution*; *(b) the farā'iḍ-shares apply to the remaining estate*; *(c) the shares are mathematically-specific*; *(d) the shares apply to spouses, children, parents, and siblings*; *(e) the inheritance is not optional but a divine-mandate*.

**1. The foundational inheritance-right verse.**

[4:7]: "The men get a share of what the parents and the relatives leave behind. The women too shall get a share of what the parents and relatives leave behind. Whether it is a small or a large inheritance, (the women must get) a definite share." — *li-r-rijāli naṣībun mim-mā taraka l-wālidāni wa-l-aqrabūn, wa-li-n-nisā'i naṣībun*.

The verse establishes *the foundational-principle*: *both men and women inherit*. The *Quranic-revolution* over *pre-Islamic Arabian practice*: *women in the pre-Islamic context typically received-no-inheritance*; *the Quran categorically-establishes the woman's right-to-inherit*. *Whether small or large, the women's-share is definite*.

**2. The 'children-and-parents' shares verse.**

[4:11]: "GOD decrees a will for the benefit of your children; the male gets twice the share of the female. If the inheritors are only women, more than two, they get two-thirds of what is bequeathed. If only one daughter is left, she gets one-half. The parents of the deceased get one-sixth of the inheritance each, if the deceased has left any children. If he left no children, and his parents are the only inheritors, the mother gets one-third. If he has siblings, then the mother gets one-sixth. All this, after fulfilling any will the deceased has left, and after paying off all debts. When it comes to your parents and your children, you do not know which of them is really the best to you and most beneficial. This is GOD's law. GOD is Omniscient, Most Wise."

The verse establishes *the children-and-parents shares*:

(a) *Male child : female child = 2 : 1*.

(b) *If only daughters* (*more than two*): *daughters share 2/3*.

(c) *If only one daughter*: *she gets 1/2*.

(d) *Parents*: *each gets 1/6 if deceased left children*.

(e) *If no children and only parents inherit*: *mother gets 1/3, father gets 2/3 by default*.

(f) *If siblings exist*: *mother gets 1/6, with siblings taking shares*.

(g) *After fulfilling wills and paying debts* — *the order-of-operations*.

**3. The 'spousal-shares' verse.**

[4:12]: "You get half of what your wives leave behind, if they had no children. If they had children, you get one-fourth of what they leave. All this, after fulfilling any will they had left, and after paying off all debts. They get one-fourth of what you leave behind, if you had no children. If you had children, they get one-eighth of what you bequeath. All this, after fulfilling any will you had left, and after paying off all debts. If the deceased man or woman was a loner, and leaves two siblings, male or female, each of them gets one-sixth of the inheritance. If there are more siblings, then they equally share one-third of the inheritance. All this, after fulfilling any will, and after paying off all debts, so that no one is hurt. This is a will decreed by GOD. GOD is Omniscient, Clement."

The verse establishes *the spousal-and-sibling shares*:

(a) *Husband from wife's-estate*: *1/2 if no children, 1/4 if children*.

(b) *Wife from husband's-estate*: *1/4 if no children, 1/8 if children*.

(c) *Two siblings (lonely-deceased)*: *each 1/6*.

(d) *More-than-two siblings*: *share 1/3 equally*.

(e) *After-wills-and-debts* — *the same order-of-operations*.

(f) *So that no one is hurt* — *the closing-clause* establishing *the protective-intent of the procedure*.

**4. The 'kalālah' detailed-case.**

[4:176]: "They consult you; say, 'GOD advises you concerning the single person. If one dies and leaves no children, and he had a sister, she gets half the inheritance. If she dies first, he inherits from her, if she left no children. If there were two sisters, they get two-thirds of the inheritance. If the siblings are men and women, the male gets twice the share of the female.' GOD thus clarifies for you, lest you go astray. GOD is fully aware of all things."

The verse establishes *the kalālah-detailed-case*: *the deceased-who-leaves-no-direct-descendants and no parents*. *The siblings inherit*. *The female-sibling gets 1/2 if she is the only-sibling*; *two-or-more sisters share 2/3*; *mixed male-and-female siblings: male gets twice the female*.

**5. The 'wills-before-inheritance' procedure.**

[2:180]: "It is decreed that when death approaches, you shall write a will for the benefit of the parents and relatives, equitably. This is a duty upon the righteous."

[2:181]: "If anyone alters a will he had heard, the sin of altering befalls those responsible for such altering. GOD is Hearer, Omniscient."

The verses establish *the will-writing duty*: *when death approaches, write a will for parents and relatives equitably*. *The will-altering is sinful*.

[5:106]–[5:108] (covered at expansion-250) — *the detailed will-witnessing procedure*.

**6. The 'one-third-maximum' bequest principle.**

The Quran does *not specify* the *exact maximum for bequests* in *one-verse*. *The hadith-tradition* establishes *one-third* as *the maximum bequest before farā'iḍ-distribution*; *the Quranic-text mentions wills* repeatedly *but does not quantify the maximum*.

The *theological-architecture*: *the deceased may direct up-to-one-third* to *non-heirs (charity, friends, distant-relatives)*; *the remaining-two-thirds-minimum is distributed by farā'iḍ*. *The wills-cannot-exceed-the-one-third* (per the *received-tradition*).

**7. The 'do-not-leave-them-without-provision' verse.**

[4:8]: "During distribution of the inheritances, if relatives, orphans, and needy persons are present, you shall give them therefrom, and treat them kindly."

The verse establishes *the secondary-charity at distribution*: *if relatives, orphans, needy are present at the distribution*, *give them something from the estate* and *treat them kindly*.

**8. The 'do-not-consume-the-orphan's-share' warnings.**

[4:9]: "Those who are concerned about their own children, in case they leave them behind as orphans, shall observe GOD and be equitable."

[4:10]: "Those who consume the orphans' properties unjustly, eat fire into their bellies, and will suffer in a blazing Hell."

The verses establish *the orphan-inheritance protection*: *the orphan's-share must be preserved and equitably-distributed*; *consuming-the-orphan's-share is one of the most-severely-condemned sins*.

**9. The 'man-share-is-twice' debate.**

The Quranic-text gives *the male-child twice the female-child's share* ([4:11]). *Modern-Islamic-debate* has examined this *in light of contemporary-gender-equality discussions*. *The classical-rationale*: *the male-heir has financial-obligations* (*supporting wife, children, parents*) that *the female-heir does not have* (*her support is the husband's obligation*); *thus the male's-double-share funds his-greater-obligations*.

The Khalifa-tradition tends to *uphold the Quranic-shares as divinely-decreed*: *the rationale is in the broader-Quranic-family-economics*; *the shares should not be reformed by post-Quranic theorizing*.

**10. The 'bequest-to-non-heirs' command.**

[4:33]: "For each of you, we have designated shares from the inheritance left by the parents and the relatives. Also those related to you through marriage, you shall give them their due share. GOD witnesses all things."

The verse establishes *the marriage-related-relatives' share* — *the in-laws may have legitimate-claims* on *the estate-distribution*. The verse closes with *God witnesses all things* — *the divine-witness covers the distribution-procedure*.

**11. The cultural-context.**

The *pre-Islamic Arabian inheritance-context*:

(a) *Women received no inheritance* — *only male-warriors inherited*.

(b) *Children received nothing* until *they reached fighting-age*.

(c) *The eldest-male received the bulk* of *the estate*.

(d) *Widows received nothing* and *were sometimes treated as part of the estate* (the *forced-remarriage to a brother-in-law*).

The *Quranic-inheritance-protections* — *women's-shares, children's-shares, widow's-shares, sibling-shares, orphan-protections* — *categorically-transformed* the *Arabian-inheritance-system*: *the female-and-minor heirs are protected*; *the bulk-to-eldest-male is replaced with mathematical-distribution*.

**12. The cultivation of inheritance-discipline.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-methods* for *inheritance-discipline*:

(a) *Will-writing-when-death-approaches* — [2:180].

(b) *Equitable-will-distribution* — [4:9].

(c) *Adherence-to-the-farā'iḍ-shares* — [4:11], [4:12], [4:176].

(d) *Wills-and-debts-before-inheritance* — [4:11], [4:12].

(e) *Secondary-charity at distribution* — [4:8].

(f) *Orphan-share preservation* — [4:9]–[4:10].

(g) *Witness-the-will* — [5:106]–[5:108].

(h) *Recognition-that-the-shares-are-divine* — *this is God's law* ([4:11]).

**13. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *the inheritance-shares* are *binding-divine-mandates*. The Submitter who *writes a will* must *(a) observe the one-third maximum for non-heir bequests*; *(b) leave the remaining estate to be distributed per the Quranic-shares*. The Submitter who *receives an inheritance* must *(a) adhere to the Quranic-distribution*; *(b) protect the orphan's-share*; *(c) provide secondary-charity to the present-relatives-orphans-needy*.

The Khalifa-translation of the *farā'iḍ-verses* preserves the *mathematical-precision*: *the male gets twice the share of the female; the parents get one-sixth each; the wife gets one-fourth or one-eighth*. The *Submitter-discipline*: *adherence to the divine-mathematics*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *inheritance-shares* (*farā'iḍ*) constitute *one of the most-detailed legal-codes* in the scripture. *Both men and women inherit* ([4:7]) — *the Quranic-revolution over pre-Islamic practice*. *Children-and-parents shares* at [4:11]: *male child : female child = 2:1; if only daughters, 2/3; if only one daughter, 1/2; parents 1/6 each if children; if no children, mother 1/3; if siblings, mother 1/6*. *Spousal-shares* at [4:12]: *husband 1/2 or 1/4 from wife; wife 1/4 or 1/8 from husband*. *Sibling-shares* (kalālah) at [4:12] and [4:176]: *two siblings 1/6 each; more than two share 1/3; sister 1/2 if only sibling; two sisters 2/3*. *Wills-and-debts before farā'iḍ-distribution* ([4:11], [4:12]). *One-third maximum for bequests* (from hadith-tradition; Quran is silent on the exact-maximum). *Will-writing duty when death approaches* ([2:180]). *Secondary-charity at distribution* ([4:8]). *Orphan-share preservation* ([4:9]–[4:10]) — *consuming the orphan's-share is among the most-severely-condemned sins*. The *pre-Islamic Arabian inheritance-context* — *women received nothing, children received nothing, eldest-male took the bulk, widows treated as part of the estate* — was *categorically-transformed* by *the Quranic-inheritance-protections*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *the inheritance-shares* as *binding-divine-mandates*; *the Submitter-discipline is adherence to the divine-mathematics*.

4:7.text: The men get a share of what the parents and the relatives leave behind. The women too shall get a share of what the parents and relatives leave behind. Whether it is a small or a large inheritance, (the women must get) a definite share.
4:7.arabic: للرجال نصيب مما ترك الولدان والأقربون وللنساء نصيب مما ترك الولدان والأقربون مما قل منه أو كثر نصيبا مفروضا

4:8.text: During distribution of the inheritances, if relatives, orphans, and needy persons are present, you shall give them therefrom, and treat them kindly.
4:8.arabic: وإذا حضر القسمة أولوا القربى واليتمى والمسكين فارزقوهم منه وقولوا لهم قولا معروفا

4:9.text: Those who are concerned about their own children, in case they leave them behind, shall observe GOD and be equitable.
4:9.arabic: وليخش الذين لو تركوا من خلفهم ذرية ضعفا خافوا عليهم فليتقوا الله وليقولوا قولا سديدا

4:10.text: Those who consume the orphans' properties unjustly, eat fire into their bellies, and will suffer in Hell.
4:10.arabic: إن الذين يأكلون أمول اليتمى ظلما إنما يأكلون في بطونهم نارا وسيصلون سعيرا

4:11.text: GOD decrees a will for the benefit of your children; the male gets twice the share of the female. If the inheritors are only women, more than two, they get two-thirds of what is bequeathed. If only one daughter is left, she gets one-half. The parents of the deceased get onesixth of the inheritance each, if the deceased has left any children. If he left no children, and his parents are the only inheritors, the mother gets one-third. If he has siblings, then the mother gets one-sixth. All this, after fulfilling any will the deceased has left, and after paying off all debts. When it comes to your parents and your children, you do not know which of them is really the best to you and the most beneficial. This is GOD's law. GOD is Omniscient, Most Wise.
4:11.arabic: يوصيكم الله في أولدكم للذكر مثل حظ الأنثيين فإن كن نساء فوق اثنتين فلهن ثلثا ما ترك وإن كانت وحدة فلها النصف ولأبويه لكل وحد منهما السدس مما ترك إن كان له ولد فإن لم يكن له ولد وورثه أبواه فلأمه الثلث فإن كان له إخوة فلأمه السدس من بعد وصية يوصي بها أو دين ءاباؤكم وأبناؤكم لا تدرون أيهم أقرب لكم نفعا فريضة من الله إن الله كان عليما حكيما

4:12.text: You get half of what your wives leave behind, if they had no children. If they had children, you get one-fourth of what they leave. All this, after fulfilling any will they had left, and after paying off all debts. They get one-fourth of what you leave behind, if you had no children. If you had children, they get one-eighth of what you bequeath. All this, after fulfilling any will you had left, and after paying off all debts. If the deceased man or woman was a loner, and leaves two siblings, male or female, each of them gets one-sixth of the inheritance. If there are more siblings, then they equally share one-third of the inheritance. All this, after fulfilling any will, and after paying off all debts, so that no one is hurt. This is a will decreed by GOD. GOD is Omniscient, Clement.
4:12.arabic: ۞ولكم نصف ما ترك أزوجكم إن لم يكن لهن ولد فإن كان لهن ولد فلكم الربع مما تركن من بعد وصية يوصين بها أو دين ولهن الربع مما تركتم إن لم يكن لكم ولد فإن كان لكم ولد فلهن الثمن مما تركتم من بعد وصية توصون بها أو دين وإن كان رجل يورث كللة أو امرأة وله أخ أو أخت فلكل وحد منهما السدس فإن كانوا أكثر من ذلك فهم شركاء في الثلث من بعد وصية يوصى بها أو دين غير مضار وصية من الله والله عليم حليم

4:13.text: These are GOD's laws. Those who obey GOD and His messenger, He will admit them into gardens with flowing streams, wherein they abide forever. This is the greatest triumph.
4:13.arabic: تلك حدود الله ومن يطع الله ورسوله يدخله جنـت تجري من تحتها الأنهر خلدين فيها وذلك الفوز العظيم

4:14.text: As for the one who disobeys GOD and His messenger, and transgresses His laws, He will admit him into Hell, wherein he abides forever. He has incurred a shameful retribution.
4:14.arabic: ومن يعص الله ورسوله ويتعد حدوده يدخله نارا خلدا فيها وله عذاب مهين

4:33.text: For each of you, we have designated shares from the inheritance left by the parents and the relatives. Also those related to you through marriage, you shall give them their due share. GOD witnesses all things.
4:33.arabic: ولكل جعلنا مولي مما ترك الولدان والأقربون والذين عقدت أيمنكم فـاتوهم نصيبهم إن الله كان على كل شيء شهيدا

4:176.text: They consult you; say, "GOD advises you concerning the single person. If one dies and leaves no children, and he had a sister, she gets half the inheritance. If she dies first, he inherits from her, if she left no children. If there were two sisters, they get two-thirds of the inheritance. If the siblings are men and women, the male gets twice the share of the female." GOD thus clarifies for you, lest you go astray. GOD is fully aware of all things.
4:176.arabic: يستفتونك قل الله يفتيكم في الكللة إن امرؤا هلك ليس له ولد وله أخت فلها نصف ما ترك وهو يرثها إن لم يكن لها ولد فإن كانتا اثنتين فلهما الثلثان مما ترك وإن كانوا إخوة رجالا ونساء فللذكر مثل حظ الأنثيين يبين الله لكم أن تضلوا والله بكل شيء عليم

2:180.text: It is decreed that when death approaches, you shall write a will for the benefit of the parents and relatives, equitably. This is a duty upon the righteous.
2:180.arabic: كتب عليكم إذا حضر أحدكم الموت إن ترك خيرا الوصية للولدين والأقربين بالمعروف حقا على المتقين

2:240.text: Those who die and leave wives, a will shall provide their wives with support for a year, provided they stay within the same household. If they leave, you commit no sin by letting them do whatever they wish, so long as righteousness is maintained. GOD is Almighty, Most Wise.
2:240.arabic: والذين يتوفون منكم ويذرون أزوجا وصية لأزوجهم متعا إلى الحول غير إخراج فإن خرجن فلا جناح عليكم في ما فعلن في أنفسهن من معروف والله عزيز حكيم

question: What does the Quran clarify about adoption (33:4-5)?
answer:
The Quran's *adoption-clarification* at [33:4]–[33:5] *categorically distinguishes* between *adoption (as social-care for the displaced child)* and *adoption-as-creating-fictional-biological-relationships*. The Quran *prohibits the latter* while *encouraging the former*. The clarification was *historically-revealed* in the context of *the Prophet's-adopted-son Zayd ibn Harithah*; the *theological-implication* is *that adopted-children retain their biological-paternity in legal-and-social-status*, *but the kindness-and-care toward them remains a virtue*.

**1. The foundational adoption-clarification verses.**

[33:4]: "GOD did not give any man two hearts in his chest. Nor did He turn your wives whom you estrange (according to your custom) into your mothers. Nor did He turn your adopted children into genetic offspring. All these are mere utterances that you have invented. GOD speaks the truth, and He guides in the (right) path." — *wa-mā ja`ala ad`iyā'akum abnā'akum*.

[33:5]: "You shall give your adopted children names that preserve their relationship to their genetic parents. This is more equitable in the sight of GOD. If you do not know their parents, then, as your brethren in religion, you shall treat them as members of your family. You do not commit a sin if you make a mistake in this respect; you are responsible for your purposeful intentions. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful."

The verses establish *the foundational-clarification*:

(a) *The adopted children are not genetic offspring* — *the adoption does not create a biological-relationship*.

(b) *The adopted children retain their relationship to their genetic-parents* — *names should reflect biological-parentage*.

(c) *If the biological-parents are unknown*, *treat the adopted-child as a brother-in-religion* — *retain the social-and-care-relationship, just without the biological-fiction*.

(d) *Mistakes are forgiven* — *only purposeful-intentions are accountable*.

**2. The historical-context: Zayd-and-Zaynab.**

The verses were revealed *in the context of the Prophet's-marriage to Zaynab bint Jahsh*. *Zayd ibn Harithah*, *Muhammad's adopted-son in the pre-Islamic-tradition*, *had been married to Zaynab*; *the marriage failed*; *Zayd divorced her*; *Muhammad married her at God's-direction* ([33:37]).

The *pre-Islamic adoption-tradition* would have *prohibited Muhammad from marrying Zaynab* — *under the pre-Islamic rule*, *the adopted-son's ex-wife was treated as the adoptive-father's daughter-in-law*, *prohibited for marriage*.

The Quranic-clarification at [33:4]–[33:5] *categorically rejects* this *pre-Islamic rule*: *Zayd is not Muhammad's biological-son*; *therefore Muhammad can marry Zaynab*. The *clarification serves both* (a) *the immediate-purpose of the Prophet's-marriage* and (b) *the broader-purpose of correcting the adoption-misunderstanding for all-time*.

**3. The 'Zayd-and-Zaynab' verses.**

[33:37]: "Recall that you said to the one who was blessed by GOD, and blessed by you, 'Keep your wife and reverence GOD,' and you hid inside yourself what GOD wished to proclaim. Thus, you feared the people, when you were supposed to fear only GOD. When Zayd was completely through with his wife, we had you marry her, in order to establish the precedent that a man may marry the divorced wife of his adopted son. GOD's commands shall be done. The prophet is not committing an error by doing anything that is made lawful by GOD..."

The verse — *one of the most-controversial Quranic-verses in non-Muslim-polemics* — establishes *the theological-purpose* of *Muhammad's-marriage to Zaynab*: *to establish the precedent that a man may marry the divorced-wife of his adopted-son*. *The precedent corrects the pre-Islamic-rule*; *the marriage is the demonstration-of-the-corrected-law*.

The verse also includes *the Prophet's-self-criticism*: *you feared the people when you were supposed to fear only God*. *Muhammad-himself was uncomfortable with the marriage* (anticipating-the-public-controversy), and *the Quran rebukes this fear-of-the-people*. The Quran's *willingness to record this self-criticism* of *the Prophet* is *theologically-significant* — *the Quran is not whitewashing the Prophet's-internal-states*.

**4. The 'Muhammad is no father of any man' verse.**

[33:40]: "Muhammad was not the father of any man among you. He was a messenger of GOD and the final prophet. GOD is fully aware of all things." — *mā kāna Muḥammadun abā aḥadin min rijālikum*.

The verse — *immediately following the Zayd-Zaynab cluster* — establishes *the categorical-claim*: *Muhammad was not the biological-father of any-man among the believers*. *The Prophet's-biological-sons all died in infancy*; *Zayd was an adopted-son, not a biological-son*. *No-man among the believers* is *Muhammad's-biological-son*.

The closing-clause: *he was a messenger of God and the final prophet (khātim an-nabiyyīn)*. The *finality-of-prophethood* is *Quranically-grounded* in *this verse*. *The Prophet's-non-biological-paternity-of-any-living-male* is *theologically-connected to the finality of his prophethood*.

**5. The 'breastfeeding-creates-mahram-relationships' parallel.**

[2:233]: "Divorced mothers shall nurse their infants two full years, if the father so wishes. The father shall provide the mother's food and clothing equitably. No one shall be burdened beyond his ability. No mother shall be harmed on account of her infant, nor shall the father be harmed because of his infant. (If the father dies), his inheritor shall assume these responsibilities. If the infant's parents mutually agree to part, after due consultation, they commit no error by doing so. You commit no error by hiring nursing mothers, so long as you pay them equitably. You shall reverence GOD, and know that GOD is Seer of everything you do."

[4:23] (in the marriage-prohibitions list): "...your foster mothers who breast-fed you, your sisters through breast feeding..."

The Quranic-architecture acknowledges *certain-relationships create mahram-status* (*marriage-prohibition status*):

(a) *Biological-relationships* (parents, children, siblings, etc.).

(b) *Marriage-relationships* (in-laws).

(c) *Breastfeeding-relationships* (the wet-nurse is *like-mother*; her biological-children are *like-siblings*).

*Adoption alone does not create mahram-status*. *The adopted-child remains marriageable to the adoptive-family's-members* (with some-caveats from the breastfeeding-relationship if applicable).

**6. The 'foster-care without false-paternity' principle.**

The Quranic-clarification at [33:5] allows *foster-care of orphans-and-displaced-children*:

(a) *Treat them kindly* — *the iḥsān-to-orphans command* throughout the Quran.

(b) *Use their biological-names where known* — *preserve their genetic-identity*.

(c) *Treat them as brothers-in-religion when biological-parents are unknown* — *the social-relationship is preserved, without the biological-fiction*.

(d) *Mahram-status is not created by adoption* — *the foster-relationship is not a marriage-bar*.

The *modern-application*: *Islamic-foster-care (kafālah)* — *the legal-and-social-care for the orphan-or-displaced-child* — *is encouraged*; *Western-legal-adoption with full biological-fiction* (*name-change, inheritance-as-biological-child, mahram-status*) *is theologically-problematic*.

**7. The 'wives-as-mothers' parallel-prohibition.**

[33:4]'s middle-clause — *nor did He turn your wives whom you estrange into your mothers* — addresses *the pre-Islamic ẓihār practice*: *the husband says to his wife "you are like my mother's-back to me"* — *intending divorce-by-this-pronouncement*. The Quran *categorically rejects* this *fictional-creation of mother-relationship*: *the wife does not become the mother by the husband's-pronouncement*.

[58:1]–[58:4] addresses *ẓihār in detail*: *the pronouncement is wrong*; *the husband must provide expiation (freeing-a-slave, fasting-two-consecutive-months, or feeding-sixty-poor)*; *then can resume normal-marital-relations*.

The *parallel*: *just as ẓihār cannot create a mother-from-a-wife*; *adoption cannot create a son-from-a-non-biological-child*. *Both involve fictional-creation of biological-relationships that are categorically-rejected*.

**8. The 'mistakes are forgiven' clause.**

[33:5]'s closing-clause: *you do not commit a sin if you make a mistake in this respect; you are responsible for your purposeful intentions*. The clause provides *the forgiveness-of-honest-mistakes*: *if you accidentally-use the adoptive-father's-name for the adopted-child (due to genuine-unawareness)*, *no sin*. *The standard is purposeful-intention*; *honest-error is forgiven*.

The *theological-architecture*: *the Quran's-law-discipline does not punish honest-mistakes*; *it punishes purposeful-violations*. The *believer's-conscience is the relevant-arbiter*.

**9. The cultural-context.**

The *pre-Islamic Arabian adoption-context*:

(a) *Adoption was common* — *the adopted-child took the adopter's-name*.

(b) *The adopted-child was considered a biological-son* in *inheritance, mahram-status, marriage-prohibitions*.

(c) *The adopted-child's-biological-parents were forgotten* in *the legal-and-social-record*.

(d) *Adoption was a tool of tribal-strengthening* — *adding-fighters to one's-tribe*.

The *Quranic-clarification* — *adoption does not create biological-status* — *categorically corrected* the *Arabian-adoption-system*: *the biological-parentage is preserved*; *inheritance follows biological-relationships*; *mahram-status follows biological-and-breastfeeding-relationships*.

**10. The cultivation of correct-adoption-practice.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-methods* for *correct-adoption-practice*:

(a) *Recognition of adopted-child's-biological-paternity* — *use the biological-father's-name* ([33:5]).

(b) *Kindness-to-the-orphan / displaced-child* — *the iḥsān-command applies* ([4:36]).

(c) *Treatment-as-brother-in-religion when biological-parents unknown* — *the social-care-relationship-preserved* ([33:5]).

(d) *No-creation-of-fictional-biological-relationships* — *no mahram-status, no inheritance-as-biological-child* ([33:4]).

(e) *Forgiveness-of-honest-mistakes* — *only purposeful-intentions count* ([33:5]).

(f) *Modern-foster-care-and-kafālah* — *as the Islamically-correct alternative* to *Western-legal-adoption*.

**11. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *the adoption-clarification* is *theologically-significant for several reasons*: *(a) it corrects the pre-Islamic practice*; *(b) it preserves biological-paternity in the social-and-legal record*; *(c) it permits-and-encourages foster-care without creating fictional-biological-status*; *(d) it grounds the Prophet's-non-biological-paternity-of-any-living-male, which connects to the finality-of-prophethood*.

The Khalifa-translation of [33:5] preserves the *forgiveness-of-honest-mistakes clause* — *you do not commit a sin if you make a mistake in this respect* — *foregrounding the conscience-as-arbiter*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's *adoption-clarification* at [33:4]–[33:5] *categorically distinguishes* between *adoption (as social-care for the displaced child)* and *adoption-as-creating-fictional-biological-relationships*. *The adopted children are not genetic offspring* ([33:4]); *give them names preserving their biological-paternity* ([33:5]); *if biological-parents unknown, treat as brothers-in-religion*. *Historical-context*: *the Prophet's-marriage to Zaynab*, *Zayd's-divorced-wife*, *establishing the precedent that a man may marry the divorced-wife of his adopted-son* ([33:37]). *Muhammad was not the father of any-man among you; he was a messenger of God and the final prophet (khātim an-nabiyyīn)* ([33:40]) — *the finality-of-prophethood is Quranically-grounded here*. *Breastfeeding-relationships create mahram-status*; *adoption alone does not* ([2:233], [4:23]). *Ẓihār-parallel*: *just as ẓihār cannot create a mother-from-a-wife, adoption cannot create a son-from-a-non-biological-child* ([33:4]). *Forgiveness-of-honest-mistakes* — *only purposeful-intentions count* ([33:5]). The *pre-Islamic Arabian adoption-context* — *adopted-child took the adopter's-name, was considered biological-son in inheritance and mahram-status, biological-parents forgotten* — was *categorically-corrected*. *Modern-application*: *Islamic-foster-care (kafālah) is encouraged*; *Western-legal-adoption with full biological-fiction is theologically-problematic*. The *Khalifa framework* identifies *the adoption-clarification* as *theologically-significant for the correction of pre-Islamic practice, the preservation of biological-paternity, the permission of foster-care without fictional-biological-status, and the grounding of the finality of prophethood*.

33:4.text: GOD did not give any man two hearts in his chest. Nor did He turn your wives whom you estrange (according to your custom) into your mothers. Nor did He turn your adopted children into genetic offspring. All these are mere utterances that you have invented. GOD speaks the truth, and He guides in the (right) path.
33:4.arabic: ما جعل الله لرجل من قلبين في جوفه وما جعل أزوجكم الــي تظهرون منهن أمهتكم وما جعل أدعياءكم أبناءكم ذلكم قولكم بأفوهكم والله يقول الحق وهو يهدي السبيل

33:5.text: You shall give your adopted children names that preserve their relationship to their genetic parents. This is more equitable in the sight of GOD. If you do not know their parents, then, as your brethren in religion, you shall treat them as members of your family. You do not commit a sin if you make a mistake in this respect; you are responsible for your purposeful intentions. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
33:5.arabic: ادعوهم لأبائهم هو أقسط عند الله فإن لم تعلموا ءاباءهم فإخونكم في الدين وموليكم وليس عليكم جناح فيما أخطأتم به ولكن ما تعمدت قلوبكم وكان الله غفورا رحيما

33:37.text: Recall that you said to the one who was blessed by GOD, and blessed by you, "Keep your wife and reverence GOD," and you hid inside yourself what GOD wished to proclaim. Thus, you feared the people, when you were supposed to fear only GOD. When Zeid was completely through with his wife, we had you marry her, in order to establish the precedent that a man may marry the divorced wife of his adopted son. GOD's commands shall be done.
33:37.arabic: وإذ تقول للذي أنعم الله عليه وأنعمت عليه أمسك عليك زوجك واتق الله وتخفي في نفسك ما الله مبديه وتخشى الناس والله أحق أن تخشىه فلما قضى زيد منها وطرا زوجنكها لكي لا يكون على المؤمنين حرج في أزوج أدعيائهم إذا قضوا منهن وطرا وكان أمر الله مفعولا

33:38.text: The prophet is not committing an error by doing anything that is made lawful by GOD. Such is GOD's system since the early generations. GOD's command is a sacred duty.
33:38.arabic: ما كان على النبي من حرج فيما فرض الله له سنة الله في الذين خلوا من قبل وكان أمر الله قدرا مقدورا

33:40.text: Muhammad was not the father of any man among you. He was a messenger of GOD and the final prophet. GOD is fully aware of all things.
33:40.arabic: ما كان محمد أبا أحد من رجالكم ولكن رسول الله وخاتم النبيـن وكان الله بكل شيء عليما

2:233.text: Divorced mothers shall nurse their infants two full years, if the father so wishes. The father shall provide the mother's food and clothing equitably. No one shall be burdened beyond his ability. No mother shall be harmed on account of her infant, nor shall the father be harmed because of his infant. (If the father dies), his inheritor shall assume these responsibilities. If the infant's parents mutually agree to part, after due consultation, they commit no error by doing so. You commit no error by hiring nursing mothers, so long as you pay them equitably. You shall observe GOD, and know that GOD is Seer of everything you do. You Shall Observe the Pre-Marriage Interims
2:233.arabic: ۞والولدت يرضعن أولدهن حولين كاملين لمن أراد أن يتم الرضاعة وعلى المولود له رزقهن وكسوتهن بالمعروف لا تكلف نفس إلا وسعها لا تضار ولدة بولدها ولا مولود له بولده وعلى الوارث مثل ذلك فإن أرادا فصالا عن تراض منهما وتشاور فلا جناح عليهما وإن أردتم أن تسترضعوا أولدكم فلا جناح عليكم إذا سلمتم ما ءاتيتم بالمعروف واتقوا الله واعلموا أن الله بما تعملون بصير

4:22.text: Do not marry the women who were previously married to your fathers - existing marriages are exempted and shall not be broken - for it is a gross offense, and an abominable act.
4:22.arabic: ولا تنكحوا ما نكح ءاباؤكم من النساء إلا ما قد سلف إنه كان فحشة ومقتا وساء سبيلا

question: Under what conditions does the Quran permit plural marriage?
answer:
The Quran's permission of *plural-marriage* (*polygyny — multiple-wives*) is *conditional and narrowly-grounded*. The foundational-verse [4:3] *originates the permission in the orphan-protection context*; *the verse simultaneously establishes the equity-condition*: *if you fear you cannot maintain equity, marry only one*. The companion-verse [4:129] *categorically declares*: *you can never be equitable in dealing with more than one wife, no matter how hard you try*. The *combined-reading* of [4:3] and [4:129] *establishes monogamy as the default* and *polygyny as an exception with strict-conditions*.

**1. The foundational polygyny-permission verse.**

[4:3]: "If you deem it best for the orphans, you may marry their mothers - you may marry two, three, or four. If you fear lest you become unfair, then you shall be content with only one, or with what you already have. Additionally, you are thus more likely to avoid financial hardship." — *wa-in khiftum allā tuqsiṭū fī l-yatāmā fa-nkiḥū mā ṭāba lakum mina n-nisā'i mathnā wa-thulātha wa-rubā`, fa-in khiftum allā ta`dilū fa-wāḥidatan*.

The verse's *contextual-frame*: *if you fear you cannot be equitable to the orphans* (under your care), *then marry their mothers* (so that *you can be the orphan's stepfather and provide directly for them*) — *you may marry two, three, or four*.

The *origin of the permission* is *the orphan-protection problem*. The *historical-context*: *the Battle of Uhud left many widows-and-orphans in the Muslim-community*; *the polygyny-permission addressed the social-protection-need* of *integrating-widows-and-orphans into existing-households*.

The *qualifier*: *if you fear lest you become unfair, then content with only one*. The *equity-condition* is *categorical*: *if equity is doubtful, marry one*. *The permission is not unconditional*; *it is conditioned on equity-and-orphan-context*.

**2. The 'you-cannot-be-equitable' verse.**

[4:129]: "You can never be equitable in dealing with more than one wife, no matter how hard you try. Therefore, do not be so biased as to leave one of them hanging (neither enjoying marriage, nor left to marry someone else). If you correct this situation and maintain righteousness, GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful." — *wa-lan tastaṭī`ū an ta`dilū bayna n-nisā'i wa-law ḥaraṣtum*.

The verse *categorically declares*: *you can never be equitable in dealing with more than one wife, no matter how hard you try*. The Arabic *lan tastaṭī`ū* — *you will never be able* — *is the strongest negative-future-construction in Arabic*. *The impossibility-of-full-equity is categorical*.

The verse continues: *do not be so biased as to leave one of them hanging*. *The lesser-prohibited-evil*: *do not leave a wife in marriage-limbo* (*neither enjoying marriage-rights nor free-to-remarry*).

The *combined-reading* of [4:3] and [4:129]: *if-you-must marry more than one* (for the orphan-protection or similar-need), *try-to-be-equitable*; *but recognize you will never be fully-equitable*; *so prefer monogamy when possible*.

**3. The Khalifa-framework's monogamy-default reading.**

For Khalifa's framework, *the combined-reading establishes monogamy as the default*:

(a) [4:3] *permits polygyny conditionally* — *for orphan-protection contexts* — *with the equity-condition*.

(b) [4:129] *categorically declares full-equity impossible* — *eliminating the standard-justification for polygyny*.

(c) *Therefore*: *in normal-conditions, monogamy is the default*; *polygyny is permitted only in specific-orphan-protection or similar-special contexts*.

The Khalifa-translation of [4:3] preserves *the orphan-context*: *if you deem it best for the orphans, you may marry their mothers*. The Khalifa-translation of [4:129] preserves the *categorical impossibility-of-equity*: *you can never be equitable in dealing with more than one wife*.

**4. The 'orphan-girls-marriage' verse.**

[4:127]: "They consult you concerning women: say, 'GOD enlightens you regarding them, as recited for you in the scripture. You shall restore the rights of orphaned girls whom you cheat out of their due dowries when you wish to marry them: you shall be equitable to the helpless children, and to deal equitably with the orphans...'"

The verse — *coming later in the same surah* — *clarifies* the *orphan-marriage protection*: *do not cheat orphan-girls out of their dowries when you marry them*. The *practical-implication*: *the orphan-marriage context of [4:3] is real*; *the orphan-girls' financial-rights must be preserved*.

**5. The 'do-not-force-women' verse.**

[4:19]: "O you who believe, it is not lawful for you to inherit the women against their will. You shall not force them to give up anything you had given them, unless they commit a proven adultery..."

The verse — *though primarily about widow-protection* — establishes *the no-coercion-in-marriage principle*. *The widow cannot be inherited against her will*; *no woman can be forced into marriage*. *Polygyny, where it occurs, must be with the consent of all parties (including the existing wife)*.

**6. The Prophet's-specific exemption.**

[33:50]: "O prophet, we made lawful for you your wives to whom you have paid their due dowry, or what you already have, as granted to you by GOD. Also lawful for you in marriage are the daughters of your father's brothers, the daughters of your father's sisters, the daughters of your mother's brothers, the daughters of your mother's sisters, who have emigrated with you. Also, if a believing woman gave herself to the prophet - by forfeiting the dowry - the prophet may marry her without a dowry, if he so wishes. However, her forfeiting of the dowry applies only to the prophet, and not to the other believers. We have already decreed their rights in regard to their spouses or what they already have..."

The verse establishes *the Prophet's-specific-exemption*: *certain marriage-categories specific to him*. The Quranic-text *acknowledges* the *Prophet's-specific-marital-arrangements are not the standard for all-believers*. *The Prophet had specific-circumstances* (*the diplomatic-marriages, the widow-care of the early-believers, etc.*); *the believer-norm is not the Prophet-norm in the marriage-context*.

[33:51]: "You may gently shun any one of them, and you may bring closer to you any one of them you wish. If you reconcile with any one you had estranged, you commit no error. In this way, they will be pleased, will have no grief, and will be content with what you equitably offer to all of them. GOD knows what is in your hearts. GOD is Omniscient, Clement."

The verse continues the *Prophet's-marital-arrangements*. *The Prophet had unique-arrangements*; *the believer-norm is the [4:3]-and-[4:129] standard*.

**7. The 'avoidance-of-injustice' continuation.**

[4:130]: "If the couple must decide to part, GOD will provide for each of them from His bounties. GOD is Bounteous, Most Wise."

The verse — *closing the polygyny-discussion cluster* — establishes *the divorce-fallback*: *if the couple cannot maintain the marriage equitably*, *they may divorce*; *God provides for both from His bounties*. *Divorce is preferable to maintaining-an-unjust-marriage*.

**8. The cultural-context.**

The *pre-Islamic Arabian polygyny-context*:

(a) *Unlimited-polygyny* — *no maximum-wives*.

(b) *No equity-requirement*.

(c) *Wives-treated-as-chattel*.

(d) *Frequent-mistreatment-of-junior-wives*.

(e) *The widow-and-orphan problem post-war* was *severe and unaddressed*.

The Quranic-polygyny-restriction — *maximum-four wives, equity-condition, orphan-context grounding, declared-impossibility-of-full-equity* — *categorically-transformed* the Arabian-polygyny-practice. *The reform is restrictive, not permissive*: *the Quran limited polygyny, did not introduce it*.

**9. The 'equity-impossibility' implications.**

The Khalifa-framework's-position: *the [4:129] declaration of equity-impossibility is the operative-principle*. *No man can be fully-equitable to more than one wife*; *therefore the standard-justification for polygyny (I can be equitable) is categorically-defeated by the Quran itself*.

The *remaining-justifications* for *polygyny* are *limited to specific-emergency-contexts*: *(a) orphan-protection*; *(b) post-war widow-integration*; *(c) infertility-compassion (with first-wife's consent)*; *(d) similar-rare-circumstances*. *Normal-monogamy is the default*.

**10. The 'first-wife's-consent' implication.**

The Quranic-text *does not explicitly require* *the first-wife's consent* for *the husband's subsequent-marriage*. *Classical-jurisprudence has debated this*: *some require consent*; *the majority does not require consent but emphasizes equity-as-condition*.

*Modern Submitter-readings* tend to *require first-wife's-consent* on the basis of *the no-coercion principle* ([4:19]) and *the equity-difficulty* ([4:129]): *if the first-wife is unhappy, full-equity becomes even-more-impossible*; *the marriage-equity requires both spouses' consent*.

**11. The cultivation of polygyny-discipline.**

The Quran *identifies* the *cultivation-discipline* for *polygyny*:

(a) *Recognition of the orphan-context origin of the permission* ([4:3]).

(b) *Recognition of the equity-condition* ([4:3]).

(c) *Recognition of the impossibility-of-full-equity* ([4:129]).

(d) *Recognition of the no-coercion principle* ([4:19]).

(e) *Preference-for-monogamy-as-default*.

(f) *Polygyny-only-in-specific-emergency-contexts* — *orphan-protection, post-war widow-integration, etc.*

(g) *First-wife's-consent-where-possible*.

(h) *Equity-effort-when-polygyny-undertaken*.

(i) *Divorce-rather-than-maintain-injustice* ([4:130]).

**12. The Khalifa framework.**

For Khalifa's framework, *plural-marriage* is *categorically-conditional*. The Submitter who *considers polygyny* must *(a) verify the orphan-protection or similar-emergency-context*; *(b) recognize the impossibility of full-equity*; *(c) prefer monogamy whenever-possible*; *(d) obtain first-wife's-consent*; *(e) make-best-effort-at-equity*; *(f) prefer-divorce-rather-than-maintain-injustice*.

The Khalifa-translation of [4:129] preserves the *categorical impossibility*: *you can never be equitable in dealing with more than one wife, no matter how hard you try*. The *Submitter-recognition*: *the Quran itself defeats the standard-justification for polygyny*; *polygyny is permitted in specific-emergency-contexts, not as a general-norm*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's permission of *plural-marriage* (polygyny) is *conditional and narrowly-grounded*. *Foundational-permission* at [4:3]: *if you deem it best for the orphans, you may marry their mothers — two, three, or four; if you fear lest you become unfair, content with only one*. *The orphan-protection is the originating-context*; *the equity-condition is categorical*. *Categorical equity-impossibility* at [4:129]: *you can never be equitable in dealing with more than one wife, no matter how hard you try*. *Combined-reading*: *if-you-must marry more than one (for orphan-protection or similar), try-to-be-equitable, but recognize you will never be fully-equitable, so prefer monogamy when possible*. *Orphan-girls' marriage-protection* at [4:127]. *No-coercion principle* at [4:19]. *Prophet's-specific exemption* at [33:50]–[33:51] — *the believer-norm is not the Prophet-norm*. *Divorce-rather-than-maintain-injustice* at [4:130]. The *pre-Islamic Arabian polygyny-context* — *unlimited, no equity-requirement, wives-as-chattel, frequent-mistreatment, post-war widow-orphan crisis* — was *categorically-restricted* by *the Quranic-reform*; *the reform is restrictive, not permissive*. The *Khalifa framework*: *plural-marriage is categorically-conditional*; *the Submitter who considers polygyny must verify the orphan-protection or similar-emergency-context, recognize the impossibility of full-equity, prefer monogamy whenever-possible, obtain first-wife's-consent, make-best-effort-at-equity, prefer-divorce-rather-than-maintain-injustice*. *The Quran itself defeats the standard-justification for polygyny*.

4:3.text: If you deem it best for the orphans, you may marry their mothers - you may marry two, three, or four. If you fear lest you become unfair, then you shall be content with only one, or with what you already have. Additionally, you are thus more likely to avoid financial hardship.
4:3.arabic: وإن خفتم ألا تقسطوا في اليتمى فانكحوا ما طاب لكم من النساء مثنى وثلث وربع فإن خفتم ألا تعدلوا فوحدة أو ما ملكت أيمنكم ذلك أدنى ألا تعولوا

4:4.text: You shall give the women their due dowries, equitably. If they willingly forfeit anything, then you may accept it; it is rightfully yours.
4:4.arabic: وءاتوا النساء صدقتهن نحلة فإن طبن لكم عن شيء منه نفسا فكلوه هنيـا مريـا

4:19.text: O you who believe, it is not lawful for you to inherit what the women leave behind, against their will. You shall not force them to give up anything you had given them, unless they commit a proven adultery. You shall treat them nicely. If you dislike them, you may dislike something wherein GOD has placed a lot of good.
4:19.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا لا يحل لكم أن ترثوا النساء كرها ولا تعضلوهن لتذهبوا ببعض ما ءاتيتموهن إلا أن يأتين بفحشة مبينة وعاشروهن بالمعروف فإن كرهتموهن فعسى أن تكرهوا شيـا ويجعل الله فيه خيرا كثيرا

4:127.text: They consult you concerning women: say, "GOD enlightens you regarding them, as recited for you in the scripture. You shall restore the rights of orphaned girls whom you cheat out of their due dowries when you wish to marry them: you shall not take advantage of them. The rights of orphaned boys must also be protected as well. You shall treat the orphans equitably. Whatever good you do, GOD is fully aware thereof."
4:127.arabic: ويستفتونك في النساء قل الله يفتيكم فيهن وما يتلى عليكم في الكتب في يتمى النساء الـتي لا تؤتونهن ما كتب لهن وترغبون أن تنكحوهن والمستضعفين من الولدن وأن تقوموا لليتمى بالقسط وما تفعلوا من خير فإن الله كان به عليما

4:129.text: You can never be equitable in dealing with more than one wife, no matter how hard you try. Therefore, do not be so biased as to leave one of them hanging (neither enjoying marriage, nor left to marry someone else). If you correct this situation and maintain righteousness, GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
4:129.arabic: ولن تستطيعوا أن تعدلوا بين النساء ولو حرصتم فلا تميلوا كل الميل فتذروها كالمعلقة وإن تصلحوا وتتقوا فإن الله كان غفورا رحيما

4:130.text: If the couple must decide to part, GOD will provide for each of them from His bounties. GOD is Bounteous, Most Wise.
4:130.arabic: وإن يتفرقا يغن الله كلا من سعته وكان الله وسعا حكيما

33:50.text: O prophet, we made lawful for you your wives to whom you have paid their due dowry, or what you already have, as granted to you by GOD. Also lawful for you in marriage are the daughters of your father's brothers, the daughters of your father's sisters, the daughters of your mother's brothers, the daughters of your mother's sisters, who have emigrated with you. Also, if a believing woman gave herself to the prophet - by forfeiting the dowry - the prophet may marry her without a dowry, if he so wishes. However, her forfeiting of the dowry applies only to the prophet, and not to the other believers. We have already decreed their rights in regard to their spouses or what they already have. This is to spare you any embarrassment. GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
33:50.arabic: يـأيها النبي إنا أحللنا لك أزوجك الـتي ءاتيت أجورهن وما ملكت يمينك مما أفاء الله عليك وبنات عمك وبنات عمـتك وبنات خالك وبنات خلتك الـتي هاجرن معك وامرأة مؤمنة إن وهبت نفسها للنبي إن أراد النبي أن يستنكحها خالصة لك من دون المؤمنين قد علمنا ما فرضنا عليهم في أزوجهم وما ملكت أيمنهم لكيلا يكون عليك حرج وكان الله غفورا رحيما

33:51.text: You may gently shun any one of them, and you may bring closer to you any one of them. If you reconcile with any one you had estranged, you commit no error. In this way, they will be pleased, will have no grief, and will be content with what you equitably offer to all of them. GOD knows what is in your hearts. GOD is Omniscient, Clement.
33:51.arabic: ۞ترجي من تشاء منهن وتـوي إليك من تشاء ومن ابتغيت ممن عزلت فلا جناح عليك ذلك أدنى أن تقر أعينهن ولا يحزن ويرضين بما ءاتيتهن كلهن والله يعلم ما في قلوبكم وكان الله عليما حليما
